tasks.name: executeActionMovement
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used as a taskString. Executes actor-movement and actor-swap effects for the action strings[0] using tileCoords[] as the targeted tiles.

uses.description: Triggers only the movement and actor-swap portions of a named action, applied across the provided sequence of target tiles. This is useful when a script needs to replay or impose the spatial side effects of an action (such as forced repositioning or knockback) without firing the action's damage, status, or other consequences. Because the targeted tiles are read from the list, this must be invoked as a task string rather than from contexts that drop the tile coordinates.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the action whose movement and actor-swap effects should be executed.

required.name: tileCoords[0+]
required.description: The sequence of tile coordinates serving as the action's targets. Each coordinate identifies a tile where the movement/swap behaviour should be applied.

tasks.name: action
tasks.officialDescription: Executes 'sValue' action at X,Y (or triggering actor if unspecified). Uses 'sValue2' as caster if specified.

uses.description: Has the actor identified by strings[1] use the named action. The action is performed from that actor's point of view (consuming costs, playing animations, applying caster-relative effects) and is aimed at the supplied tile coordinate. If no tile coordinate is provided, the action is aimed at an off-map fallback position.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the action to perform.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The ID of the actor that should perform the action. Must reference an actor currently present, otherwise this branch is not taken.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The tile that the named actor should aim the action at.

uses.description: Executes the action directly through the zone manager without an actor casting it, so caster-specific costs and triggers are not applied. The action originates from and targets the same tile, defaulting to the triggering actor's position if no tile coordinate is given. This branch runs when strings[1] is provided but is empty or names an actor that does not exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the action to execute.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: Reserved slot that must be present (typically empty) for this branch to be reached. If it names an existing actor, the actor-cast branch runs instead.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The tile that the action originates from and targets. If omitted, the triggering actor's tile is used.

uses.description: The triggering actor casts the named action on themselves at their current position. This branch is taken when only the action ID is supplied, so it is the simplest form of having the task's own actor perform an action.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the action to cast on the triggering actor.

tasks.name: actionOnPartyNumActor
tasks.officialDescription: Executes 'sValue' action on the current landing party actor in slot 'fValue' (starting at 0).

uses.description: Locates the landing-party member in the indicated party slot and executes the named action centred on that actor's tile. The action runs through the zone manager without an explicit caster, so it does not consume the party member's resources or trigger caster-specific effects. If the party has fewer members than the requested slot, nothing happens.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the action to execute on the chosen party member.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: Zero-based index into the landing party identifying which member to target. Slot 0 is the first party member.

tasks.name: applyElement
tasks.officialDescription: Applies 'sValue' element at X,Y.

uses.description: Triggers a named elemental reaction on a tile, allowing the element (such as fire, water, electricity, etc.) to interact with that tile's surface and anything currently standing on it. The actual effects depend on the element's defined reactions and the contents of the target tile. If no target coordinate is supplied, the task targets the off-map fallback position (-1,-1), so it effectively does nothing visible.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The element to apply. Must match a defined Element enum value such as fire, water, lightning, etc.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The tile at which the element reaction occurs. If omitted, defaults to an off-map fallback tile.

tasks.name: applyElementToArea
tasks.officialDescription: Applies 'sValue' element to a rectangle starting at XY with width/height set by sValue2 (X,Y). fValue adds an incremental delay between tiles.

uses.description: Triggers a named elemental reaction across a rectangular block of tiles, applying the element to each tile in turn. The rectangle starts at the supplied tile coordinate and extends by the width and height encoded in the second string (in 'X,Y' format). A per-tile incremental delay can be supplied so the element appears to wash across the area rather than affecting every tile at once.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The element to apply across the area. Must match a defined Element enum value.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The dimensions of the rectangle, encoded as a TileCoord string 'X,Y' where X is the width and Y is the height (in tiles).

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: An incremental delay (in seconds) added between successive tile reactions. Each new tile waits this much longer than the previous one, producing a sweeping effect across the area.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The top-left tile coordinate of the rectangular area. If omitted, the area starts at the off-map fallback tile (-1,-1).

tasks.name: applyElementToZone
tasks.officialDescription: Applies 'sValue' element to entire zone. If bValue1, also applies to Actors in the zone.

uses.description: Triggers a named elemental reaction across every tile in the current zone in a single fast pass. Optionally, the reaction can also be applied to all actors in the zone, allowing the element to affect creatures and characters as well as terrain.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The element to apply zone-wide. Must match a defined Element enum value.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the elemental reaction is also applied to every actor in the zone (in addition to the tiles). If false or omitted, only tiles are affected.

tasks.name: setTileTerrain
tasks.officialDescription: Sets tile at X,Y to 'sValue' terrainTypeID. If bValue1, shows terrain change FX.

uses.description: Replaces the terrain type of a specific tile with a different terrain definition, immediately changing how that tile behaves (movement cost, blocking, visuals, etc.). An optional flag controls whether a visual FX flourish plays during the change.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The coordinate of the tile whose terrain type should be replaced.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The ID of the new terrainType for that tile. If omitted or empty, the tile is cleared to an empty/default terrain ID.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, plays a terrain-change FX flourish during the swap. If false or omitted, no FX is shown.

uses.description: Schedules a series of setTileTerrain operations to fill a rectangular area between two tile coordinates with the same terrain type. The corners are taken from tileCoords[0] and tileCoords[1] (or from strings[1] in 'X,Y' format), and the area is iterated row by row. An optional incremental delay can be set so the terrain change sweeps across the area over time, and an optional flag can reverse the order in which tiles are processed.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the new terrainType to apply across the area.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: An alternate way to supply the second corner of the rectangle as a TileCoord string 'X,Y'. If non-empty, this overrides tileCoords[1].

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: An incremental delay (in seconds) added between successive tiles. Each tile in the iteration waits this much longer than the previous one, producing a sweeping animation across the area.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, plays the terrain-change FX flourish on each tile as it is updated. If false or omitted, no FX is shown.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the order of tiles is reversed so the change progresses from the far corner back to the start.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The first corner of the rectangular area. If omitted, defaults to (-1,-1).

optional.name: tileCoords[1]
optional.description: The opposite corner of the rectangular area. If omitted, defaults to (-1,-1) unless overridden by strings[1].

tasks.name: script
tasks.officialDescription: Executes sValue.lua.

uses.description: Runs the named Lua script file via the LuaManager, allowing scripted logic to extend or override task behaviour. The script name is passed through to the Lua loader, which typically resolves it inside the mod's script directory.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the Lua script to execute (typically without the .lua extension).

tasks.name: consolecommand
tasks.officialDescription: Executes consolecommand sValue.

uses.description: Runs an in-game developer/debug console command by feeding the supplied text to the InputManager's console handler, then clears any debug-drawn strings left on screen. This is intended for cheat-style or scripted invocations of the same commands a developer would type into the console manually.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The full console command text to execute, exactly as it would be typed at the in-game console.

tasks.name: togglePause
tasks.officialDescription: Toggles pause of all actors and actions.

uses.description: Flips the global pause state for all actors and ongoing actions in the current zone, freezing or resuming them depending on the previous state. There are no inputs; calling the task always toggles, so two consecutive invocations cancel each other out.

tasks.name: preventInteraction
tasks.officialDescription: Disables player input for fValue.

uses.description: Blocks the player from interacting with the game (input is locked out) for the supplied number of seconds. Useful for short scripted moments where the player should watch but not act. If no duration is supplied, the delay defaults to 0 (no lockout).

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The duration, in seconds, that player interaction is blocked. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: delayActions
tasks.officialDescription: Disables actors and actions for fValue.

uses.description: Inserts a delay before the next action can occur, preventing all actors and pending actions from progressing for the supplied number of seconds. The delay is clamped to be at least as long as any delay already in flight, so calling this never shortens a longer pending delay. Defaults to 0 if no duration is supplied.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The duration, in seconds, that actors and actions are held off. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: cutsceneMode
tasks.officialDescription: cutsceneMode allows actors to keep moving/etc regardless of turn. If bValue1, cutsceneMode = true. Otherwise false.

uses.description: Toggles the zone's cutscene mode flag, which lets actors and actions continue to play regardless of the normal turn order. When cutscene mode becomes active, the enemy-turn speed-up toggle is also forcibly cleared so the cutscene plays at the intended pace. If no bool is supplied, cutscene mode is turned on; if a bool is supplied, cutscene mode follows that bool's value.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, enables cutscene mode. If false, disables it. If omitted entirely, cutscene mode is enabled.

tasks.name: timeSpeed
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the game speed to fValue.

uses.description: Sets the global time-speed multiplier used by the game loop. Larger values speed time up; smaller values slow it down. If no value is supplied, the speed defaults to 0 (effectively pausing time progression).

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The new global time-speed multiplier. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: setFactionHostile
tasks.officialDescription: Sets all actors of 'sValue' faction to hostile if fValue =1+. Otherwise sets faction nonhostile. If xValue != -1, clears dialogNode.

uses.description: Marks every actor belonging to the named faction as hostile, so they treat the player (and other appropriate factions) as enemies for the rest of combat / encounter logic. If a tile coordinate is supplied with an X other than -1, the task additionally clears the dialogNode that was driving the current conversation, ending any in-progress dialogue.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The faction whose actors should be set hostile.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: A numeric flag that must be 1 or greater to select this hostile branch. Values less than 1 select the non-hostile branch instead.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: If provided with an X coordinate other than -1, also clears the current dialogNode to end any active dialogue.

uses.description: Marks every actor belonging to the named faction as non-hostile, so they will no longer attack the player by default. If a tile coordinate is supplied with an X other than -1, the task additionally clears the dialogNode that was driving the current conversation, ending any in-progress dialogue.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The faction whose actors should be set non-hostile.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: If provided with an X coordinate other than -1, also clears the current dialogNode to end any active dialogue.

tasks.name: setFactionAI
tasks.officialDescription: Sets AI script of all actors of 'sValue' faction to 'sValue2'.

uses.description: Replaces the AI script of every actor in the named faction with a different AI script, immediately changing how those actors decide their actions. Useful for shifting a group from passive to aggressive behaviour (or vice versa) mid-scene.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The faction whose AI script should be swapped.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The ID of the new AI script to apply to every actor in that faction.

tasks.name: killFaction
tasks.officialDescription: Incaps all actors of 'sValue' faction.

uses.description: Incapacitates every actor belonging to the named faction, putting them into the downed/incap state and marking them as incapacitated. They are not removed from the zone; they remain as defeated bodies.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The faction whose actors should be incapacitated.

tasks.name: removeFaction
tasks.officialDescription: Removes all actors of 'sValue' faction.

uses.description: Removes every actor belonging to the named faction from the current zone, and also from the special (parallel) zone if one exists. Unlike killFaction, the actors are deleted outright rather than left as incapped bodies.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The faction whose actors should be removed from the zone.

tasks.name: changeFaction
tasks.officialDescription: Changes all actors of 'sValue' faction to 'sValue2' faction.

uses.description: Reassigns every actor that currently belongs to one faction so that they belong to a different faction instead. This is useful for switching allegiances mid-encounter (e.g. converting bandits to a friendly faction after a dialogue choice).

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The original faction whose actors should be reassigned.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The new faction to assign those actors to.

tasks.name: moveToNewZone
tasks.officialDescription: Cutscene & scripting use. Transfers party to 'sValue' zone at X,Y. fValue, if specified, sets the fade to black speed.

uses.description: Immediately transfers the entire party to the named zone, placing them at the supplied tile coordinate. This is intended for cutscenes and scripted transitions rather than ordinary travel, so it does not autosave or play the usual travel prompts. A short interaction lockout is enforced automatically so the player can't immediately act after the transfer.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the destination zone.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The fade-to-black speed used during the transition. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The tile within the destination zone where the party should arrive. If omitted, the destination is the off-map fallback tile (-1,-1).

tasks.name: travel
tasks.officialDescription: Travel-friendly trigger moves party to 'sValue' zone at X,Y. fValue = party facingRot. Autosaves unless bValue1=true

uses.description: Sends the party to the named zone at the given tile coordinate, integrating with the normal travel flow so it can prompt the player about fleeing if combat is active. An autosave is performed before the travel, unless explicitly disabled. The party's facing rotation in the destination zone is set from floats[0].

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the destination zone.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, suppresses the autosave that would otherwise occur before travelling. Defaults to false (autosave enabled).

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The party's facing rotation in the destination zone. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The tile in the destination zone where the party should arrive. If omitted, the destination is the off-map fallback tile (-1,-1).

tasks.name: travel_point
tasks.officialDescription: Travel-friendly trigger moves party to 'sValue' zone at point fValue. If bValue1, don't autosave. If bValue2, acts as if entered via console (doesn't roll loot tables, etc).

uses.description: Sends the party to a named zone, arriving at a specific 'travel point' index rather than at fixed tile coordinates. If combat is active, the player is prompted about fleeing first. An autosave runs before travel by default, and the entry can be flagged as 'via console' to skip random rolls (loot tables, encounters, etc.) that would normally occur on entry.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the destination zone.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The travel-point index identifying where in the zone the party arrives. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, suppresses the autosave that would otherwise occur before travelling.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the zone is treated as if entered via the developer console, skipping the loot/encounter rolls that normally fire on entry.

tasks.name: travel_point_g
tasks.officialDescription: As travel_point, but goes to zoneID of globalVar 'sValue'.

uses.description: Sends the party to the zone whose ID is stored in the supplied global variable, arriving at a specific 'travel point' index. Behaves like travel_point except that the destination zone is looked up indirectly through the global variable rather than naming the zone directly. Combat fleeing prompts, autosave, and console-entry flags work the same way.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable whose string value holds the ID of the destination zone.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The travel-point index identifying where in the zone the party arrives. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, suppresses the autosave that would otherwise occur before travelling.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the zone is treated as if entered via the developer console, skipping the loot/encounter rolls that normally fire on entry.

tasks.name: travel_leaveLocation
tasks.officialDescription: Moves party to where the current Location is. IMPORTANT: XY determines pos offset from that Location. If !bValue1, XY of -1,-1 is treated as 0,0.

uses.description: Exits the current sub-location, moving the party back out to the parent Location on the world map. The supplied tile coordinate is interpreted as an offset relative to the parent Location, not absolute coordinates. If bools[0] is false (or omitted) and no tile is supplied, the offset is treated as (0,0) rather than (-1,-1). If combat is active, the player is prompted about fleeing first; otherwise the leave is performed directly, with an optional autosave.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: Controls treatment of an unset tile coordinate. If false or omitted, an unset (-1,-1) tile is replaced with (0,0). If true, the unset value is left as (-1,-1).

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, suppresses the autosave that would otherwise occur during the leave. Defaults to false (autosave enabled).

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The offset from the parent Location at which the party should arrive. See bools[0] for treatment of the unset case.

tasks.name: travel_gVars
tasks.officialDescription: Travel to zone gVar 'playerPrevZone' at XY 'playerX', 'playerY'. fValue = facingRot.

uses.description: Sends the party back to the zone whose ID is stored in the 'playerPrevZone' global variable, placing them at the tile stored in the 'playerX' / 'playerY' global variables. Useful for scripted 'return to wherever the player came from' transitions. If combat is active, the player is prompted about fleeing first; otherwise an autosave runs unless suppressed.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, suppresses the autosave that would otherwise occur before travelling.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The party's facing rotation in the destination zone. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: travel_noSave
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Use 'travel' task instead.

uses.description: Sends the party to the named zone without performing an autosave. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description; modders should use 'travel' with the no-autosave flag instead. If combat is active, prompts the player about fleeing; otherwise calls both travelToZone and moveToNewZone in succession and enforces a brief interaction lockout afterwards.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the destination zone.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The party's facing rotation in the destination zone (also reused as the fade-to-black speed for the moveToNewZone follow-up).

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The tile in the destination zone where the party should arrive. If omitted, the destination is the off-map fallback tile (-1,-1).

tasks.name: travel_procedural
tasks.officialDescription: Travel-friendly trigger moves party to new procedural world made from levelData 'sValue', which leads to 'sValue2'. If bValue1, there is no way back. If XY set a specific returnXY. bValue2 uses trigger area as returnXY. If sValue2 matches a globalTriggerID the exit executes that trigger instead of a travel trigger. If sValue2 has 2 values (EX: z1, z2) the 2nd one is the zone entered from.

uses.description: Generates and travels to a freshly built procedural world from the supplied levelData template, recording where to send the party when they finally exit at the bottom. The 'return' coordinate (where the party reappears upon exiting) is taken from the supplied tile coordinate, or derived from the calling trigger's area if bools[1] is set. The second string may include a comma-separated 'bottomExitOverride' value identifying the zone considered as the entry-from when later returning.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The levelData ID used to procedurally build the new world.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The destination the bottom exit should lead to. May be a zone ID, a globalTriggerID, or two comma-separated values 'endDestinationZone,bottomExitOverride'.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, there is no way to return to the starting zone (the procedural world becomes a one-way trip).

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the calling trigger's area is used to compute the returnXY (bottom-mid of the trigger area) instead of the supplied tileCoord.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: Specifies the returnXY: the tile the party reappears at when exiting the procedural world. Ignored when bools[1] is true.

tasks.name: travel_procedural_point
tasks.officialDescription: As 'travel_procedural', but fValue specifies a travelPoint to place the party on.

uses.description: Generates and travels to a freshly built procedural world, like travel_procedural, but additionally specifies a travel-point index where the party should be placed on arrival. The 'return' coordinate (where the party reappears upon exiting) is taken from the supplied tile coordinate, or derived from the calling trigger's area if bools[1] is set. The second string may include a comma-separated 'bottomExitOverride' value.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The levelData ID used to procedurally build the new world.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The destination the bottom exit should lead to. May be a zone ID, a globalTriggerID, or two comma-separated values 'endDestinationZone,bottomExitOverride'.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The travel-point index identifying where in the generated world the party should be placed. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, there is no way to return to the starting zone (the procedural world becomes a one-way trip).

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the calling trigger's area is used to compute the returnXY (bottom-mid of the trigger area) instead of the supplied tileCoord.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: Specifies the returnXY: the tile the party reappears at when exiting the procedural world. Ignored when bools[1] is true.

tasks.name: travel_setReturnPath
tasks.officialDescription: Like 'travel', but sets the closest 'returnPath' trigger in the next zone to go back to this zone. If bValue, sets returnTC to the btm mid of the trigger area.

uses.description: Travels to the named zone exactly like the travel task, but also records a return path so that a future 'returnPath' task in the new zone can send the party back to this zone. By default, the return tile is taken from the supplied tile coordinate; if bools[0] is true, the return tile is computed as the bottom-mid of the calling trigger's area instead. Triggers an autosave and applies a short interaction lockout after the transition.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the destination zone.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The party's facing rotation in the destination zone (also passed as the int parameter when prompting about fleeing combat). Defaults to 0 if omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the return tile is taken from the bottom-mid of the calling trigger's area instead of the supplied tile coordinate.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The tile in the destination zone where the party should arrive (and, by default, the return tile recorded for a future returnPath).

tasks.name: returnPath
tasks.officialDescription: Travel trigger that is set by 'travel_setReturnPath'.

uses.description: Returns the party to a zone previously recorded by a travel_setReturnPath task. If no destination string is supplied (or it is empty), the task does nothing; otherwise it autosaves and moves the party to that zone, optionally using a recorded tile and facing rotation. While combat is active, triggers a fleeing prompt instead of travelling immediately.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The ID of the destination zone (typically supplied by the system when this trigger is fired by a previous travel_setReturnPath). If empty or omitted, the task does nothing.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The party's facing rotation in the destination zone (also passed as the int parameter when prompting about fleeing combat). Defaults to 0 if omitted.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The tile in the destination zone where the party should arrive.

tasks.name: travel_g
tasks.officialDescription: Travel to the zone name stored in globalVar 'sValue'.

uses.description: Sends the party to whichever zone is currently named by the 'lastProcZoneEntry' global variable (the destination is read from this fixed global variable rather than from any task input). Useful for returning from a procedural world to whatever zone last produced one. If combat is active, the player is prompted about fleeing first; otherwise an autosave is performed and the party is moved to the destination zone.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The party's facing rotation in the destination zone (also passed as the int parameter when prompting about fleeing combat). Defaults to 0 if omitted.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The tile in the destination zone where the party should arrive. If omitted, the destination is the off-map fallback tile (-1,-1).

tasks.name: setGlobalVar
tasks.officialDescription: Sets globalVar sValue to fValue (or sValue2 if specified).

uses.description: Stores a string value into a named global variable. The variable is set to whatever is in strings[1] verbatim, overwriting any previous value. Use this form when the global variable should hold text, IDs, or other non-numeric data.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to write.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The string value to assign. Must be non-empty; if empty, the numeric form (using floats[0]) is used instead.

uses.description: Stores a numeric value into a named global variable. The variable is set to whatever is in floats[0]. This branch is selected when strings[1] is omitted or empty.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to write.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The numeric value to assign to the global variable.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_math
tasks.officialDescription: Sets globalVar sValue to Formula in sValue2. Decimals will be truncated. If b1, decimals won't be truncated.

uses.description: Evaluates a formula and stores its numeric result into a named global variable. The variable is first reset to 0, then the formula's value is added to it (effectively assigning the result). By default decimals are truncated to an integer; setting bools[0] preserves the full float value.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to write.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The formula string to evaluate.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the result of the formula is stored without truncating decimals. If false or omitted, the result is truncated to an integer.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toOtherGlobalVar
tasks.officialDescription: Sets globalVar sValue to globalVar sValue2.

uses.description: Copies the value of one named global variable into another. Reads the current value of strings[1] (as a string) and assigns it to strings[0]. Useful for snapshotting, swapping, or aliasing global state.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the destination global variable (where the value is written).

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The name of the source global variable (whose string value is read).

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toNearestLocID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the nearest discoverable non-port location's ID within fValue radius of X,Y in the overworld zone set in sValue2 (defaults to eral if not set) to the gVar set in sValue. If X,Y is unset, will use player's X,Y. You can specify tags the location needs to contain in comma,separated,values after the overworld in sValue2 (eg, sValue2 = eral,grove). If b1, will also check locations that can't be discovered. If b2, locations must NOT have any of the tags set.

uses.description: Searches the named overworld zone for the closest non-port Location within the supplied radius of an origin tile, and writes its ID into the named global variable. Tag filters can be appended after the overworld name as comma-separated values, with bools[1] flipping the filter from 'must contain these tags' to 'must NOT contain these tags'. Has no effect if strings[0] or floats[0] is missing.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to receive the located Location's ID.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The maximum search radius (in tiles) within which a Location is considered.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The overworld zone ID to search within, optionally followed by comma-separated tag values. Defaults to 'eral' if omitted or empty. Example: 'eral,grove' searches the eral overworld for locations tagged 'grove'.

optional.name: strings[2+]
optional.description: Additional tag values that are appended (comma-separated) onto strings[1]'s tag list.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, locations that are not yet discoverable are also considered. If false or omitted, only discoverable locations are searched.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the tag filter is inverted: matching locations must NOT contain any of the supplied tags. If false or omitted, locations must contain the supplied tags.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The origin tile for the radius search. If omitted (or X is -1), defaults to the current actor's position.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toNearestPortID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the nearest discoverable port location's ID within fValue radius of X,Y in the overworld zone set in sValue2 (defaults to eral if not set) to the gVar set in sValue. If X,Y is unset, will use player's X,Y. You can specify tags the location needs to contain in comma,separated,values after the overworld in sValue2 (eg, sValue2 = eral,hidden). If b1, will also check locations that can't be discovered. If b2, locations must NOT have any of the tags set.

uses.description: Like setGlobalVar_toNearestLocID, but restricts the search to port Locations (sea/coast-accessible) only and excludes regular land locations. Writes the closest such port's ID into the named global variable. Has no effect if strings[0] or floats[0] is missing.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to receive the located port Location's ID.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The maximum search radius (in tiles) within which a port Location is considered.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The overworld zone ID to search within, optionally followed by comma-separated tag values. Defaults to 'eral' if omitted or empty.

optional.name: strings[2+]
optional.description: Additional tag values that are appended (comma-separated) onto strings[1]'s tag list.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, ports that are not yet discoverable are also considered. If false or omitted, only discoverable ports are searched.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the tag filter is inverted: matching ports must NOT contain any of the supplied tags. If false or omitted, ports must contain the supplied tags.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The origin tile for the radius search. If omitted (or X is -1), defaults to the current actor's position.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toNearestRandomLocID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets a random discoverable non-port location's ID that is within fValue radius of X,Y and in the overworld zone set in sValue2 (defaults to eral if not set) to the gVar set in sValue. If X,Y is unset, will use player's X,Y. You can specify tags the location needs to contain in comma,separated,values after the overworld in sValue2 (eg, sValue2 = eral,grove). If b1, will also check locations that can't be discovered. If b2, locations must NOT have any of the tags set.

uses.description: Like setGlobalVar_toNearestLocID, but chooses a random non-port Location from those that pass the filters within the supplied radius instead of returning the strictly closest one. Writes the chosen Location's ID into the named global variable. Has no effect if strings[0] or floats[0] is missing.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to receive the chosen Location's ID.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The maximum search radius (in tiles) within which Locations are considered.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The overworld zone ID to search within, optionally followed by comma-separated tag values. Defaults to 'eral' if omitted or empty.

optional.name: strings[2+]
optional.description: Additional tag values that are appended (comma-separated) onto strings[1]'s tag list.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, locations that are not yet discoverable are also considered. If false or omitted, only discoverable locations are searched.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the tag filter is inverted: matching locations must NOT contain any of the supplied tags. If false or omitted, locations must contain the supplied tags.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The origin tile for the radius search. If omitted (or X is -1), defaults to the current actor's position.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toNearestRandomPortID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets a random discoverable port location's ID that is within fValue radius of X,Y and in the overworld zone set in sValue2 (defaults to eral if not set) to the gVar set in sValue. If X,Y is unset, will use player's X,Y. You can specify tags the location needs to contain in comma,separated,values after the overworld in sValue2 (eg, sValue2 = eral,hidden). If b1, will also check locations that can't be discovered. If b2, locations must NOT have any of the tags set.

uses.description: Like setGlobalVar_toNearestPortID, but chooses a random port from those that pass the filters within the supplied radius instead of returning the strictly closest one. Writes the chosen port's ID into the named global variable. Has no effect if strings[0] or floats[0] is missing.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to receive the chosen port Location's ID.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The maximum search radius (in tiles) within which port Locations are considered.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The overworld zone ID to search within, optionally followed by comma-separated tag values. Defaults to 'eral' if omitted or empty.

optional.name: strings[2+]
optional.description: Additional tag values that are appended (comma-separated) onto strings[1]'s tag list.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, ports that are not yet discoverable are also considered. If false or omitted, only discoverable ports are searched.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the tag filter is inverted: matching ports must NOT contain any of the supplied tags. If false or omitted, ports must contain the supplied tags.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The origin tile for the radius search. If omitted (or X is -1), defaults to the current actor's position.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarToZoneProperty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets globalVar 'sValue' to the string representation of the current zone's property, set in sValue2.

uses.description: Looks up a named property on the current Zone object via reflection-style data access and stores its string representation into a named global variable. Useful for pulling zone-level metadata (name, region, weather state, etc.) into a global that can be referenced elsewhere. Has no effect if there is no current zone.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to receive the property's string value.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The name of the property on the current zone to read. Must match a property the data lookup can resolve on the Zone class.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toPartyActorID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets globalVar sValue to ID of partyActor[fValue].

uses.description: Writes the ID of the party member at the given index into a named global variable. If the party has fewer members than the requested index, the variable is set to an empty string instead. The index is 0-based.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to receive the party member's ID.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: Zero-based index into the landing party identifying which member's ID to read. Defaults to 0 if omitted (first party member).

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toActorID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets globalVar sValue to ID of the Actor that executed the trigger.

uses.description: Stores the ID of the actor that triggered this task into a named global variable. If no triggering actor is known (actorID is empty), the variable is set to an empty string instead.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to receive the triggering actor's ID.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toCrewActorID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the ID of the crew Actor in the 'fValue'-th slot (starting at 0) of the ship Actor specified in 'sValue2'.

uses.description: Looks up the crew member at a specified slot of a named ship actor and writes that crew actor's ID into a named global variable. If the ship actor does not exist, has no crew list, or the slot is out of range, the variable is set to an empty string. If strings[1] is omitted entirely, the lookup is skipped and the variable is also set to empty.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to receive the crew actor's ID.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The ID of the ship actor whose crew list is read. If omitted, the variable is set to an empty string.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: Zero-based index into the ship's crew list identifying which crew actor to read. Required when strings[1] is provided (the underlying code assumes it exists in that case).

tasks.name: setGlobalVarWithCommas
tasks.officialDescription: Sets globalVar strings[0] to the remaining combined strings[] values, joined with commas between each.

uses.description: Joins every string from strings[1] onward into a single comma-separated value and stores the result into the named global variable. Useful for storing a list of related IDs/tags in one variable. If only strings[0] is provided, the variable is set to an empty string.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to write.

optional.name: strings[1+]
optional.description: Any number of additional strings; each is concatenated together with commas between them and stored as the variable's value.

tasks.name: clearGlobalVar
tasks.officialDescription: Removes the globalVar 'sValue' from the list of globalVars.

uses.description: Removes the named global variable from the global variable table entirely, so that subsequent reads return the default unset value (rather than just being set to 0 or empty). Useful for resetting state when a quest or flag should be considered untouched.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to remove.

tasks.name: sanitizeGlobalVar
tasks.officialDescription: Removes all characters that appear in sValue2 from the gVar set in sValue. If b1, only KEEPS characters that appear in sValue2 instead. Comparisons are case-insensitive. If sValue2 is blank or unset, runs the standard sanitize on it (spaces, periods, commas, math operators and colons), which also sets it to all lower case.

uses.description: Cleans up the string value of a named global variable by filtering its characters. If strings[1] is empty or omitted, runs the standard sanitize (strips spaces, periods, commas, math operators, and colons, and lower-cases the result). If strings[1] is supplied, the filter is applied character-by-character: by default characters in strings[1] are stripped, but with bools[0] = true the filter is inverted so only characters in strings[1] are kept. After filtering, a remaining value of '0' is normalised to an empty string.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to sanitize in place.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: A set of characters used as the filter. If empty or omitted, the standard sanitize runs instead.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the filter is inverted: only characters appearing in strings[1] are kept; all others are stripped. If false or omitted, characters in strings[1] are stripped.

tasks.name: modGlobalVar
tasks.officialDescription: Adds 'fValue' to globalVar 'sValue'.

uses.description: Adds a numeric delta to the current value of a named global variable. The variable is interpreted numerically; the delta can be positive or negative. Useful for incrementing counters, accumulating scores, or applying small adjustments without overwriting the existing value.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to modify.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The numeric value to add (or subtract, if negative) to the variable's current value.

tasks.name: modNodeIDGlobalVar_1
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used in dialog. Adds 1 to the globalVar with the same ID as the currentDialogNodeID.

uses.description: Adds 1 to a global variable whose name matches the ID of the dialog node currently being executed. This is the standard way to record that the player has visited or chosen a particular dialog branch, so other tasks/conditions can check how many times it has happened.

tasks.name: modNodeIDGlobalVar_999
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used in dialog. Adds 999 to the globalVar with the same ID as the currentDialogNodeID.

uses.description: Adds 999 to a global variable whose name matches the ID of the dialog node currently being executed. Used to permanently mark a dialog branch as 'consumed' (since 999 is effectively unreachable through normal increments).

tasks.name: appendToGlobalVar_math
tasks.officialDescription: Appends the result of Formula 'sValue2' to the end of globalVar 'sValue'.

uses.description: Evaluates a formula and appends the numeric result to the current string value of a named global variable. The result is concatenated as text, so e.g. appending 5 to '3' produces '35', not '8'. Useful for building up multi-digit or multi-value strings.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable whose value will be appended to.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The formula string whose calculated result is appended.

tasks.name: appendToGlobalVar_string
tasks.officialDescription: Appends 'sValue2' to the end of globalVar 'sValue'.

uses.description: Concatenates the supplied text onto the current string value of a named global variable. Useful for building up strings or accumulating CSV-style lists without erasing the existing value.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable whose value will be appended to.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The string to append to the variable's current value.

tasks.name: appendToGlobalVar_zoneID
tasks.officialDescription: Appends the ID of the current zone to the end of globalVar 'sValue'.

uses.description: Appends the ID of the current zone onto the current string value of a named global variable. Useful for recording a sequence of zones the player has been in.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable whose value will be appended to.

tasks.name: appendToGlobalVar_locID
tasks.officialDescription: Appends the ID of the current location to the end of globalVar 'sValue'.

uses.description: Appends the ID of the current Location onto the current string value of a named global variable. Has no effect if the player is not currently in a Location. Useful for recording a sequence of locations visited.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable whose value will be appended to.

tasks.name: setNodeIDGlobalVar_1
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used in dialog. Sets the globalVar with the same ID as the currentDialogNodeID to 1.

uses.description: Sets a global variable named the same as the currently executing dialog node's ID to 1, but only if it isn't already 1. This is the canonical way to mark a dialog node as 'visited' without overwriting any larger value that might have been set by other tasks.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarTo0
tasks.officialDescription: Sets globalVar 'sValue' to 0.

uses.description: Resets a named global variable to the numeric value 0. Convenience form of setGlobalVar when the intended value is known to be zero.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to reset to 0.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarToTalkerID
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used as a dialog specialEffect. Sets the globalVar 'strings[0]' to the currentActorTalkingTo's ID.

uses.description: Stores the ID of the actor the player is currently talking to into a named global variable. Intended for use as a dialog specialEffect so the dialog target's identity is remembered after the conversation ends.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to receive the talker's ID.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarWithTalkerID
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used as a dialog specialEffect. Sets globalVar 'sValue' + the currentActorTalkingTo's ID to the string set in sValue2, or 'fValue' if not set.

uses.description: Composes a global variable name by concatenating strings[0] with the current talker's actor ID, then writes a value into that composite-named variable. The value is strings[1] if it is provided and non-empty, otherwise the numeric value floats[0]. Useful for storing per-NPC state without colliding between different actors.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: A prefix that, combined with the current talker's actor ID, forms the actual global variable name to write.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The string value to write into the composite variable. If empty or omitted, floats[0] is used instead.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The numeric value to write into the composite variable when strings[1] is empty/omitted. Defaults to 0.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarToLocID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets globalVar 'sValue' to the ID of the current location. Doesn't get set if not in a location.

uses.description: Stores the ID of the current Location into a named global variable. Has no effect (does not overwrite) if the player is not currently in a Location. Useful for recording where a quest event happened.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to receive the current location's ID.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarToPartyActorNum
tasks.officialDescription: Sets globalVar 'sValue' to 'fValue'.

uses.description: Writes a numeric value into a named global variable. Despite the task's name, the value written is simply floats[0] verbatim, with no special party-actor lookup — equivalent to the numeric form of setGlobalVar.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable to write.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The numeric value to assign.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarPlusLocID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the globalVar with the ID of 'sValue' + the current location's ID to 'fValue'.

uses.description: Composes a global variable name by concatenating strings[0] with the current Location's ID, then writes a numeric value into that composite-named variable. Useful for storing per-location state (e.g. quest progress at a particular town).

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: A prefix that, combined with the current location's ID, forms the actual global variable name to write.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The numeric value to assign to the composite-named variable.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarPlusLocID_math
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the globalVar with the ID of 'sValue' + the current location's ID to the result of the formula set in sValue2.

uses.description: Composes a global variable name by concatenating strings[0] with the current Location's ID, evaluates a formula, and stores the formula's numeric result into that composite-named variable. The variable is first reset to 0 then modified by the formula's value, effectively assigning the result.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: A prefix that, combined with the current location's ID, forms the actual global variable name to write.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The formula string whose calculated value is stored in the composite-named variable.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarPlusZoneID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the globalVar with the ID of 'sValue' + the current zone's ID to 'fValue'.

uses.description: Composes a global variable name by concatenating strings[0] with the current Zone's ID, then writes a numeric value into that composite-named variable. Useful for storing per-zone state.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: A prefix that, combined with the current zone's ID, forms the actual global variable name to write.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The numeric value to assign to the composite-named variable.

tasks.name: modFame
tasks.officialDescription: Adds 'fValue' to globalVar 'fame_' + 'sValue', displays its associated icon and plays a sound.

uses.description: Adjusts a named 'fame' tracker by a numeric delta. Internally this modifies a global variable named 'fame_<sValue>', and also displays the fame's associated icon notification and plays a sound effect so the player sees the change occur.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The fame tracker's name (without the 'fame_' prefix). For example, supplying 'pirate' modifies the 'fame_pirate' global variable.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The amount of fame to add (or subtract, if negative).

tasks.name: goToNodeID_plus_GlobalVar
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used in dialog. Tries to go to the dialogNode with the ID of the current dialogNode, plus the string set in the globalVar with the same ID as the dialogNode ID.

uses.description: Branches dialog to a node whose ID is computed by appending the value of a global variable (whose name matches the current dialog node's ID) onto the current dialog node's ID. Used for dynamic dialog branching where the next node depends on prior state stored in a global variable.

tasks.name: enableUI
tasks.officialDescription: Shows the quickslots and character stats windows if they had been previously hidden with 'disableUI'.

uses.description: Re-shows the quickslots bar, character stats window, and the general HUD if they had been hidden by disableUI. Use after a cutscene or scripted sequence to restore the player's normal interface.

tasks.name: disableUI
tasks.officialDescription: Hides the quickslots and character stats windows. Show them again with 'enableUI' task.

uses.description: Hides the quickslots bar, the character stats window, the general HUD, and any small tabbed windows that are open. Use during cutscenes or scripted sequences to declutter the screen; restore later with enableUI.

tasks.name: unlockHelpGem
tasks.officialDescription: Unlocks use of the Help Gem and recharges it.

uses.description: Marks the Help Gem as unlocked and enabled, and resets its recharge timer to 0 so it is immediately usable. Typically called once early in the game when the player first acquires the Help Gem.

tasks.name: enableHelpGem
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Enables use of the Help Gem (if recharged).

uses.description: Sets the Help Gem to the enabled state, allowing the player to use it once its recharge timer reaches 0. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description; unlockHelpGem is preferred for first-time enabling.

tasks.name: disableHelpGem
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Disables use of the Help Gem.

uses.description: Disables the Help Gem so the player cannot use it, and forces its recharge timer to -1 so it never finishes charging. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: save
tasks.officialDescription: Saves to Globals.AUTOSAVE_NAME, and notifies player. Saves to slot sValue if specified (or unused variant if save sValue already exists).

uses.description: Writes the game to a named save slot. If a save with the supplied name already exists, the task appends one or more '+' characters to the name until it finds an unused slot, so existing saves are never overwritten by this branch.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The base save name to write. If a save with this name already exists, '+' is appended until an unused variant is found.

uses.description: Performs an autosave (to the standard autosave slot) with a player-facing notification, ignoring hardcore mode restrictions that would normally suppress saving. This branch runs when no save name is supplied or it is empty.

tasks.name: quietsave
tasks.officialDescription: Saves to Globals.AUTOSAVE_NAME, with no notification.

uses.description: Performs an autosave to the standard autosave slot silently, with no on-screen notification, ignoring hardcore mode restrictions that would normally suppress saving. Useful as a checkpoint that doesn't interrupt the player.

tasks.name: heal
tasks.officialDescription: Heals triggering actor, or actor 'sValue', by fValue.

uses.description: Heals a specifically named actor for a set amount of HP. If floats[0] is 0, it is treated as 9999 (effectively a full heal). The named actor must currently exist; if they do not, nothing happens.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor to heal.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The amount of HP to restore. A value of 0 is treated as 9999 (effectively full heal).

uses.description: Heals the triggering actor for a set amount of HP. If floats[0] is 0, it is treated as 9999 (effectively a full heal). This branch runs when no actor ID is supplied (or it is empty).

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The amount of HP to restore. A value of 0 is treated as 9999 (effectively full heal).

uses.description: Performs a full heal on the entire player party (equivalent to healparty). This branch runs when no float is supplied at all.

tasks.name: healparty
tasks.officialDescription: Heals playerParty, without restoring HPMax lost from being KO'd.

uses.description: Fully restores HP for every member of the player party, but does not undo any HPMax penalty incurred from being knocked out. Use when the party should recover from current damage without removing long-term incap consequences.

tasks.name: fullrestore
tasks.officialDescription: Fully heals playerParty, including HPMax lost from being KO'd.

uses.description: Fully restores HP for every member of the player party, additionally clearing any HPMax penalty incurred from being knocked out. Use when the party should be completely refreshed, including long-term incap consequences.

tasks.name: revive
tasks.officialDescription: Revives triggering actor, or actor 'sValue', by fValue.

uses.description: Revives a specifically named actor (clearing their incapacitated state) and restores HP equal to floats[0]. If floats[0] is 0, it is treated as 9999 (effectively a full revive). If bools[0] is true, the 'cure' AV effect is also applied to clear status conditions. Has no effect if the named actor does not currently exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor to revive.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The amount of HP to restore alongside the revive. A value of 0 (or omitted) is treated as 9999 (effectively full HP).

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, additionally applies the 'cure' AV effect to clear status conditions in addition to the revive.

uses.description: Revives the triggering actor (clearing their incapacitated state) and restores HP equal to floats[0]. If floats[0] is 0, it is treated as 9999 (effectively a full revive). If bools[0] is true, the 'cure' AV effect is also applied. This branch runs when no actor ID is supplied.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The amount of HP to restore alongside the revive. A value of 0 (or omitted) is treated as 9999 (effectively full HP).

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, additionally applies the 'cure' AV effect to clear status conditions in addition to the revive.

tasks.name: fx
tasks.officialDescription: Plays fx 'sValue' at X,Y. 'sValue2' uses that actor's position instead. If nothing specified, plays fx at camera position. If b1, X,Y is screen position. If used in a task, string 3 sets fx color and a second TC specifies fx target.

uses.description: Plays a named visual effect anchored on the position of a specifically named actor. If the named actor does not currently exist, nothing happens. The optional color string controls the FX tint (defaults to 'White' if empty).

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the FX to play.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The ID of the actor on whose position the FX should be anchored.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The FX color (tint). If omitted or empty, defaults to 'White'.

uses.description: Plays a named visual effect anchored at one tile coordinate and targeted at a second tile coordinate (useful for projectiles or directional effects). The second tile coordinate must be present with X != -1 for this branch to run.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the FX to play.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The origin tile coordinate for the FX.

required.name: tileCoords[1]
required.description: The target tile coordinate for the FX. Must have X != -1.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The FX color (tint). If omitted or empty, defaults to 'White'.

uses.description: Plays a named visual effect at a screen-space position derived from a tile coordinate, rather than at a world position. This branch runs when bools[0] is true and a tile coordinate is supplied without an actor or second tile.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the FX to play.

required.name: bools[0]
required.description: Must be true to select this screen-position branch.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The tile coordinate whose corresponding screen position the FX should play at.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The FX color (tint). If omitted or empty, defaults to 'White'.

uses.description: Plays a named visual effect at a single world tile coordinate. This is the default branch when a tile is supplied without an actor, second tile, or screen-position flag.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the FX to play.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The tile coordinate at which the FX should be played.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The FX color (tint). If omitted or empty, defaults to 'White'.

uses.description: Plays a named visual effect at the current camera position (the centre of the player's view). This branch runs when neither an actor nor a tile coordinate is supplied.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the FX to play.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The FX color (tint). If omitted or empty, defaults to 'White'.

tasks.name: sfx
tasks.officialDescription: Play unlooped sound FX 'sValue' at volume 1, pitch 0.

uses.description: Plays a named sound effect once, with volume 1 and pitch 0 (no looping). The sound is played globally rather than at a particular position. Has no effect if no sound name is supplied.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the sound effect to play.

tasks.name: fxOnTalker
tasks.officialDescription: Plays fx 'sValue' at the X,Y of the currentActorTalkingTo.

uses.description: Plays a named visual effect anchored on the position of the actor the player is currently talking to in dialog. Intended for use during dialog so the FX appears on the conversation partner. Has no effect if there is no current talker or no FX name is supplied.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the FX to play on the current talker.

tasks.name: fxOnParty
tasks.officialDescription: Plays fx 'sValue' at the X,Y of each partyActor.

uses.description: Plays a named visual effect at every party member's current position, simultaneously. Useful for whole-party effects like collective buffs or healing visuals. Has no effect if no FX name is supplied.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the FX to play on each party member.

tasks.name: damageNumber
tasks.officialDescription: Displays 'sValue' text above actor 'sValue2's head for 2 + fValue seconds. If sValue2 is blank, uses current player actor. Can set text color using 'actorID,color'. If X,Y is set, displays the text at that TC instead. If b1, text doesn't bounce. If b2, 'sValue' should be a gVar and its value is used as the text instead.

uses.description: Displays floating text (like a damage number) above an actor or at a specific tile, for 2 + floats[0] seconds. The text is strings[0] verbatim by default. If a tile coordinate is supplied, the text appears at that tile; otherwise it appears above the named actor (or the player if strings[1] is empty). The color can be supplied either as a third string, by encoding 'actorID,color' in strings[1], or via RGB triples in additional floats.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The text to display. If bools[1] is true, this is treated as the name of a global variable whose value is displayed instead. Defaults to empty.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The ID of the actor over whose head the text should appear (defaults to the player if empty). May also be written as 'actorID,color' to combine the actor ID and color into one string.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The display color of the text.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: Extra duration (in seconds) added to a base of 2 seconds for how long the text remains visible. Defaults to 0.

optional.name: floats[1+]
optional.description: When floats[0] is unused for duration, an RGB triple in floats[1], floats[2], floats[3] can supply the color instead (in 'R,G,B' format).

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the text does not bounce as it rises. If false or omitted, the text bounces.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, strings[0] is treated as a global variable name and its value is displayed instead of the literal string.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: If supplied, the text is displayed at this tile rather than above the named actor.

tasks.name: damageNumber_worldTime
tasks.officialDescription: Displays the current time above actor 'sValue's head for 2 + fValue seconds. If sValue is blank, uses current player actor. Can set text color in sValue2. If X,Y is set, displays the text at that TC instead. If b1, text doesn't bounce.

uses.description: Displays the current in-game hour:minute as floating text above an actor (or at a specific tile), for 2 + floats[0] seconds. Behaves like damageNumber except the text is always the current time rather than supplied text. If a tile coordinate is provided, the text appears at that tile; otherwise it appears above the named actor (or the player if strings[0] is empty).

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The ID of the actor over whose head the time text should appear. Defaults to the current player if empty or omitted.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The display color of the text.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: Extra duration (in seconds) added to a base of 2 seconds for how long the text remains visible. Defaults to 0.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the text does not bounce as it rises. If false or omitted, the text bounces.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: If supplied, the text is displayed at this tile rather than above the named actor.

tasks.name: animate
tasks.officialDescription: Animates triggering actor (or actor 'sValue2') with animation 'sValue'. Sets busyTimer to fValue if specified.

uses.description: Plays a named animation on a specified actor (or the triggering actor if no actor ID is given). If floats[0] is greater than 0, the actor's stun is cleared and their busy timer is set to that many seconds, so they are unavailable until the animation completes. Has no effect if neither a named actor nor a triggering actor is available.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the animation to play.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The ID of the actor to animate. If omitted or empty, the triggering actor is animated.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: If greater than 0, clears the actor's stun state and sets their busy timer to this many seconds, locking them out of acting until the animation finishes.

tasks.name: textOverlay
tasks.officialDescription: Sets sValue as overlaid text. If sValue = '', clears overlaid text. fValue (0-255) is the alpha of the black behind the overlay. xValue & yValue offset the text. If bValue1, centers text Xpos. Bvalue2, Ypos.

uses.description: Displays text as a UI overlay on top of the screen, with a black backdrop whose opacity is controlled by floats[0] (0-255). Supplying an empty string for the text clears any current overlay. The tile coordinate's X/Y are used as pixel offsets for the text position; bools[0] and bools[1] can force the text to be centred horizontally or vertically, respectively.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The overlay text to display. If empty, the current overlay is cleared.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The alpha (0-255) of the black backdrop behind the overlay text. Defaults to 0 (no backdrop) if omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the text is centred horizontally on the screen, overriding the X offset.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the text is centred vertically on the screen, overriding the Y offset.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The X and Y of this coordinate are used as pixel offsets for the overlay text position. Defaults to (0,0) if omitted.

tasks.name: textOverlay_tween
tasks.officialDescription: Tweens in sValue as overlaid text. If sValue = '', tweens out. fValue (0-255) is the alpha of the black behind the overlay. xValue & yValue offset the text.

uses.description: Tweens (animates in) text as a UI overlay on top of the screen, with a black backdrop whose opacity is controlled by floats[0] (0-255). Supplying an empty string for the text tweens the overlay back out instead. The tile coordinate's X/Y are used as pixel offsets for the text position.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The overlay text to tween in. If empty, the existing overlay tweens out.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The alpha (0-255) of the black backdrop behind the overlay text. Defaults to 0 (no backdrop) if omitted.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The X and Y of this coordinate are used as pixel offsets for the overlay text position. Defaults to (0,0) if omitted.

tasks.name: textOverlay_dynamic
tasks.officialDescription: Same as 'textOverlay', except reveals text dynamically.

uses.description: Displays text as a UI overlay on top of the screen, revealing it dynamically (typewriter-style) rather than all at once. The black backdrop opacity is controlled by floats[0] (0-255). The tile coordinate's X/Y are used as pixel offsets; bools[0] and bools[1] can centre the text horizontally and/or vertically.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The overlay text to reveal dynamically. Defaults to empty.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The alpha (0-255) of the black backdrop behind the overlay text. Defaults to 0 (no backdrop) if omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the text is centred horizontally on the screen, overriding the X offset.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the text is centred vertically on the screen, overriding the Y offset.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The X and Y of this coordinate are used as pixel offsets for the overlay text position. Defaults to (0,0) if omitted.

tasks.name: pointCameraAt
tasks.officialDescription: Points camera at X,Y, or actor.ID if X,Y is unspecified. fValue alters speed if specified. bValue1 sets cameraCantBeChangedTimer to delay_preventInteraction. If bValue2, camera goes under roofs.

uses.description: Snaps or scrolls the camera to focus on a specific tile coordinate, optionally adjusting the camera's tracking speed. Also reveals fog of war around the tile from the triggering actor's perspective. After the move, bools[0] can lock the camera in place for the duration of any active interaction lockout, and bools[1] forces the camera under roofs.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The tile coordinate the camera should look at.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: Tracking speed for the camera move. If non-zero, overrides the default tracking speed.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the camera is locked from further changes for the duration of the current interaction lockout.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the camera is allowed to descend under roofs.

uses.description: Scrolls the camera to track a named actor. If the actor exists, the camera follows them; otherwise, falls back to the triggering actor or the current actor. After the move, bools[0] can lock the camera in place for the duration of any active interaction lockout, and bools[1] forces the camera under roofs.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor for the camera to track.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: Tracking speed for the camera. Currently only applied when a tile coordinate is supplied, but accepted here for symmetry.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the camera is locked from further changes for the duration of the current interaction lockout.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the camera is allowed to descend under roofs.

tasks.name: lockCameraAt
tasks.officialDescription: Points camera at X,Y, or actor.ID if X,Y is unspecified. fValue alters speed if specified. Locks camera indefinitely. If bValue1, camera goes under roofs.

uses.description: Snaps or scrolls the camera to focus on a specific tile coordinate (like pointCameraAt) and locks it there indefinitely. Also reveals fog of war around the tile from the triggering actor's perspective. bools[0] forces the camera under roofs.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The tile coordinate the camera should look at and lock onto.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: Tracking speed for the camera move. If non-zero, overrides the default tracking speed.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the camera is allowed to descend under roofs.

uses.description: Locks the camera to track the triggering actor (or the current actor if there is no triggering actor). Falls back when no tile coordinate is supplied. bools[0] forces the camera under roofs.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: Tracking speed for the camera. Currently only applied when a tile coordinate is supplied, but accepted here for symmetry.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the camera is allowed to descend under roofs.

tasks.name: scaleCameraTo
tasks.officialDescription: Sets camera target scale to fValue, scaling at speed X/10. If speed=0 or less, sets scale instantly.

uses.description: Animates the camera's zoom toward a new scale value over time. The tile coordinate's X is divided by 10 to determine the tracking speed. Used when X > 0 to gradually scale the view.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The target camera scale value.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The X of this coordinate (divided by 10) is the scaling speed. Must be > 0 to select this animated branch.

uses.description: Sets the camera's scale instantly to the supplied value, with no animation. This branch runs when no tile is supplied or its X is 0 or negative.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The new camera scale value. Defaults to 0 if omitted (which would make the camera invisible).

tasks.name: setRoofDrawNoMatterWhat
tasks.officialDescription: Sets camera.drawRoofsNoMatterWhat to bValue1.

uses.description: Toggles the camera's force-draw-roofs flag. When true, roofs are always rendered even in situations where they would normally be hidden (such as the player walking inside a building).

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, roofs are always drawn. If false or omitted, roof drawing returns to its normal context-sensitive behaviour.

tasks.name: cancelIfEnemiesPresent
tasks.officialDescription: Cancels further effects if any non-incapped hostiles are within 'fValue' of triggering actor or X,Y. If fValue=0, distance is infinite. If sValue1 specified, only checks actors with that actorTypeID.

uses.description: Aborts the remainder of the current task list if any hostile, non-incapped actors are within range of an origin tile. The origin is the supplied tile coordinate (if any) or the triggering actor's position. If floats[0] is 0, the range check is treated as infinite. Optionally restricts the check to actors of a specific actorTypeID.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: Only actors with this actorTypeID are considered. If empty or omitted, all hostile actors are considered.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The detection range, in tiles. A value of 0 (or omitted) is treated as infinite range.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The origin tile from which to measure the range. If omitted, the triggering actor's tile is used.

tasks.name: cancelIfEnemiesNotPresent
tasks.officialDescription: Cancels further effects if no non-incapped hostiles are within 'fValue' of triggering actor or X,Y. If fValue=0, distance is infinite.
