uses.description: Aborts the remainder of the current task list if no hostile, non-incapped actors are within range of an origin tile. The origin is the supplied tile coordinate (if any) or the triggering actor's position. If floats[0] is 0, the range check is treated as infinite.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The detection range, in tiles. A value of 0 (or omitted) is treated as infinite range.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The origin tile from which to measure the range. If omitted, the triggering actor's tile is used.

tasks.name: cancelIfItemPresent
tasks.officialDescription: Cancels further effects if item 'sValue' is found at X,Y. If X = -1, checks trigger area for the item.

uses.description: Aborts the remainder of the current task list if a specific item is found at the indicated tile, or anywhere within the calling trigger's area when no tile is supplied. Useful for guarding follow-up tasks against double-spawning or duplicating items.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the item to look for.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The tile to inspect for the item. If omitted, the calling trigger's whole area is scanned.

tasks.name: cancelIfItemNotPresent
tasks.officialDescription: Cancels further effects if item 'sValue' is not found at X,Y. If X = -1, checks trigger area for the item.

uses.description: Aborts the remainder of the current task list unless a specific item is present at the indicated tile (or anywhere within the calling trigger's area when no tile is supplied). Useful for gating follow-up tasks on the presence of a required item.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the item to look for.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The tile to inspect for the item. If omitted, the calling trigger's whole area is scanned.

tasks.name: cancelIf
tasks.officialDescription: Cancels further effects if sValue calculates true (> 0). If bValue, cancels when sValue calcs as false (less or = 0)

uses.description: Evaluates a formula and aborts the remainder of the current task list based on the result. By default, cancellation happens when the formula evaluates to a positive value; setting bools[0] inverts the check so cancellation happens when the result is <= 0. The formula has access to the triggering actor and the supplied tile coordinate.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The formula string to evaluate.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: A numeric input passed into the formula calculation. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, inverts the cancellation rule (cancels when the formula evaluates to <= 0 rather than > 0).

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: A tile coordinate passed into the formula calculation.

tasks.name: facingRot
tasks.officialDescription: Sets triggering actor (or actor 'sValue' if specified) to fValue rot.

uses.description: Sets an actor's facing rotation to a specified value (in the game's rotation units). If a non-empty actor ID is provided, that actor is targeted; otherwise, the triggering actor is targeted. Has no effect if neither an explicit nor a triggering actor is available.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor to face-rotate. If empty, the triggering actor is used.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The new facing rotation value to assign.

tasks.name: facingRotParty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets party actors to fValue rot.

uses.description: Sets the facing rotation of every party actor to the same value. Useful for lining up the party for a cutscene or scripted shot.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The new facing rotation value to assign to every party actor.

tasks.name: playSong
tasks.officialDescription: Plays 'sValue' song at 'fValue' pitch. If sValue = 'travelSong' or 'combatSong', uses Zone defaults instead. If X is set, volume = X/100. If Y set, delay = Y. bValue1 prevents the song from being changed to travelSong. To stop music immediately, set volume to 0. If bValue2, song starts at full volume instead of fading in.

uses.description: Starts a song playing in a loop with the given pitch and (optional) volume/delay. Two song name aliases are recognised: 'travelSong'/'travel' plays the current zone's travel song using the zone's defaults, and 'combatSong'/'combat' plays the current zone's combat song using the zone's defaults; any other value plays that song name directly. If the song name is omitted, an empty song name is passed (which stops/clears the current song). Volume is taken from the tile coordinate's X (divided by 100); delay is taken from the tile coordinate's Y. The bools control whether the song can be auto-replaced by travel music and whether it fades in.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The song ID to play. Special values 'travelSong'/'travel' and 'combatSong'/'combat' play the current zone's travel/combat song with its defaults. Empty stops or clears the current song.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The pitch the song is played at. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the song overrides travel-music changes so it isn't replaced by the zone's travel song.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the song starts at full volume instead of fading in (internally sets the delay parameter to -99).

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The X of this coordinate (divided by 100) sets the playback volume; the Y sets the song's start delay.

tasks.name: playNearestLocationSong
tasks.officialDescription: Plays the externalSong of the location in the current zone that is closest to the player.

uses.description: Identifies the Location in the current zone closest to the player and plays that Location's externalSong (the music that should play near it on the overworld). Has no effect if there is no current player or no nearby Location.

tasks.name: setZoneSongs
tasks.officialDescription: Sets zone travel song to 'sValue' &combat song to sValue2 at 'fValue' pitch. If X is set, volume = X/100. Y = combatSongDelay. If bValue1, force assign travelSong even if blank. Same for bValue2 & combat.

uses.description: Replaces the current zone's travel song and/or combat song settings, then begins playing the appropriate one based on whether combat is currently active. Pitch and volume apply to both songs. Travel/combat song assignment is normally skipped if its string is blank, but the corresponding bool can force the blank assignment to occur (effectively clearing that song).

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The new travel song ID for this zone. Skipped if blank, unless bools[0] forces assignment.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new combat song ID for this zone. Skipped if blank, unless bools[1] forces assignment.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The pitch to apply to both songs.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, force-assigns the travel song even when strings[0] is blank (effectively clearing it).

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, force-assigns the combat song even when strings[1] is blank (effectively clearing it).

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The X of this coordinate (divided by 100) sets the playback volume; the Y sets the combat song delay (when > -1).

tasks.name: removeItem
tasks.officialDescription: Deletes 'sValue' item at X,Y. If unspecified, removes 'fValue' num of the item from triggering actor's inventory. if sValue2 is used, remove itemID of globalVar[sValue2]. If bValue2, XY specifies a travelpoint.

uses.description: Removes one or more copies of a named item from a specific tile (or, with bools[1], from the travel point at that tile). All copies of the item on that tile are stripped regardless of the count value.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the item to remove. May be overridden indirectly by strings[1] (see below).

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The tile (or travelpoint, with bools[1] = true) from which to remove the item.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: If non-empty, the item ID is overridden by reading the value of this global variable instead of using strings[0] directly.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the supplied tile coordinate is treated as a travel-point key; the item is removed from that travelpoint rather than the literal tile.

uses.description: Removes one or more copies of a named item from the triggering actor's inventory. If the actor doesn't have it but is in the party, the other party members are searched in turn. If still not found, the stash inventory and finally the held-items pile are searched. The count of items removed is taken from floats[0].

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the item to remove.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: If non-empty, the item ID is overridden by reading the value of this global variable instead of using strings[0] directly.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The number of copies of the item to remove. Defaults to 1 if omitted or 0.

tasks.name: removeActorItem
tasks.officialDescription: Removes item 'sValue2' from actor 'sValue'.

uses.description: Removes a specific item from a named actor's inventory by unequipping it (no sound). Has no effect if the actor does not exist or does not currently hold that item.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose inventory should be modified.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The ID of the item to remove from that actor's inventory.

tasks.name: removeItemOfValue
tasks.officialDescription: Deletes items at X,Y of value fValue or greater.

uses.description: Removes all items at the indicated tile whose individual value is at least floats[0]. If no value threshold is supplied, the threshold defaults to -1 (which matches any value, effectively removing all items at the tile).

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The minimum value threshold. Items at the tile with value >= this are removed. Defaults to -1 if omitted (matches everything).

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The tile whose items should be inspected. If omitted, defaults to the off-map fallback tile (-1,-1).

tasks.name: removeEmptyItem
tasks.officialDescription: Removes all containers from the current zone that have the ID of 'sValue' and have no items inside of them.

uses.description: Scans every tile in the current zone for container items matching the supplied ID that currently hold zero items, and removes them. If no ID is supplied, every empty container in the zone (of any ID) is removed.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The ID of the empty container to remove. If omitted or empty, every empty container is removed regardless of ID.

tasks.name: removeItemsNearPlayerActor
tasks.officialDescription: Deletes items of type sValue1 within fValue tiles of player Actor.

uses.description: Scans every tile within floats[0] tiles of the player actor and removes every copy of the named item. Optionally, the item ID can be looked up indirectly via a global variable name.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the item to remove from nearby tiles.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: If non-empty, the item ID is overridden by reading the value of this global variable instead of using strings[0] directly.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The detection radius in tiles. Defaults to 0 if omitted (only the player's own tile is affected).

tasks.name: removeAllItems
tasks.officialDescription: Deletes 'sValue' item from zone. Uses gVar if 'sValue2' specified. Can also remove all at XY if no sValue given.

uses.description: Removes every copy of a particular item from every tile in the current zone. The item ID can be supplied directly or, if strings[1] is non-empty, looked up from a global variable. If a tile coordinate is provided (and is not (-1,-1)), this branch is bypassed in favour of the per-tile branch instead.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the item to remove from the zone.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: If non-empty, the item ID is overridden by reading the value of this global variable instead of using strings[0] directly.

uses.description: Removes every item at a specific tile coordinate, regardless of item ID. This branch runs when a non-(-1,-1) tile coordinate is supplied.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The tile from which all items should be removed.

tasks.name: removeAllItemsOfValue
tasks.officialDescription: Deletes items from zone of value fValue or greater.

uses.description: Scans every tile in the current zone and removes any items whose individual value is at least floats[0]. Useful for sweeping up high-value loot from a zone (or, with floats[0] = 0, all items).

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The minimum value threshold; items with value >= this are removed. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: removeHeldItems
tasks.officialDescription: Deletes all held items.

uses.description: Clears the player's 'held items' buffer (the temporary stack used while picking up or moving items) by removing every item from it. Useful for cleaning up if the player is holding leftover items between scripted sequences.

tasks.name: addItem
tasks.officialDescription: Adds 'fValue' num of item 'sValue' ID to X,Y (or sValue2 actor or triggering actor inventory if unspecified). If bValue1, force item to fit.

uses.description: Drops the specified item onto a specific tile in the world. The supplied count determines how many copies; if bools[0] is true, the item is forced to fit (overriding any tile capacity constraint). Has no effect if the item ID is unknown or the resulting quantity is zero.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the item type to create.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The tile on which to drop the item.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The quantity to create. Defaults to 1 if omitted or 0.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, forces the item to fit on the tile (e.g. ignoring stacking or tile limits).

uses.description: Equips the specified item onto a specifically named actor (if the actor type can carry items/weapons). This branch runs when no tile is supplied and strings[1] names an existing actor.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the item type to create.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The ID of the actor to receive the item.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The quantity to create. Defaults to 1 if omitted or 0.

uses.description: Equips the specified item onto the triggering actor, with several special cases. If the item is flagged as cargo and the actor is controllable, the cargo goes into the global cargo inventory instead. The actor must be able to carry items/weapons. The effective quantity added is reduced by however many of that item the actor already carries; the leftover (if any) is placed at the actor's tile if it doesn't fit on their inventory.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the item type to create.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The quantity to create. Defaults to 1 if omitted or 0.

uses.description: With no actor and no tile, adds the item to the global cargo inventory if it has the cargo special property, or to the global stash inventory otherwise.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the item type to create.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The quantity to create. Defaults to 1 if omitted or 0.

tasks.name: addItemToContainer
tasks.officialDescription: Adds 'fValue' quantity of 'sValue' itemType ID to the first container at X,Y that the item can fit into. If 'sValue2' is specified, that is the itemType ID the container must be. If fValue is 0, it acts as 1.

uses.description: Places a stack of an item into the first container at the indicated tile that the item can fit into. Optionally restricts which container is used by requiring it to match a particular item ID. Has no effect if the item ID is unknown, the optional container ID is unknown, or no suitable container exists at the tile.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the item type to add.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The tile to search for a container.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: If non-empty, the container at the tile must be of this item ID for the addition to happen. If empty or omitted, the first fitting container at the tile is used.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The quantity to add. A value of 0 (or omitted) is treated as 1.

tasks.name: spawnItem
tasks.officialDescription: Adds 'fValue' num of item 'sValue' ID to X,Y (or random spot in trigger area if unspecified) and plays 'sValue2' fx if specified. If sValue is not an item type, checks globalVar for an ID. If bValue1, force item to fit. If bValue2, XY refers to a travelpoint (#X, set Y).

uses.description: Creates an item and drops it at the supplied tile coordinate (or, with bools[1], at the travel point identified by the tile coordinates). If strings[0] is not a known item type, it is treated as a global variable name and its value is used as the item ID instead. bools[0] forces the item to fit even if the tile is full. After placement, an optional FX (strings[1]) can be played at the spawn position.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the item type to spawn, or the name of a global variable holding the actual ID.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The tile (or travel-point key, with bools[1] = true) where the item should appear.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: If non-empty, the ID of a visual FX to play at the spawn position after the item appears.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The quantity of the item to spawn. A value of 0 (or omitted) is treated as 1.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, forces the item to fit on the target tile even if it would otherwise be too full.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the supplied tile coordinate is treated as a travel-point key (not literal coordinates).

uses.description: Creates an item and drops it on a random tile within the calling trigger's area, retrying up to 50 times until a tile is found where the item can fit. This branch runs when no tile coordinate is supplied. Optionally plays an FX at the chosen position after placement.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the item type to spawn, or the name of a global variable holding the actual ID.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: If non-empty, the ID of a visual FX to play at the chosen spawn position.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The quantity of the item to spawn. A value of 0 (or omitted) is treated as 1.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, forces the item to fit on the chosen tile even if it would otherwise be too full.

tasks.name: moveItems
tasks.officialDescription: Moves items from topLeft of trigger area to X,Y.

uses.description: Sweeps the calling trigger's whole area for items and transports them to a target tile. Items can be filtered by item ID (strings[0]) and/or by a specialProperty enum value (strings[1]); supplying neither moves every item in the area. Optionally restricted to items that can normally be picked up. Has no effect if no destination tile is supplied.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The destination tile where collected items are deposited (with forced fit).

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: Filters to items with this ID. If empty or omitted, items of any ID are eligible.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: Filters to items with this specialProperty (either by enum name or by integer index). If empty or omitted, all properties pass.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, only items that can normally be picked up are moved. If false or omitted, even non-pickup items are moved.

tasks.name: moveItemsToActor
tasks.officialDescription: Moves items from X,Y to actor 'sValue'.

uses.description: Picks every item up off the supplied tile and equips it onto a named actor (dropping any that don't fit back onto the ground). The named actor must currently exist and must be of an actor type capable of carrying items/weapons. After the transfer, any remaining items on the tile are deleted.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor that should receive the items.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The tile from which to pick up items. If omitted, defaults to the off-map fallback tile (-1,-1).

tasks.name: setSpecialInfoTC
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the specialInfoTC of any valid items that have the ID of 'sValue' (or all items if none set) at tile X,Y to a new specialInfoTC set in 'sValue2' as 'X,Y'. If b1, the specialInfoTC is offset by the X,Y rather than set to it. If b2, 'sValue' is a specialProperty to check for instead of ID.

uses.description: Adjusts the specialInfoTC field of items at the supplied tile that have the 'canAssignSpecialTC' property. The new tile coordinate is read from strings[1] in 'X,Y' format, or from tileCoords[1], or from floats[0]/floats[1], whichever is supplied. Items can be filtered by ID (strings[0]) or by a specialProperty when bools[1] is true. Setting bools[0] makes the new coordinate an additive offset to the existing specialInfoTC instead of replacing it. Has no effect if no tile is supplied or no new specialInfoTC source is provided.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The tile whose items should be inspected and updated.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: Filter for matching items. By default, items whose ID matches this string are updated; if bools[1] is true, this is interpreted as a specialProperty (by name or integer) instead. If empty, every eligible item on the tile is updated.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: A new specialInfoTC encoded as 'X,Y'. If supplied, takes priority over tileCoords[1] or floats[0]/floats[1] for sourcing the new value.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: Together with floats[1], an alternative way to supply the new specialInfoTC's X coordinate (Y is taken from floats[1]). Used only when strings[1] and tileCoords[1] are absent.

optional.name: floats[1]
optional.description: Together with floats[0], the Y coordinate of the new specialInfoTC. Used only when strings[1] and tileCoords[1] are absent.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the supplied X,Y is treated as an offset added to each item's existing specialInfoTC instead of replacing it.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, strings[0] is interpreted as a specialProperty enum (by name or integer) rather than as an item ID, and items matching that property are targeted.

optional.name: tileCoords[1]
optional.description: An alternative way to supply the new specialInfoTC. Used only when strings[1] does not contain a comma.

tasks.name: replaceEquipment
tasks.officialDescription: Equips item 'sValue2' to actor 'sValue'. If fValue is specified, replaces that slot. Otherwise replaces the 1st relevant slot (0 for wpns, etc.)

uses.description: Replaces an actor's current equipment in a specific slot with a freshly created item. Behaviour differs by item category: weapons are placed into the indicated slot index, while armor defaults to slot 2 if floats[0] is 0. Has no effect if the named actor doesn't exist or their actor type can't carry items/weapons.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose equipment should be replaced.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The ID of the item type to equip.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The slot index to replace. For armor, a value of 0 is auto-corrected to slot 2.

tasks.name: dropItem
tasks.officialDescription: Drops item 'sValue2' from actor 'sValue'.

uses.description: Removes a stack of an item from a named actor's inventory and drops it at their feet. If the actor does not exist, nothing happens. The dropped quantity comes from floats[0]; a value of 0 is treated as 1.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose inventory should be searched.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The ID of the item to remove from that actor.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The quantity to drop. A value of 0 is treated as 1.

tasks.name: buryAllItemsOfType
tasks.officialDescription: buries all items of type sValue. if bValue1, unburies them. sValue2 = itemType.specialProperty to (un)bury, if specified.

uses.description: Iterates every tile in the current zone and toggles the 'buried' state of items that match a given ID or a given specialProperty. Items are buried (hidden) by default; bools[0] inverts the action so they are unburied instead. Both filters can be supplied: the ID match in strings[0] and the specialProperty filter in strings[1] both contribute (any matching item is affected).

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The item ID to (un)bury. Items with this ID are toggled.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: An additional specialProperty filter (either by enum name or integer index). Items in the zone with this special property are also (un)buried.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, items are unburied instead of buried.

tasks.name: buryGlobalVarItems
tasks.officialDescription: buries or unburies items that convert to globals, depending on if their globalVar is set.

uses.description: Iterates every tile in the current zone and updates the 'buried' state of items that have the setGlobalVarWhenConverted special property: items whose backing global variable has a positive value are unburied (revealed), and the rest are buried (hidden). Used to keep persistent collectible state in sync with the world view.

tasks.name: buryLocations
tasks.officialDescription: buries undiscovered locations.

uses.description: For every undiscovered Location in the current zone, marks the location-flagged items at the Location's tile and the four cardinally adjacent tiles as buried. This hides the visual indicators that a discoverable location is here until the player actually finds it.

tasks.name: reactOnMatchingItems
tasks.officialDescription: Finds all 'sValue' items in the zone that are adjacent & facing eachother, and applies Element.matched. If sValue2 is specified, matches sValue & sValue2 items.

uses.description: Scans the whole zone looking for items that are placed adjacent to each other, facing toward each other. When such a matching pair is found, both items receive an elemental reaction: 'matchedSelf' if the two items share the same ID, or 'matched' if they have different IDs (controlled by whether strings[1] is supplied and differs from strings[0]).

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the items to scan for (the 'left side' of the match).

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The ID of the items to match against the first set. If empty, items with strings[0]'s own ID are matched against each other and react with 'matchedSelf'.

tasks.name: specialKeyStatusUpdate
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE.

uses.description: Iterates every tile in the current zone looking for 'pedestal_keyhole' items, and emits the 'nearKeyPedestal' elemental reaction on each party actor when one is present. Additionally, if the zone ID contains 'castle', emits the 'inCastleZone' reaction on each party actor. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: removeActor
tasks.officialDescription: Removes actor 'sValue', or actor at X,Y if specified. If bValue1, adds actor_flee fx. If bValue2, puts their items in your stash.

uses.description: Removes a specific actor from the zone. If a tile coordinate is supplied, the actor at that tile is removed; otherwise the actor identified by strings[0] is removed. Optionally plays a flee FX (defaulting to 'actor_flee', or a custom one from strings[1]) on the actor first, and optionally dumps the removed actor's inventory into the player's stash before removal.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The ID of the actor to remove. Used when no tile coordinate is supplied. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The ID of the FX to play when bools[0] is true. If omitted, defaults to 'actor_flee'.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, plays a flee FX on the actor before they are removed.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the actor's inventory is dumped into the player's stash inventory before they are removed.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: If supplied with an X != -1, the actor at this tile is removed instead of the actor named in strings[0].

tasks.name: removeActorType
tasks.officialDescription: Remove all actors of type 'sValue'. If bValue1, adds actor_flee fx.

uses.description: Removes every actor in the zone whose actorTypeID matches the supplied ID. Optionally plays a flee FX on each one before they are removed. Useful for clearing out a category of enemies in bulk.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The actorTypeID to match. Every actor with this type in the zone is removed.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, plays the 'actor_flee' FX on each matching actor before they are removed.

tasks.name: removeActors_despawnable
tasks.officialDescription: Remove all actors with canDespawn=true. If bValue1, adds actor_flee fx. If bValue2, only affects incapped actors.

uses.description: Removes every despawnable actor (canDespawn = true) from the current zone, plus from the special (parallel) zone if one exists. With bools[1] set, only despawnable actors that are also incapacitated or drowning are removed; in combat, removing such an actor still awards their XP. Optionally plays a flee FX on each removed actor first. Any held actor (e.g., being carried) that can despawn is also cleared.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, plays the 'actor_flee' FX on each affected actor before they are removed.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, restricts removal to actors that are incapacitated or drowning (and grants XP for hostiles in combat).

tasks.name: setActorFloat
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's float property 'sValue2' to 'fValue'.

uses.description: Sets a named float property on a specific actor via reflective data assignment. Useful for tweaking individual stats or scalar fields on a single actor. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose property should be modified.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The name of the float-valued property on the actor to assign.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The new value to assign to the property. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: setActorBool
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's bool property 'sValue2' to bValue1.

uses.description: Sets a named boolean property on a specific actor via reflective data assignment. The value is true if either floats[0] > 0 or bools[0] is true; otherwise false. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose property should be modified.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The name of the bool-valued property on the actor to assign.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: If > 0, the assigned value becomes true. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the assigned value becomes true (regardless of floats[0]).

tasks.name: setActorAI
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's lua ai script to 'sValue2'. If bValue1, run the ai script once.

uses.description: Switches a specific actor's AI Lua script to a different one (so their decision-making logic changes immediately). Optionally also runs the new script once right away. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose AI script should be swapped.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The name of the AI Lua script to switch to. Defaults to empty if omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, runs the AI script once immediately after switching.

tasks.name: setActorName
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's name to 'sValue2'.

uses.description: Changes the display name of a specific actor. Useful for revealing hidden NPC names mid-quest, or changing a generic 'Stranger' label into something specific. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose name should be changed.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new display name. Defaults to empty if omitted.

tasks.name: setActorIdleAnimOverride
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's idleAnimOverride to  'sValue2'.

uses.description: Overrides a specific actor's idle animation with a custom one, so they appear to be doing something different (sitting, waving, etc.) while idle. Setting an empty value clears the override. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose idle animation should be overridden.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The name of the override animation. Empty/omitted clears the override.

tasks.name: setActorDialogNodeID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's dialogNodeID to  'sValue2'.

uses.description: Sets the dialog node a specific actor uses when talked to. The special value 'defaultDialogNode' is replaced with the actor type's default dialog node. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose dialog node should be changed.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new dialog node ID. The special value 'defaultDialogNode' uses the actor type's default. Empty/omitted clears the dialog.

tasks.name: setActorAIActions
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's queuedAiActions to 'sValue2' (ex: action1, action2, ...).

uses.description: Replaces a specific actor's queued AI actions list with a new comma-separated sequence. Useful for scripting a deterministic series of moves or attacks for an NPC. Empty list entries are preserved. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose action queue should be set.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new queued AI actions, as a comma-separated list. Defaults to empty if omitted (clears the queue).

tasks.name: setActorMovementType
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's movementType to 'sValue2'. If sValue2 is set to 'defaultMovementType', actorType.movementType is used.

uses.description: Switches a specific actor's movement type (the rules controlling how they move, e.g. on-foot, swimming, flying). The special value 'defaultMovementType' is replaced with the actor type's default. The actor's specialMovementType is also reset and reassigned to match. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose movement type should be changed.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new movement type ID. The special value 'defaultMovementType' uses the actor type's default. Defaults to empty if omitted.

tasks.name: setActorPalettes
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's palettes to 'sValue2'.

uses.description: Changes a specific actor's armor palette (recolor) to a different defined palette. If the supplied palette doesn't exist, the actor's armor palette is cleared to empty. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose palette should be changed.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new palette ID. If it doesn't match a defined palette, the actor's palette is cleared instead. Defaults to empty if omitted.

tasks.name: setActorArmorPalette
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's armorPalette to 'sValue2'. If sValue2 is set to 'defaultPalette', actorType.armorPalette is used. If sValue2 is 'currentArmorPalette', the equipped armor palette is used.

uses.description: Changes a specific actor's armor recolor palette. Two special values are recognised: 'defaultPalette' substitutes the actor type's default armorPalette, and 'currentArmorPalette' uses the palette of whatever armor the actor currently has equipped (falling back to their unarmored palette if no armor is equipped). Unknown palette IDs are cleared to empty. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose armor palette should be changed.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new armor palette ID, or one of the special values 'defaultPalette' / 'currentArmorPalette'. Unknown palettes are treated as empty.

tasks.name: setActorSkinPalette
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's skinPalette to 'sValue2'. If sValue2 is 'defaultPalette', actorType.skinPalette is used. If sValue2 is 'randomPalette', a random palette from actorType.skinPalettes is used.

uses.description: Changes a specific actor's skin recolor palette. The special value 'defaultPalette' substitutes the actor type's default skinPalette, and 'randomPalette' picks a random palette from the actor type's defined skinPalettes list (or the default if none defined). Unknown palette IDs are cleared to empty. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose skin palette should be changed.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new skin palette ID, or one of the special values 'defaultPalette' / 'randomPalette'. Unknown palettes are treated as empty.

tasks.name: setActorClothPalette
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's clothPalette to 'sValue2'. If sValue2 is 'defaultPalette', actorType.clothPalette is used. If sValue2 is 'randomPalette', a random selectable player palette is chosen. If sValue2 is 'playerPalette', the player's current clothPalette is used.

uses.description: Changes a specific actor's cloth (clothing) recolor palette. Three special values are recognised: 'defaultPalette' substitutes the actor type's default clothPalette, 'playerPalette' copies the current player's cloth palette, and 'randomPalette' picks a random palette from the player-usable cloth palette pool. Unknown palette IDs are cleared to empty. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose cloth palette should be changed.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new cloth palette ID, or one of the special values 'defaultPalette' / 'playerPalette' / 'randomPalette'. Unknown palettes are treated as empty.

tasks.name: setActorUnarmoredPalette
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's unarmoredPalette to 'sValue2'. Updates armorPalette if no armor is equipped.

uses.description: Changes a specific actor's 'unarmored' fallback palette, used when they aren't wearing armor. The special value 'defaultPalette' substitutes the actor type's default armorPalette. If the actor isn't currently wearing armor, the change is also propagated to their armor palette so it takes effect immediately. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose unarmored palette should be changed.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new unarmored palette ID, or the special value 'defaultPalette'. Unknown palettes are treated as empty.

tasks.name: setActorCombatLabel
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's combatLabel to 'sValue2'.

uses.description: Changes the label shown above a specific actor during combat (e.g. 'Pirate Captain' vs 'Pirate'). Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose combat label should be changed.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new combat label text. Defaults to empty if omitted.

tasks.name: setActorCombatLabel_color
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's combatLabel_color to 'sValue2'. If sValue2 is 'enemyFleet', 'playerFleet', 'assistFleet' or 'assistFleet_hostile', that set global Color is used.

uses.description: Changes the color of a specific actor's combat label. The color can be supplied as a color name (such as 'White'), as a comma-separated RGB triple/quad spread across strings[1+] or floats[0+], or as one of four reserved special names ('enemyFleet', 'playerFleet', 'assistFleet', 'assistFleet_hostile') which use predefined global colors. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose combat label color should be changed.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The color name, special reserved name, or first component of an RGB(A) triple/quad. Defaults to 'White' if omitted.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: Optional second comma component of an RGB(A) color (used when strings.Count >= 4).

optional.name: strings[3]
optional.description: Optional third comma component of an RGB(A) color (used when strings.Count >= 4).

optional.name: strings[4]
optional.description: Optional fourth comma component of an RGBA color (alpha).

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: An alternative way to express the color as floats: floats[0..3] form an RGB(A) tuple when at least 3 floats are supplied and the strings form isn't used.

optional.name: floats[1]
optional.description: Second float color component.

optional.name: floats[2]
optional.description: Third float color component.

optional.name: floats[3]
optional.description: Optional fourth float color component (alpha).

tasks.name: setActorCombatTeam
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's combatTeam to 'sValue2'. If 'sValue2' is 'defaultCombatTeam', actorType.defaultCombatTeam is used.

uses.description: Changes which combat team a specific actor belongs to (used to identify allies, enemies, neutrals, etc. in tactical combat). The special value 'defaultCombatTeam' substitutes the actor type's default combat team. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose combat team should be changed.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new combat team identifier, or the special value 'defaultCombatTeam'. Defaults to empty if omitted.

tasks.name: setActorCombatMusicOverride
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's combatMusicOverride to 'sValue2'.

uses.description: Sets a specific actor's combat music override, so combat involving this actor uses a particular song instead of the zone default. Clearing the override (empty value) returns the actor to using the zone's combat music. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose combat music override should be changed.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The song ID to use as the combat music override. Empty/omitted clears any override.

tasks.name: setActorFaction
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's faction to 'sValue2'. If 'sValue2' is 'defaultFaction', actorType.defaultFaction is used. Can't change to/from 'player' faction.

uses.description: Changes a specific actor's faction. The special value 'defaultFaction' substitutes the actor type's default faction. The 'player' faction is protected: actors currently in the player faction cannot be changed away from it, and other actors cannot be changed into it. Controllable actors are also excluded. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist or is otherwise protected.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose faction should be changed.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new faction ID, or the special value 'defaultFaction'. Changing to 'player' is silently ignored.

tasks.name: setActorID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's ID to 'sValue2'. If bValue1 is true, forces a unique ID based on sValue2. Cant change an ID to/from 'player'.

uses.description: Renames a specific actor by changing its ID. If bools[0] is true (or another actor already has that ID), the supplied new ID is automatically uniquified by appending a suffix. The 'player' ID is reserved: you can't rename the player, or rename any actor to 'player'. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist or the new ID is empty/'player'.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The current ID of the actor to rename.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new ID to assign. If empty or 'player', the rename is skipped.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the new ID is uniquified automatically even when no conflict exists.

tasks.name: setActorSprite
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's sprite to 'sValue2'. If 'sValue2' is 'defaultSprite', actorType.defaultSprite is used.

uses.description: Replaces a specific actor's main sprite identifier. The special value 'defaultSprite' substitutes the actor type's defaultSprite. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose sprite should be changed.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new sprite ID, or the special value 'defaultSprite'. Defaults to empty if omitted.

tasks.name: setActorBodyPartSprite
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the sprite for actor 'sValue's bodyPart set in 'sValue2'. sValue2 needs to be in the format 'bodyPartID, spriteSheet'. If the 'spriteSheet' part of sValue2 is 'defaultSprite', actorType.defaultBodyPartSprites for the bodyPart is used. If it is 'randomSprite', a random sprite for the bodyPart from actorType.bodyPartSprites is used.

uses.description: Sets the sprite for one of a specific actor's body parts (e.g. head, torso, armR). The body part name and sprite sheet name may be supplied either as two separate strings (strings[1], strings[2]) or combined into one comma-separated string in strings[1] (e.g. 'head, head_blonde'). Two special spriteSheet values are recognised: 'defaultSprite' uses the actor type's default for that body part, and 'randomSprite' picks a random sprite from the actor type's list for that part. Has no effect if the actor or body part doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose body part sprite should be changed.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: Either the body part ID alone (when strings[2] is also supplied) or a combined 'bodyPart, spriteSheet' string. If the combined form is used and contains no comma, the task has no effect.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The sprite sheet ID. Used when strings.Count > 2; recognises 'defaultSprite' and 'randomSprite' special values.

tasks.name: setFaceSprite
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the 'face' bodyPart sprite for the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) to 'sValue'.

uses.description: Sets the 'face' body part sprite for the actor associated with this task (the dialog talker if in dialog, otherwise the triggering actor). Useful for swapping facial expressions during conversations. Has no effect if no relevant actor is available.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The new face sprite ID.

tasks.name: setCurrentActor_partSpritesThatTakePriorityOverClass
tasks.officialDescription: Sets 'partSpritesThatTakePriorityOverClass' of the current player leader to sValue1 (ex: 'torso, armR, shoulderR')

uses.description: Updates the current player leader's list of body parts whose sprites should override class-based sprites. Useful for forcing a custom appearance on the active player character. Has no effect if there is no current actor.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The comma-separated list of body part names whose sprites should override class-based sprites (e.g. 'torso, armR, shoulderR').

tasks.name: setActor_partSpritesThatTakePriorityOverClass
tasks.officialDescription: Sets actor 'sValue's partSpritesThatTakePriorityOverClass to 'sValue2' (ex: 'torso, armR, shoulderR')

uses.description: Updates a specific actor's list of body parts whose sprites should override class-based sprites. Any additional strings after strings[1] are concatenated onto the list with commas, so the list can be supplied across multiple strings if convenient. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose priority list should be set.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The first entry of the comma-separated priority list, or the whole list.

optional.name: strings[2+]
optional.description: Additional body part names that are concatenated onto strings[1] with commas before assignment.

tasks.name: giveActorsBaseXP
tasks.officialDescription: Awards between 54 and 84 XP in all classes to every actor in the zone, if they have less than 54 XP.

uses.description: Iterates every actor in the current zone and awards them their 'base XP', which grants them a small random amount of XP in every class (between 54 and 84) provided they currently have less than 54 XP in that class. Useful for ensuring NPCs aren't completely unleveled when entering a scripted scene.

tasks.name: moveActor
tasks.officialDescription: Moves actor 'sValue' to X,Y and sets facingRot to 'fValue'.

uses.description: Teleports a specific actor to a tile coordinate and sets their facing rotation. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor to move.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The new facing rotation for the actor.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The destination tile coordinate.

tasks.name: moveActor_point
tasks.officialDescription: Moves actor 'sValue' to #X, set Y (A=0, B=1...) travel point.

uses.description: Teleports a specific actor to a travel-point item identified by the tile coordinate (interpreted as a travel-point key) and sets their facing rotation. The actor is moved to the nearest tile that contains a matching travel-point item. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor to move.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The new facing rotation for the actor.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The travel-point key (the X identifies the number, the Y identifies the letter set).

tasks.name: movePartyTo
tasks.officialDescription: Moves entire party and camera by X,Y - triggerActor.position. If fValue != 0, sets actors' facingRot to fValue.

uses.description: Teleports the entire player party (and the camera) to a tile coordinate. While travelling, applies a short interaction lockout so the move isn't immediately interrupted. If floats[0] is non-zero, every party actor's facing rotation is also set to that value.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The new facing rotation for every party actor. If 0, facing is left unchanged.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The destination tile coordinate for the party (and camera).

tasks.name: movePartyTo_point
tasks.officialDescription: Moves entire party and camera to travel point #X, set Y (A=0, B=1...) If bValue1, doesn't use X - just puts party on the set. If b2, brings out boats from player fleet.

uses.description: Teleports the entire player party (and the camera) to the nearest tile holding a travel-point item identified by the tile coordinate. If bools[1] is true and the zone is a boat zone, the player's fleet boats are also spawned and added to the party. If bools[0] is true, the X is ignored and the party is placed on the indicated travel set rather than at a specific travel-point index.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The travel-point key (the X identifies the number, the Y identifies the letter set).

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the X is ignored and the party is placed onto the entire travel set rather than at a specific travel point.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true (and the zone is a boat zone), boats from the player's fleet are spawned and added to the party.

tasks.name: movePartyBy
tasks.officialDescription: Moves entire party and camera by X,Y. If fValue != 0, sets actors' facingRot to fValue.

uses.description: Shifts every party actor and the camera by a delta vector (in world pixels, not tiles) without snapping them to a particular tile. Useful for nudging the party during cutscenes. Out-of-bounds positions are clamped back inside the zone. If floats[0] is non-zero, every party actor's facing rotation is also set.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The X,Y offset vector to add to every party actor's position and to the camera.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The new facing rotation for every party actor. If 0, facing is left unchanged.

tasks.name: moveAllActorsBy
tasks.officialDescription: Moves all actors and camera by X,Y. If fValue != 0, sets actors' facingRot to fValue.

uses.description: Shifts every actor in the current zone (party and otherwise) and the camera by a delta vector. Useful for repositioning a whole scene at once. Out-of-bounds positions are clamped back inside the zone. If floats[0] is non-zero, every actor's facing rotation is also set.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The X,Y offset vector to add to every actor's position and to the camera.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The new facing rotation for every actor. If 0, facing is left unchanged.

tasks.name: addToParty
tasks.officialDescription: Adds actor ID 'sValue' to the party.

uses.description: Adds a specific existing actor to the player's party (crew). Has no effect if no actor with the supplied ID currently exists.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor to add to the player's party.

tasks.name: addTalkerToParty
tasks.officialDescription: Adds the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) to the player's crew. Also adds to the landing party and makes their ID unique if their ID starts with 'hire_'. If b1, adds to the landing party regardless of actor ID.

uses.description: Adds the actor associated with this task (the dialog talker if in dialog, otherwise the triggering actor) to the player's crew, converting them to an ally and adding them to the fleet. If the actor's ID starts with 'hire_', the prefix is rewritten to 'hired_' and uniquified, and the actor is also added to the landing party. The bools[0] flag forces landing-party addition regardless of the ID prefix. As a side effect, crew morale is reduced by 1 if it was above 70.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the actor is added to the landing party regardless of whether their ID starts with 'hire_'.

tasks.name: addTalkerToParty_temp
tasks.officialDescription: Adds the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) to the player's landing party as a temporary party actor. Does not add the actor to the player's crew. Can be removed using the 'removeTempPartyActors' task.

uses.description: Adds the actor associated with this task (the dialog talker if in dialog, otherwise the triggering actor) to the landing party as a temporary member, without adding them to the player's crew. The temporary actor is configured so they cannot have their appearance edited, cannot be looted, and cannot be fully controlled. Their cloth palette is matched to the player's. If the actor previously had a difficulty-mode support ability, equivalent support abilities for their actor type and class are equipped, and class actions below max rank are bumped by 1.

tasks.name: removeFromParty
tasks.officialDescription: Removes actor ID 'sValue' from the party. If bValue1, removes from fleet as well (if applicable).

uses.description: Removes a specific actor from the player's party. The actor is not added to the barracks (so they are not retained as a recruit). With bools[0] = true, the actor is also removed from the player's fleet, freeing up that fleet slot. Has no effect if the actor isn't currently in the party.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor to remove from the party.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the actor is also removed from the player's fleet (if applicable).

uses.description: Clears the current pathfinding command on the player's party, stopping them from continuing along any previously set path. Useful for cancelling auto-move commands mid-cutscene.

tasks.name: addToLandingParty
tasks.officialDescription: Adds the player crew actor with the ID of 'sValue' to the player's landing party if they can fit, and to the current Zone. If b1, don't add them to the current Zone. If b2, adds them to the landing party even if they can't fit.

uses.description: Adds a specific crew member to the player's landing party (the subset of crew currently active in the zone). The actor must exist, must be in the player fleet's crew, and there must be a free landing-party slot (unless bools[1] is true). With bools[0] set, the actor is added to the landing party but not added to the current zone visually.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the crew actor to add to the landing party.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the actor is added to the landing party but NOT to the current zone.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the actor is added even if the landing party is already at maximum size.

tasks.name: removeFromLandingParty
tasks.officialDescription: Removes the player crew actor with the ID of 'sValue' from the player's landing party, if they are currently in it, and the current Zone. If b1, don't remove them from the current Zone.

uses.description: Removes a specific crew member from the player's active landing party. The main player and the last remaining party member are protected; this task has no effect on them. With bools[0] = true, the actor remains in the current zone visually but is removed from the landing party.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the crew actor to remove from the landing party.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the actor is removed from the landing party but kept in the current zone.

tasks.name: handleTiredStates
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Makes party tired, and makes barracks actors rested.

uses.description: Fully heals every party actor (without restoring max HP) and applies a permanent 'tired' status effect. Barracks (off-duty) actors are fully healed including restored max HP, reflecting that they've been resting. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: hideParty
tasks.officialDescription: Turns party invisible for 'fValue' time.

uses.description: Makes every party actor invisible for a number of seconds. Useful for cutscene effects or sneaking sequences. The invisibility timer is set per-actor.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: How long (in seconds) the party should remain invisible.

tasks.name: path
tasks.officialDescription: Sets path of actor of ID 'sValue' (or triggerActor if unspecifed) to X,Y. If bValue is true, uses waypoints. fValue permanently sets movementSpeed if specified.

uses.description: Tells an actor to walk along a path to a tile coordinate. Clears any existing path first, then either uses the AI pathfinder or (with bools[0] = true) finds a path via the zone's predefined waypoints (reversed). If floats[0] is greater than 0, the actor's movementSpeed is permanently set to that value. If no actor ID is supplied or the named one doesn't exist, the triggering actor is used.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The destination tile coordinate.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The ID of the actor to path. If empty or not found, the triggering actor is pathed.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: If > 0, permanently sets the actor's movementSpeed to this value.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the path is computed via the zone's waypoints rather than the standard AI pathfinder, and then reversed.

tasks.name: pathParty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets path of all party actors to X,Y.

uses.description: Tells every party actor to walk along a freshly computed path to the same destination tile coordinate. Each actor's existing path is cleared first.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The destination tile coordinate for the whole party.

tasks.name: destination
tasks.officialDescription: Sets destination of actor of ID 'sValue' (or triggerActor if unspecifed) to X,Y. fValue permanently sets movementSpeed if specified.

uses.description: Sets an actor's destination tile (clearing any existing path) so they will pursue that tile via their normal movement logic. If floats[0] is greater than 0, the actor's movementSpeed is permanently set to that value. If no actor ID is supplied or the named one doesn't exist, the triggering actor is used.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: If > 0, permanently sets the actor's movementSpeed to this value.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The destination tile coordinate.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The ID of the actor whose destination should be set. If empty or not found, the triggering actor is used.

tasks.name: movement
tasks.officialDescription: Sets movement of actor of ID 'sValue' (or triggerActor if unspecifed) to go to X,Y. (If X or Y are unspecified, only moves along the specified axis.) If bValue1, actor uses pain animation. fValue permanently sets movementSpeed if specified.

uses.description: Triggers a direct, intangible movement on an actor toward a tile coordinate (skipping pathfinding). Any X or Y of -1 is replaced with the actor's current value, so axis-specific moves are possible. The actor's existing inputs and path are cleared first; if the actor is the player, the input manager is reset too. With bools[0] = true, the actor plays a 'pain' animation. If floats[0] > 0, movementSpeed is permanently set to that value. If no actor ID is supplied or the named one doesn't exist, the triggering actor is used.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: If > 0, permanently sets the actor's movementSpeed.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The target tile coordinate. An X or Y of -1 means 'keep the actor's current value on that axis'.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The ID of the actor to move. If empty, the triggering actor is used.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the actor plays a 'pain' animation as they move.

tasks.name: addZoneFX
tasks.officialDescription: Adds a recurring FX 'sValue' to the zone at XY that recurs every 'fValue' indefinitely. If bValue1, then only add FX if it isn't already in the zone.

uses.description: Schedules a recurring visual effect on the zone at a tile coordinate, recurring every floats[0] seconds. With bools[0] = true, the FX is only added if the zone doesn't already contain an instance of it (preventing duplicates).

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the FX to add as a recurring zone effect.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The recurrence interval, in seconds. Defaults to 0 if omitted (which fires continuously).

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the FX is only added if it isn't already present in the zone.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The X,Y position where the FX recurs. Defaults to (-1,-1) if omitted.

tasks.name: createZoneSpecialFX
tasks.officialDescription: Creates FX based on zone_tintMap, or if bValue1, deepWater. sValue = pattern texture override. XY * 0.1 = veloc. fValue = -alpha offset. If bValue2, saves textures to the desktop.

uses.description: Generates and registers a tiled set of special FX textures that cover the whole zone, used for things like water reflections or atmospheric overlays. The texture is built from the zone's tintMap (or, with bools[0] = true, a deep-water-style pattern). The tile coordinate's X/Y * 0.1 set the FX's per-frame velocity offset. floats[0]'s negated value sets the alpha offset. With bools[1] = true, each generated texture is also written to the developer's desktop for inspection.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: An override pattern texture name used in place of the default tintMap-based generation.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: If non-zero, sets the alpha offset of the generated FX to -floats[0].

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, uses a deep-water-style pattern; the FX is configured with a deep depthMod and a fixed alpha override.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, each generated FX texture is also saved to the developer's desktop.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The X and Y are multiplied by 0.1 and used as the velocity offset for the looping FX. Also enables zone-edge looping when supplied.

tasks.name: removeZoneFX
tasks.officialDescription: Removes recurring FX 'sValue' from the zone. If bValue1, removes all instances of that FX.

uses.description: Removes a recurring zone FX. By default the first matching instance is removed; with bools[0] = true, every instance of that FX in the zone is removed.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The ID of the FX to remove. Defaults to empty if omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, every instance of the named FX is removed; otherwise only one is removed.

tasks.name: addZoneBackgroundTex
tasks.officialDescription: Adds texture 'sValue' to the list of background textures for the current Zone, if it doesn't already exist.

uses.description: Adds a named texture to the zone's background texture list, so it will be drawn as part of the zone's background. If the texture is already in the list, the task does nothing (avoiding duplicates).

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the background texture to add to the current zone.

tasks.name: removeZoneBackgroundTex
tasks.officialDescription: Removes texture 'sValue' from the list of background textures for the current zone, if it exists. If b1, removes all background textures.

uses.description: Removes a named texture from the zone's background texture list. With bools[0] = true, all background textures are cleared instead of removing just the named one.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The ID of the background texture to remove. Ignored when bools[0] is true.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, all background textures for the current zone are cleared.

tasks.name: setZonePalettes
tasks.officialDescription: Sets 'sValue' zone palette to 'sValue2'. If 'sValue' is blank, sets all palettes.

uses.description: Replaces one of the current zone's terrain palettes with a different palette, immediately changing the zone's color treatment. The first string selects which palette to set ('stone', 'dirt', 'grass', etc. — including dark/light variants and the long-form names like 'zoneStonePalette'); the second string is the palette ID to assign. If strings[0] is blank or unrecognised, the task may instead apply the new palette across all terrain palettes via the fall-through branch.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: Which terrain palette to set. Accepted short names include 'stone', 'stoneDark', 'stoneLight', 'dirt', 'dirtDark', 'grass', 'grassDark' (plus capitalised and long-form variants). A blank or unrecognised value sets all palettes at once.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The palette ID to assign to the selected terrain palette.

tasks.name: setZonePeaceful
tasks.officialDescription: Set zone.peaceful true if bValue1. False otherwise.

uses.description: Toggles the zone's 'peaceful' flag, which makes all actors invincible while it is true. Combat mode is force-exited (without a victory) when the task runs. If the flag actually changes, every actor's peaceful-invincible state is refreshed.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the zone becomes peaceful (actors invincible). If false or omitted, the zone returns to non-peaceful.

tasks.name: disableFog
tasks.officialDescription: Disables fog for this zone.

uses.description: Disables fog-of-war for the current zone, both at the zone level and at the camera level, so the entire map is visible. There is no way to re-enable it from the same task; it is a one-way switch for the current zone.

tasks.name: setZoneRollingPinStyle
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the current zone's rollingPinStyle to bValue1.

uses.description: Toggles the current zone's 'rollingPinStyle' rendering flag, which affects how the zone's terrain is drawn. The exact visual difference depends on terrain configuration.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, enables rolling pin style. If false or omitted, disables it.

tasks.name: screenDarkWaveEffect
tasks.officialDescription: Sets zone's darkness wave frequency to fValue.

uses.description: Changes the wave-sin frequency parameter on the screen-darkness shader, controlling how the zone's darkness ripples. Also resets the screen shader's time modifier so the change starts cleanly. Has no effect if the new frequency matches the current value.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The new wave-sin frequency for the darkness shader.

tasks.name: changeZoneDarkColor
tasks.officialDescription: Changes zone darkness color to sValue (given as 'R, G, B'). If bValue1, sets color immediately instead of easing.

uses.description: Marks the zone as dark and sets its darkness color from an 'R,G,B' string. With bools[0] = true, the camera's own darkness color is immediately overwritten too (skipping the usual easing). Useful for instantly switching to a different lighting tint.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The new darkness color, expressed as 'R, G, B' (and optionally alpha).

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the camera's darkness color is set immediately rather than easing toward the new value.

tasks.name: setZoneDarknessToWorldDarkness
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the current zone's darknessColor based on the time of day. If b1, also change camera's darknessColor.

uses.description: Marks the zone as dark and recomputes its darkness color from the current time of day (using the location's port flag as a town-color multiplier when applicable). With bools[0] = true, the camera's darkness color is also overwritten immediately. Useful for syncing a zone's lighting to the world clock.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the camera's darkness color is set immediately rather than easing toward the new value.

tasks.name: screenWaveEffect
tasks.officialDescription: Sets zone wave frequency to fValue.

uses.description: Changes the wave-sin frequency parameter on the screen shader, controlling how the zone's full-screen wave distortion ripples. Also resets the screen shader's time modifier so the change starts cleanly. Has no effect if the new frequency matches the current value.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The new wave-sin frequency for the screen shader.

tasks.name: setZoneBG
tasks.officialDescription: Set zone BG; sValue = terrainType.

uses.description: Replaces the current zone's background terrain type with a different one, immediately changing how empty zone background tiles are drawn.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The terrainType ID to use as the zone's background terrain.

tasks.name: setZoneBGFX
tasks.officialDescription: Sets zone bgFXID to 'sValue'.

uses.description: Sets the zone's background FX ID, attaching a named visual effect that plays on the background layer.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The FX ID to assign to the zone's background.

tasks.name: setZoneFloatProperty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the float-type property of the current zone, specified in 'sValue', to the float set in 'fValue'.

uses.description: Sets a named float (or int) property on the current Zone object via reflective data assignment. The list of forbidden property names is hard-coded (sizeX and sizeY cannot be set through this task). For shader-affecting fields (screenWaveFreq, screenWaveAmp, etc.), the screen shader parameters are also synced afterwards. Has no effect when there is no current zone or no property name is provided.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the float-typed (or int-typed) property on the current zone to modify. sizeX and sizeY are explicitly blocked.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The new value to assign. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: setZoneStringProperty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the string-type property of the current zone, specified in 'sValue', to the string set in 'sValue2'.

uses.description: Sets a named string (or Color) property on the current Zone object via reflective data assignment. If strings[1] is empty and floats supply at least 3 components, the value is constructed as a comma-separated RGB(A) string from floats[0..3]. The 'ID' field is explicitly blocked. After assignment, certain shader/audio fields trigger an immediate refresh (darknessColor refreshes the camera; travelSong/combatSong restart the appropriate song). Has no effect if no zone, no property name, or the field type doesn't match.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the string/Color property on the current zone to modify. 'ID' is explicitly blocked.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new value, as a string. May be a comma-separated 'R,G,B[,A]' for Color fields.

optional.name: strings[2+]
optional.description: Additional strings are concatenated onto strings[1] with commas to form the final value.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: When strings[1] is empty and at least 3 floats are supplied, floats[0..3] form the R,G,B(,A) components of the assigned value.

optional.name: floats[1]
optional.description: Second float color component (see floats[0]).

optional.name: floats[2]
optional.description: Third float color component (see floats[0]).

optional.name: floats[3]
optional.description: Optional alpha float color component (see floats[0]).

tasks.name: setZoneBoolProperty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the bool-type property of the current zone, specified in 'sValue', to the bool set by 'b1'.

uses.description: Sets a named bool property on the current Zone object via reflective data assignment. As a special case, setting the 'peaceful' property additionally fires the setZonePeaceful task to apply the peaceful-refresh side effects. Has no effect when there is no zone, no property name, or the field isn't a bool.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the bool property on the current zone to modify.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: The new bool value to assign. Defaults to false if omitted.

tasks.name: initWaypoints
tasks.officialDescription: refreshes waypoint data of the current zone

uses.description: Forces an immediate refresh of the AI waypoint graph for the current zone, recomputing path data used by AI movement. Useful after the zone's terrain has been modified at runtime.

tasks.name: safe
tasks.officialDescription: When in this trigger, ZoneManager.I.safe is true. Safe is used by the AI.

uses.description: Marks the zone as 'safe' (ZoneManager.I.safe = true), which AI scripts may consult to suspend hostile behaviour. Typically used inside triggers that surround safe locations.

tasks.name: sendAISignal
tasks.officialDescription: Sends signal 'sValue' to all actors within 'fValue' of X,Y (or triggering actor pos, or camera).

uses.description: Broadcasts a Lua AI signal to every actor within a radius of an origin position. The origin is the supplied tile coordinate if any, otherwise the triggering actor's position (falling back to the camera). The signal name and radius are supplied as task inputs.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The signal name to send to nearby actors' AI scripts.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The radius (in tiles) around the origin within which actors receive the signal.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The origin position for the broadcast. If omitted, the triggering actor's position is used (or the camera if no actor).

tasks.name: disableTrigger
tasks.officialDescription: Disables trigger of IDnum fValue or alias sValue.

uses.description: Marks a specific trigger as completed (added to the 'done' list) for the current zone, preventing it from firing again. The trigger can be identified by its numeric ID (floats[0]) or by its alias ID (strings[0]); if both are present, the alias-resolved numID wins.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The numeric ID of the trigger to disable. Used when strings[0] is empty or doesn't resolve to a trigger.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The alias ID of the trigger to disable. Takes priority over floats[0] when it resolves to a real trigger.

tasks.name: enableTrigger
tasks.officialDescription: Re-enables trigger of IDnum fValue or alias sValue.

uses.description: Removes a specific trigger from the 'done' list for the current zone, re-enabling it so it can fire again. The trigger can be identified by its numeric ID (floats[0]) or by its alias ID (strings[0]); if both are present, the alias-resolved numID wins.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The numeric ID of the trigger to re-enable. Used when strings[0] is empty or doesn't resolve to a trigger.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The alias ID of the trigger to re-enable. Takes priority over floats[0] when it resolves to a real trigger.

tasks.name: trigger
tasks.officialDescription: Executes trigger of sValue aliasID (including globalTriggers), or of IDnum fValue. sValue2 is an additional fReq. If bValue1, then execute regardless of the trigger's fReq/Once-only. If bValue2, can't rerun a Once-Only trigger.

uses.description: Fires another trigger from this one. The trigger is located by alias ID (strings[0]) — first searching the current zone, then the global trigger pool — or by numeric ID (floats[0]). If strings[1] is non-empty, it is treated as a formula gate (the task is skipped if the formula evaluates <= 0).
