By default, the trigger's own pre-conditions are evaluated; bools[0] forces execution regardless of them, and bools[1] disables the override that lets Once-Only triggers run again.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The alias ID of the trigger to fire. Searches the current zone first, then global triggers.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: An additional formula requirement; if it evaluates <= 0 the trigger is not fired.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The numeric ID of the trigger to fire (when strings[0] is empty or unresolved). Defaults to 0 if omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the trigger is executed regardless of its own pre-conditions or once-only flag.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, a once-only trigger cannot be rerun (the override that normally allows rerun is disabled).

tasks.name: addTrigger
tasks.officialDescription: Adds global trigger 'sValue' to the current zone. Only usable if delay is not 0 (otherwise it crashes).

uses.description: Copies a globally defined trigger into the current zone and registers it under a fresh numeric ID. The trigger is only added if it isn't already present in the zone. The official description warns that delay must not be 0 to avoid a crash.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The alias ID of the global trigger to add to the current zone.

tasks.name: removeTrigger
tasks.officialDescription: Removes trigger of aliasID 'sValue' from the current zone.

uses.description: Removes a trigger from the current zone, identified by its alias ID. Has no effect if no trigger with that alias exists in the zone.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The alias ID of the trigger to remove from the current zone.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarToTriggerProperty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the property 'sValue2' of a trigger in the current zone whose numID is 'fValue'. If sValue2 is 'aliasID,property', then it will instead set the property from the global trigger whose 'aliasID' matches, even if fValue is set. If fValue is negative, gets the property from the currently executing trigger, if it exists. Result is an empty string if no trigger was found.

uses.description: Reads a named property from a trigger and stores its string representation into a named global variable. The trigger can be identified by numID (floats[0]) in the current zone, by alias ID via 'aliasID,property' in strings[1] (which targets a global trigger), or implicitly as the currently executing trigger when floats[0] is negative. The property name is taken from strings[2] (or from the second comma-separated part of strings[1]). An empty string is written if no trigger is found.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable that should receive the trigger's property value.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: Either the trigger property name, or a combined 'aliasID,property' string. When the comma form is used, the global trigger with that aliasID is targeted (overriding floats[0]).

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The property name to read from the trigger. Required when strings[1] doesn't contain a comma.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The numID of the trigger in the current zone to read from. A negative value selects the currently executing trigger. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toTriggerNumIDSearch
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the numID of the 'fValue'-th (starting at 0) active trigger at X,Y, or the entire current zone if X,Y is not set, that matches a check set in 'sValue2'. If b1 is false, the check is for if the fReq of the trigger matches sValue2. If b1 is true, the check is for if the effectID in sValue2 is used in any trigger effect. If b2, only checks disabled triggers. Result is -1 if no triggers found.

uses.description: Searches the current zone for a trigger matching certain criteria and writes that trigger's numID into a named global variable. The search can be restricted to a particular tile coordinate (else the whole zone). Triggers can be matched on their fReq formula string or, with bools[0] set, on whether one of their effectIDs equals strings[1]. By default only active (still triggerable) triggers are considered; bools[1] flips this to inspect only disabled triggers. floats[0] is interpreted as an index into the matching list (the Nth match). Writes -1 when no trigger matches.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable that receives the located trigger's numID (or -1 if none).

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The fReq formula string to match against, or (with bools[0]) the effectID to look up in trigger effects.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: Zero-based index — the Nth matching trigger is selected. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, matching is done by whether any effectID equals strings[1]. If false, matching is done by fReq formula string equality.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, only triggers that are currently disabled (already 'done') are considered. If false, only active triggers are considered.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: If supplied with an X >= 0, only triggers whose area contains this tile are searched.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toTriggerNumID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the numID of the trigger in the current zone that has an aliasID matching 'sValue2'. If fValue is negative or sValue2 is blank, sets gVar to the currently executing trigger numID, if there is one. Result is -1 if no triggers found.

uses.description: Looks up a trigger in the current zone by its alias ID and writes that trigger's numID into a named global variable. When floats[0] is negative or strings[1] is blank, the currently executing trigger's numID is used instead. Writes -1 if no matching trigger is found.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable that receives the located trigger's numID (or -1 if none).

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The aliasID to match against. If blank, the currently executing trigger is used.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: If negative, selects the currently executing trigger regardless of strings[1]. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toTriggerAliasID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the aliasID of the trigger in the current zone that has 'fValue' as its numID. If fValue is negative, sets gVar to the currently executing trigger aliasID, if there is one. Sets a blank string if the trigger doesn't have an aliasID set or isn't found.

uses.description: Looks up a trigger in the current zone by its numID and writes that trigger's aliasID into a named global variable. When floats[0] is negative, the currently executing trigger's aliasID is used instead. Writes an empty string if the matching trigger has no alias or no trigger matches.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable that receives the located trigger's aliasID (or empty).

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The numID to match. If negative, selects the currently executing trigger. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: specialSpawn
tasks.officialDescription: Applies specialSpawn 'sValue' to the zone.

uses.description: Runs a named 'specialSpawn' definition on the current zone, applying whatever spawn rules that definition contains. Useful for invoking predefined spawn presets at scripted moments.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the specialSpawn definition to apply to the current zone.

tasks.name: spawn
tasks.officialDescription: Creates SpawnData 'sValue'. (Executes 'spawn_actorPrefab' instead if sValue matches an actorPrefab ID.) 'sValue2' is the groupID. Groups won't spawn if actors of groupID already exist. NOTE: group spawns in random spots IN TRIGGER AREA, unless X,Y specified. fValue = group power level mod. bValue1 = group face toward trigger area center.

uses.description: Creates a group of actors using a named SpawnData definition. The desired power level for the group is the supplied modifier plus the party's power level (with zone/global mods). Actors are placed on random tiles inside the calling trigger's area, or inside a small region around the supplied tile coordinate if one is provided. With bools[0] set, the spawned actors face the centre of the placement area; without it, they face the player when a tile is provided or with bools[1]. The spawn FX defined by the SpawnData (if any) is played on each spawn.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The SpawnData ID to spawn. (If it instead matches an actorPrefab ID, the spawn_actorPrefab path is taken.)

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The groupID under which the spawned actors are registered. If a group with this ID already exists, nothing is spawned. Defaults to strings[0] if omitted.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: A modifier added to the party's power level to determine the spawned group's desired power. Defaults to 0.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, each spawned actor faces the centre of the placement area instead of randomly or toward the player.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true (and bools[0] is false), each spawned actor faces the player after placement.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The placement origin. If supplied, actors are placed within a small region around this tile; otherwise the calling trigger's whole area is used.

uses.description: Branches to the prefab-clone path when strings[0] matches an actorPrefab ID. Creates a single clone of the prefab at the supplied tile coordinate (or random within the trigger area), with the supplied facing rotation. Optionally plays a startup animation.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The actorPrefab ID to clone (taking this branch when it doesn't match a SpawnData entry).

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: If non-empty, an animation name to play on the freshly spawned actor.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The spawned actor's facing rotation.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the spawned actor uses the prefab's ID directly rather than a uniquified zone-specific ID.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The spawn position. If omitted, a random occupiable tile within the calling trigger's area is chosen.

tasks.name: spawn_actorPrefab
tasks.officialDescription: Creates actorPrefab 'sValue'at X,Y. 'sValue2' specifies an animation to use when spawning. fValue = facingRot. bValue1 = use actorPrefab ID exactly (instead of zone-specific actorID).

uses.description: Spawns a single clone of an actorPrefab at the supplied tile coordinate (or random within the calling trigger's area). The actor's facing rotation is set from floats[0]. With bools[0] = true, the spawned actor takes the prefab's ID exactly rather than a uniquified zone-specific ID. If strings[1] is non-empty, that animation is played on the actor after placement. If strings[0] is not a known actorPrefab, falls through to the SpawnData group-spawn behaviour (see spawn).

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The actorPrefab ID to clone.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: An animation to play on the freshly spawned actor.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The spawned actor's facing rotation.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the spawned actor uses the prefab's ID directly rather than a uniquified zone-specific ID.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The spawn position. If omitted, a random occupiable tile within the calling trigger's area is chosen.

tasks.name: spawn_actorPrefab_point
tasks.officialDescription: As 'spawn_actorPrefab', except X specifies what travelPoint to use, and Y specifies which set of travelPoints to use (0 red, 1 blue, 2 green, 3 brown).

uses.description: Spawns a single clone of an actorPrefab at a travel-point identified by the tile coordinate's X (the travel point number) and Y (the travel-point set, e.g. 0 red, 1 blue, 2 green, 3 brown). The spawned actor's facing rotation is taken from the travel-point's rotation rather than floats[0]. Other behaviour matches spawn_actorPrefab. If strings[0] is not a known actorPrefab, falls through to the SpawnData group-spawn behaviour (see spawn).

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The actorPrefab ID to clone.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: Travel-point identifier — the X is the travel-point number, the Y is the travel-point set (0 red, 1 blue, 2 green, 3 brown).

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: An animation to play on the freshly spawned actor.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The spawned actor's facing rotation (note: overwritten by the travel-point's own rotation).

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the spawned actor uses the prefab's ID directly rather than a uniquified zone-specific ID.

tasks.name: spawn_g
tasks.officialDescription: Same as 'spawn', but sValue is a globalVar to use as the spawnData.

uses.description: Looks up a SpawnData ID indirectly through a global variable's value, then dispatches the standard 'spawn' task with that resolved ID. Useful when the spawn definition depends on game state rather than being fixed in the task definition. All other inputs (group ID, power mod, facing flags, position) are passed through unchanged.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable whose string value holds the SpawnData ID to spawn.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The groupID under which the spawned actors are registered.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: A modifier added to the party's power level to determine the spawned group's desired power. Defaults to 0.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, each spawned actor faces the centre of the placement area.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, each spawned actor faces the player after placement.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The placement origin. If supplied, actors are placed within a small region around this tile.

tasks.name: spawn_g_nearPlayer
tasks.officialDescription: Same as 'spawn', but sValue is a globalVar to use as the spawnData, and the group is placed near the player. If b2, group is placed roughly 3 tiles behind the player.

uses.description: Like spawn_g, except the placement origin is forced to be near the player's tile. With bools[1] = true, the placement origin is shifted roughly 3 tiles 'behind' the player (using their facing direction). Convenient for ambush-style spawns close to the player without specifying coordinates.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable whose string value holds the SpawnData ID to spawn.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The groupID under which the spawned actors are registered.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: A modifier added to the party's power level to determine the spawned group's desired power. Defaults to 0.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, each spawned actor faces the centre of the placement area.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the placement origin is shifted roughly 3 tiles behind the player.

tasks.name: spawn_actorPrefab_point_specialZone
tasks.officialDescription: PROBABLY DO NOT USE. Adds a copy of actorPrefab 'sValue' to the current specialZone, on the xValue'th travelPoint of travelPoint set yValue.

uses.description: Clones an actorPrefab into the current specialZone (a parallel zone used for off-screen actors), placing it on a specific travel point in a specific travel-point set. The tile coordinate's X picks the travel-point index; the Y picks the set (0 red, 1 blue, etc.). The actor is fully healed and stripped of effects before insertion. With bools[0] = true, the actor uses the prefab's ID directly rather than a uniquified one. An optional spawn animation may be played. Marked PROBABLY DO NOT USE by the official description.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The actorPrefab ID to clone into the specialZone.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: Travel-point identifier — X is the travel-point index, Y is the travel-point set.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: An animation to play on the freshly spawned actor.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the spawned actor uses the prefab's ID directly rather than a uniquified actor ID.

tasks.name: spawn_location
tasks.officialDescription: Spawns actorPrefabs from the current location's auto_spawnDataID list, up the a maximum based on the current wealthLevel. Places them on the first unoccupied travelPoint set B item.

uses.description: Spawns the standard collection of actors for the current Location using its auto_spawnDataID list, picking randomly from that list up to a count determined by the Location's wealthLevel. Placed actors are dropped onto unoccupied travel_point0B items (set B). If the player owns a home in this location (via the 'bought_home_' globalVar), three special-spawn presets ('ss_sPortSpawn1/2/3') are additionally applied.

tasks.name: spawn_onItem
tasks.officialDescription: Creates SpawnData 'sValue' actors on items with 'sValue2' specialProperty. fValue = group power level mod. If bValue1, can spawn even if group is already extant. Actors look at XY if specified.

uses.description: Creates a group of actors using a named SpawnData and places each actor on a random tile whose items have a specified specialProperty. Currently, the group will always be named "spawn", which might be a bug. The desired power level for the group is the supplied modifier plus the party's power level (with zone/global mods). With bools[0] = true, the group is created even if a group with this name already exists. If a tile coordinate is supplied, each spawned actor faces it. The SpawnData's spawnFX (if any) is played on each spawn.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The SpawnData ID to spawn. Defaults to empty (which means no spawn occurs).

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The specialProperty (by name or integer) that target items must have. Defaults to 'none' if omitted.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: A modifier added to the party's power level to determine the spawned group's desired power. Defaults to 0.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the group is created even if a group with the same name already exists. Note: As of right now, all groups will be named "spawn", so this is required in order to work correctly if called more than once.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: If supplied, each spawned actor faces toward this tile after placement.

tasks.name: spawn_seamonster
tasks.officialDescription: Spawns a sea monster using spawnData 'sValue'. fValue specifies the desiredPowerLevel. If sValue is an empty string, finds the closest monsterBeacon item and uses 'sm' + its quantity as the spawnData ID.

uses.description: Spawns a sea monster fleet using a SpawnData definition, but only if the triggering actor is on or adjacent to deepwater terrain. The monster is placed at a random tile within the 'deepwater_search' AoE around the triggering actor. If strings[0] is empty, the closest nearby monsterBeacon item is consulted (its quantity is appended to 'sm' to form the SpawnData ID) and that beacon is marked used. A 'sm' Fleet is created from the spawned actors, with trade-good cargo gathered from each crew member and food added based on the total power level.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The desired power level for the sea monster's group.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The SpawnData ID for the sea monster. If empty, the nearest monsterBeacon item is consulted to determine the ID.

tasks.name: spawn_fleet
tasks.officialDescription: Spawns a battle fleet in the current zone using spawnData 'sValue' at TC X,Y with the commodoreID set in sValue2. fValue sets the fleetPowerLevelMod. If b1, is a merchant fleet instead. If b2, is elite.

uses.description: Creates a new Fleet using a SpawnData definition and places it at a tile coordinate, with a specified commodore actor ID. The fleet's faction comes from the SpawnData's factionOverride, and floats[0] modifies its power level. bools[0] flags the fleet as a merchant fleet (rather than a battle fleet), and bools[1] makes it elite.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The SpawnData ID used to build the fleet.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The actor ID to use as the fleet's commodore.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: A modifier added to the fleet's power level. Defaults to 0.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the fleet is created as a merchant fleet instead of a battle fleet.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the fleet is created as elite.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The tile coordinate where the fleet should appear.

tasks.name: spawn_corpse
tasks.officialDescription: Spawns a random corpse from partyActors_corpse either at XY or in the centermost occupiable tile in the trigger area.

uses.description: Spawns one of the player's previously-dead party-actor corpses (a 'partyActors_corpse' entry) onto the map. The corpse is placed at the supplied tile coordinate, or at the centremost occupiable tile in the calling trigger's area when none is supplied. Up to two corpses are tracked per save via the 'spawnedCorpse1' and 'spawnedCorpse2' global variables, and this task is a no-op once both slots are filled.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The position to place the spawned corpse. If omitted, the centremost occupiable tile in the trigger area is used.

tasks.name: talk
tasks.officialDescription: Enter dialog with actor 'sValue'. 'sValue2' specifies dialogNode if assigned. If bValue1, dialog is centered on speaker. If bValue2, makes dialog fullscreen.

uses.description: Starts a dialog conversation with a specific actor, optionally at a specific dialog node. The camera is normally pointed between the player and the talker; bools[0] suppresses this. With bools[1] = true, the dialog window is rendered fullscreen rather than near the speaker. If no actor is named (or the named actor doesn't exist), the 'talkToNoOne' path runs instead, opening dialog without a target actor.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The ID of the actor to talk to. If empty or not found, dialog opens without a specific speaker.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: An explicit dialog node ID to start at. If empty, the actor's default dialog node is used.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, suppresses the camera-between-talker-and-player movement that normally happens at the start of dialog.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the dialog UI is rendered fullscreen.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The Y coordinate is used as the dialog overlay's vertical anchor when bools[1] is false. Largely ignored when fullscreen mode is active.

tasks.name: tryDialog
tasks.officialDescription: Checks for any dialogOverrides for nodeID sValue.

uses.description: Attempts to enter dialog at a given node ID, applying any registered dialogOverrides that might redirect the conversation. If a tile coordinate is supplied and dialog was successfully entered, the camera is also pointed at that tile.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The dialog node ID to try entering.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: If supplied and dialog mode is successfully entered, the camera is pointed at this tile.

tasks.name: tryDialog_commodore
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Used specifically for dialog with an enemyFleet during fleet combat.

uses.description: Attempts to enter dialog at a given node, intended specifically for talking with an enemyFleet's commodore during fleet combat. On success, the camera is pointed at the enemy flagship and an 'exclamation' FX is played on it. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The dialog node ID to try entering.

tasks.name: exitDialog
tasks.officialDescription: Exits dialog mode.

uses.description: Forces the game back to travel mode, ending any current dialog. Has no effect if the game is not currently in dialog mode.

tasks.name: attractAttention
tasks.officialDescription: All actors (within fValue if specified) turn to face X,Y. If bValue1, works on all actors.

uses.description: Makes nearby actors face a target position. If no tile coordinate is supplied, the source is the triggering actor's position and the range is the default 12 tiles; otherwise the source is the supplied tile and the range is floats[0] (if non-zero). By default only enemies of the source are affected; bools[0] expands the effect to all actors.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The detection range, in tiles. If 0 or omitted, defaults to 12.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, affects all actors rather than just enemies.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The target position. If omitted, the triggering actor's position is used.

tasks.name: attractAttentionToPlayer
tasks.officialDescription: All actors (within fValue if specified) turn to face the player. If bValue1, works on all actors.

uses.description: Makes nearby actors face the current player's position. The default detection range is 12 tiles; floats[0] overrides this when non-zero. By default only enemies of the player are affected; bools[0] expands the effect to all actors.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The detection range, in tiles. If 0 or omitted, defaults to 12.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, affects all actors rather than just enemies.

tasks.name: executeDialogEffect
tasks.officialDescription: Executes special dialog effect 'sValue', using 'sValue2' dialogNode (optional), and passing in fValue (optional).

uses.description: Invokes a named DialogNode 'special dialog effect' from outside the dialog system. The triggering actor (if any) is passed as the talking actor, the optional dialog node ID is passed for context, and floats[0] is passed as a numeric argument the effect can use.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the special dialog effect to execute.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The dialog node ID associated with the effect (optional context).

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: A numeric argument passed through to the special dialog effect.

tasks.name: loadSpecialZone
tasks.officialDescription: Loads in zone 'sValue' as a non-interactive overlay at X,Y. fValue = depthMod. sValue2 = scale, if specified (default to 1). If bValue1, only draws zone if roofs are drawn.

uses.description: Loads a named zone as a non-interactive overlay anchored at the supplied tile coordinate. Its depth modifier comes from floats[0], its scale from strings[1] (parsed as a float, defaulting to 1), and bools[0] restricts drawing to only when roofs are visible. Useful for layering a backdrop or ship interior on top of the main zone.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the zone to load as a special overlay.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The overlay's anchor position (the tile its origin will sit at).

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The overlay's scale, parsed as a float. Defaults to 1 if missing or unparseable.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The depth modifier applied to the overlay.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the overlay is only drawn when the camera is drawing roofs.

tasks.name: loadSpecialZone_precision
tasks.officialDescription: Same as 'loadSpecialZone' task, but X,Y is divided by 100 to get a precise tile position.

uses.description: Like loadSpecialZone, except the supplied tile coordinate is divided by 100 to produce a high-precision sub-tile anchor position. Useful when you need to position an overlay with finer granularity than the integer tile grid.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the zone to load as a special overlay.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The overlay's anchor position (each component divided by 100 to give sub-tile precision).

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The overlay's scale, parsed as a float. Defaults to 1 if missing or unparseable.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The depth modifier applied to the overlay.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the overlay is only drawn when the camera is drawing roofs.

tasks.name: loadSpecialZone_playerFlagship
tasks.officialDescription: As 'loadSpecialZone', but disregards 'sValue' and loads the player's flagship bz instead.

uses.description: Loads the boarding-zone (interior) of the player's flagship as a non-interactive overlay anchored at the supplied tile coordinate. strings[0] is ignored — the zone is always derived from the player's current flagship. Other inputs match loadSpecialZone.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The overlay's anchor position.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The overlay's scale, parsed as a float. Defaults to 1 if missing or unparseable.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The depth modifier applied to the overlay.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the overlay is only drawn when the camera is drawing roofs.

tasks.name: loadSpecialZone_playerFlagship_precision
tasks.officialDescription: Same as 'loadSpecialZone_playerFlagship' task, but X,Y is divided by 100 to get a precise tile position.

uses.description: Like loadSpecialZone_playerFlagship, except the supplied tile coordinate is divided by 100 to produce a high-precision sub-tile anchor position.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The overlay's anchor position (each component divided by 100 to give sub-tile precision).

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The overlay's scale, parsed as a float. Defaults to 1 if missing or unparseable.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The depth modifier applied to the overlay.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the overlay is only drawn when the camera is drawing roofs.

tasks.name: moveSpecialZone
tasks.officialDescription: Moves specialZone to X,Y at fValue speed. Eases if bValue1.

uses.description: Animates the currently loaded special-zone overlay toward a new tile-coordinate position. With floats[0] > 0, that value sets the overlay's movement speed. With bools[0] set, the motion is eased rather than linear. With bools[1] set, the overlay yoyos: after reaching the target it returns to its starting position.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The destination anchor position for the special-zone overlay.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: If > 0, sets the overlay's movement speed.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the movement eases (decelerates) toward the target.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the overlay yoyos back to its starting position after reaching the target.

tasks.name: moveSpecialZone_precision
tasks.officialDescription: Same as 'moveSpecialZone' task, but X,Y is divided by 100 to get a precise tile position.

uses.description: Like moveSpecialZone, except the supplied tile coordinate is divided by 100 to produce a high-precision sub-tile target position.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The destination anchor position (each component divided by 100 to give sub-tile precision).

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: If > 0, sets the overlay's movement speed.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the movement eases (decelerates) toward the target.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the overlay yoyos back to its starting position after reaching the target.

tasks.name: scaleSpecialZone
tasks.officialDescription: Scale specialZone to sValue scale at fValue speed.

uses.description: Animates the currently loaded special-zone overlay toward a new scale value. The target scale comes from parsing strings[0] as a float; if it doesn't parse, the scale is unchanged. With floats[0] > 0, that value sets the overlay's scale-change speed.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The target scale, parsed as a float. Unparseable values leave the scale unchanged.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: If > 0, sets the overlay's scale-change speed.

tasks.name: mergeSpecialZone
tasks.officialDescription: Overwrites area with specialZone (excluding transparent/BG terrain). Sets specialZone at X,Y if specified. If b1, doesn't overwrite existing items.

uses.description: Bakes the currently loaded special-zone overlay into the current zone, copying its non-transparent terrain (and optionally items) onto the main zone at the overlay's current position. If a tile coordinate is supplied, the overlay is repositioned to that tile before merging. With bools[0] = true, existing items on the target tiles are preserved rather than overwritten.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, existing items on the target tiles are preserved; if false, they are overwritten by the overlay's items.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: If supplied, the special-zone overlay is moved to this tile before being merged.

tasks.name: barracksLineup
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE.

uses.description: Arranges every party actor (including barracks members) into a grid lineup at the supplied tile coordinate, with floats[0] tiles of horizontal spacing. The lineup wraps every 7 columns and breaks rows every 4 tiles. Each actor faces 90° (south). Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The horizontal spacing between lineup positions in tiles. Values <= 0 are treated as 1.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The origin tile of the lineup; actors are arranged in a grid starting here.

tasks.name: unlockExtras
tasks.officialDescription: Sets extra races and extra options to be unlocked and saves the config.ini file.

uses.description: Unlocks the 'extra options' and 'extra races' meta-progression flags, making any actorType flagged playerUnlockable available for the player to use. Also fires the WIN_ANYDIFFICULTY achievement attempt and writes the current game settings out to config.ini. Typically used after the player completes the game.

tasks.name: trySetAchievement
tasks.officialDescription: Attempts to set Steam achievement sValue.

uses.description: Sets a global variable named strings[0] to 1 and, if Steam is currently initialised and running, requests the corresponding Steam achievement. Used for unlocking achievements at scripted moments.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The Steam achievement ID (also used as the local globalVar name) to unlock.

tasks.name: retro
tasks.officialDescription: toggles Retro Visuals mode

uses.description: Flips the global RetroVisualsMode flag, switching the game's rendering between modern and retro pixel styles.

tasks.name: resetLocations
tasks.officialDescription: clears all data about the specified Location and reloads them from defaultLocations.txt (ex: resetloc port1)

uses.description: Clears the current game state's location data and reloads it from defaultLocations.txt. Useful for forcing locations back to their starting configuration when modding or debugging.

tasks.name: refreshUndamagedShipZones
tasks.officialDescription: Deletes all saved boat zones from the current save file for every ship actor in the player's fleet.

uses.description: Iterates every boat actor in the player's fleet and deletes its saved boarding-zone data (keys like 'bz_<id>') from the current save file. The next time those boarding zones are entered, they will be regenerated freshly. Useful for repairing ships visually after damage.

tasks.name: boardActor
tasks.officialDescription: sValue = target to board. sValue2 = boarder

uses.description: Initiates a boarding action: the boarder actor moves into the boarding zone of the target actor. Also applies a short interaction lockout after the transition.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the target actor being boarded.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The ID of the boarding actor (the one doing the boarding).

tasks.name: endBoarding
tasks.officialDescription: calls ZoneManager.I.returnFromBoardingZone()

uses.description: Returns the player from the current boarding zone back to the main zone. If a string is supplied, it is passed to the return call (typically identifying which boarding session to end); otherwise the default return is used.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: An optional identifier passed through to returnFromBoardingZone.

tasks.name: enterFleetBattle
tasks.officialDescription: sets gVars playerX & playerY to player pos. Enters new fleetBattle zone, bringing Player, sValue, and sValue2 Actors' fleets with them. Puts fleets on travel_point A,B,C respectively.

uses.description: Loads the fleet battle zone, bringing along the player's fleet plus the fleets of the actors named in strings[0] and strings[1]. The three fleets are positioned on travel-point sets A, B, and C respectively. This branch is taken when at least two strings are supplied (explicit fleet designations).

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose fleet should join the battle on side B.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose fleet should join the battle on side C.

uses.description: Auto-detects the opposing fleet from a nearby actor and enters the fleet battle zone. The triggering actor's fleet is the primary side; the nearest non-friendly actor within ~2 tiles (excluding the player and the same faction) supplies the opposing fleet. This branch runs when only one or zero strings are supplied.

tasks.name: enterFleetBattle_player
tasks.officialDescription: sets gVars playerX & playerY to player pos. Enters new fleetBattle zone, bringing Player, sValue, and sValue2 Actors' fleets with them. Puts fleets on travel_point A,B,C respectively. Sets the fleet battle as having been initiated by the player.

uses.description: As enterFleetBattle, but additionally flags the resulting battle as having been initiated by the player (which affects loot/honour rules). Brings the player's fleet plus the fleets of strings[0] and strings[1] onto sets A, B, and C respectively when at least two strings are supplied.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose fleet should join the battle on side B.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The ID of the actor whose fleet should join the battle on side C.

uses.description: Auto-detects the opposing fleet from a nearby actor and enters the fleet battle zone, flagging the battle as player-initiated. The triggering actor's fleet is the primary side; the nearest non-friendly actor within ~2 tiles supplies the opposing fleet. This branch runs when only one or zero strings are supplied.

tasks.name: setDialogSpeed
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the dialogSpeedMult game option to 'fValue'.

uses.description: Changes the global dialog-speed multiplier game option to a new value, controlling how fast dialog text renders.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The new dialog-speed multiplier.

tasks.name: setMoney
tasks.officialDescription: Sets current money to fValue, or formula sValue.

uses.description: Sets the player's current money. If strings[0] is empty, floats[0] is used as the new value directly; otherwise strings[0] is evaluated as a formula and its result is used. The result is truncated to an integer.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: A formula string whose calculated result is the new money value. Used when non-empty.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The new money value (truncated to integer). Used when strings[0] is empty.

tasks.name: enterCombat
tasks.officialDescription: Enter combat at X,Y with radius 'fValue' (or default radius if unspecified). 'sValue' is which actor intiated combat, if any.

uses.description: Forces the game into combat mode at the supplied tile coordinate. The battlefield radius is taken from floats[0] when non-zero (defaulting to 18 otherwise). If strings[0] is supplied, that actor is recorded as the combat initiator.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The ID of the actor that initiated combat. Defaults to empty (no initiator) if omitted.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The battlefield radius in tiles. Defaults to 18 if 0 or omitted.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The combat origin tile.

tasks.name: enterCombat_timer
tasks.officialDescription: Enters combat at X,Y, with a radius of 999 and sets action 'sValue' to be executed at X,Y after 'fValue' actTime.

uses.description: Forces combat mode with an effectively zone-wide radius (999) and schedules a delayed actionExecution: the named action will fire at the supplied tile coordinate after floats[0] act-time elapses. Useful for setting up a scripted timer-driven attack at the start of an encounter.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the action to execute when the timer fires.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The actTime (in act-time units) before the action fires.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The position used both for entering combat and for the action's location.

tasks.name: exitCombat
tasks.officialDescription: Exits combat

uses.description: Forcibly exits combat mode if the game is currently in combat. By default the exit is treated as 'victorious'; bools[0] flips this to a non-victorious exit. Has no effect outside combat mode.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the combat exit is non-victorious (e.g. the player retreated). If false or omitted, the exit is treated as a victory.

tasks.name: offerRest
tasks.officialDescription: Opens dialog box allowing player to rest and heal party, if nothing threatening is nearby.

uses.description: Opens a dialog prompt offering the player a chance to rest. If hostile enemies are nearby (making it unsafe to rest), the prompt instead displays a 'not safe to rest' message. Only runs while in travel mode.

tasks.name: advanceTimers
tasks.officialDescription: Advance all actTimers by fValue.

uses.description: Advances every actTimer in the current zone forward by floats[0] units of act-time. Useful for fast-forwarding scripted timed effects.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The amount of act-time to advance every active timer by.

tasks.name: scoreScreen
tasks.officialDescription: Brings up score screen, converts consumables into cash.

uses.description: Tallies the player's run results into the 'scoreScreen' dialog node: counts current cash, converts all 'convertIntoCashAtEnd' items in the party's inventories into gold (with item icons), grants any 'setGlobalVarWhenConverted' rewards (like crafting kits or class unlocks), updates per-run global vars (cashEarned, moneyReserves, valueThisRun, missionsSuccess), then opens the score-screen dialog. Resets the player's money to 0 afterwards.

tasks.name: setupGraveyard
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE.

uses.description: Lines up every actor in partyActors_dead (deceased party members) into a graveyard grid starting at the supplied tile coordinate. Each corpse is petrified, fully healed, dropped onto a 'platform' item, and faced south. The grid extends 18 tiles wide before wrapping. Caps at 122 entries. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The origin tile where the graveyard layout starts.

tasks.name: endGame
tasks.officialDescription: Sends the player to the main menu.

uses.description: Returns the game to the main menu, ending the current run. If extras were unlocked during the session, schedules a one-second timer that will trigger the 'unlockExtras' task to finalise the unlocks.

tasks.name: handleDeaths
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Updates 'dying' and 'dead' AVs for each actor in the current zone.

uses.description: Iterates every actor in the current zone and progresses any 'dying' actor toward 'dead' once their dying-duration runs out. Controllable actors get a deferred 'actorDying' UI timer (staggered 7.8 seconds apart) so their death animation plays in sequence; non-controllable actors are killed instantly with hitDust and smog FX. Has no effect if an 'actorDying' timer is already scheduled. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: getResponse_combat
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Sets the currentDialogNode's nextNodeID to the response for combat. Used in auto dialog.

uses.description: During auto-dialog with a hostile actor, resolves which 'combat' negotiation response should follow next, based on the triggering actor's power level, the player's power level, an unused float (the last one in the floats list), and the player's actor type. Then writes the resolved next-node ID into the current dialog node's nextNodeID list. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

optional.name: floats[0+]
optional.description: The negotiation parameter is taken from the LAST float in the list. Earlier floats are ignored. Defaults to whatever is supplied; missing floats may crash.

tasks.name: getResponse_peace
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Sets the currentDialogNode's nextNodeID to the response for wanting peace. Used in auto dialog.

uses.description: During auto-dialog with a hostile actor, resolves which 'peace' negotiation response should follow next, based on power levels and the player's actor type. Writes the resolved next-node ID into the current dialog node's nextNodeID list. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

optional.name: floats[0+]
optional.description: The negotiation parameter is taken from the LAST float in the list. Earlier floats are ignored. Defaults to whatever is supplied.

tasks.name: getResponse_bribe
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Sets the currentDialogNode's nextNodeID to the response for being bribed. Used in auto dialog.

uses.description: During auto-dialog with a hostile actor, resolves which 'bribe' negotiation response should follow next, based on power levels and the player's actor type. Writes the resolved next-node ID into the current dialog node's nextNodeID list. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

optional.name: floats[0+]
optional.description: The negotiation parameter is taken from the LAST float in the list. Earlier floats are ignored. Defaults to whatever is supplied.

tasks.name: getBribeAmount
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Sets the currentDialogNode's statements[0] to reflect a bribe amount used for auto dialog.

uses.description: Computes the bribe price for the triggering actor's combat group (14% of their combined power level) and rewrites the current dialog node's first statement to display that gold amount in a 'Give a gift?' prompt. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: subtractBribeAmount
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Subtracts the bribe amount used for auto dialog from the player's gold.

uses.description: Deducts the auto-dialog bribe amount (14% of the triggering actor's combat group power level) from the player's gold and adds that gold to the triggering actor's inventory as 'gp' items. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: add50gp
tasks.officialDescription: Gives 50 gold to the player.

uses.description: Adds 50 gold to the player's money.

tasks.name: addgp
tasks.officialDescription: Gives 'fValue' gold to the player. If 'sValue' is set, it should be a formula, with the result of that formula being the amount to give.

uses.description: Adds gold to the player. If strings[0] is non-empty, the gold amount comes from evaluating that string as a formula (with the player as actor context, floored); otherwise floats[0] is used directly as the amount.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: A formula whose calculated value (floored) is the amount of gold to add.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The amount of gold to add, used when strings[0] is empty or omitted.

tasks.name: subtractgp
tasks.officialDescription: Removes 'fValue' gold from the player. If 'sValue' is set, it should be a formula, with the result of that formula being the amount to remove.

uses.description: Deducts gold from the player. If strings[0] is non-empty, the amount comes from evaluating that string as a formula (with the player as actor context, floored); otherwise floats[0] is used directly as the amount.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: A formula whose calculated value (floored) is the amount of gold to remove.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The amount of gold to remove, used when strings[0] is empty or omitted.

tasks.name: subtract10gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 10 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 10 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract20gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 20 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 20 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract30gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 30 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 30 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract40gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 40 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 40 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract50gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 50 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 50 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract60gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 60 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 60 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract75gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 75 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 75 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract100gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 100 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 100 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract130gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 130 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 130 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract150gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 150 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 150 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract200gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 200 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 200 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract250gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 250 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 250 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract300gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 300 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 300 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract350gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 350 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 350 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract400gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 400 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 400 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract500gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 500 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 500 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract800gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 800 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 800 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract1000gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 1,000 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 1,000 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract5000gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 5,000 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 5,000 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtract10000gp
tasks.officialDescription: Subtracts 10,000 from the player's gold.

uses.description: Subtracts 10,000 gold from the player's money.

tasks.name: subtractAllgp
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the player's gold to 0.

uses.description: Resets the player's gold to 0 (effectively removing all of it).

tasks.name: giveGlobalVarGP
tasks.officialDescription: Adds the value set in 'sValue' globalVar of gold to the player.

uses.description: Reads a global variable's numeric value and adds that amount of gold to the player's money. The variable's value is truncated to an integer before being applied.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable whose numeric value is the amount of gold to add.

tasks.name: enterSellMode
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Opens player actor inventories and sets tradeMode=true.

uses.description: Enables the global trade-mode flag and opens the party members' inventories side-by-side for selling. Used as the entry point to sell-only trading screens. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: openCargo
tasks.officialDescription: Shows the player's cargo inventory window.

uses.description: Opens the player's cargo inventory window via the input manager, allowing the player to inspect or rearrange their cargo.

tasks.name: autobuy_food
tasks.officialDescription: Forces the player to buy as much food as will fit into their cargo, or that they can afford, whichever is lower.

uses.description: Buys as many units of 'food' as possible: the smaller of (cargo space available) and (gold / food buy price). The total cost is deducted from the player's gold and the food is added to the cargo inventory.

tasks.name: enterSellMode_cargo
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Opens player cargo inventory and sets tradeMode=true.

uses.description: Enables the global trade-mode flag and opens the player's cargo inventory window for selling. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: enterSellMode_stash
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Opens player stash inventory and sets tradeMode=true.

uses.description: Enables the global trade-mode flag and opens the player's stash inventory window for selling. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: enterBuyMode
tasks.officialDescription: Enters buy/sell mode with the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog).

uses.description: Opens the buy/sell trading UI with the triggering actor (the dialog talker, if in dialog). The shop side shows that actor's priority container if any (or their main inventory otherwise); the player side shows the stash inventory. Buttons/cursors are reconfigured for trading.

tasks.name: enterBuyMode_location
tasks.officialDescription: Enters buy/sell mode for the current location's cargo inventory.

uses.description: Opens the buy/sell trading UI against the current Location's cargo inventory (the shop side) and the player's cargo (the player side). Has no effect if the player is not currently in a Location.

tasks.name: enterBuyMode_locationItems
tasks.officialDescription: Enters buy/sell mode for the current location's stash inventory.

uses.description: Opens the buy/sell trading UI against the current Location's stash inventory (items for sale) and the player's stash. Has no effect if the player is not currently in a Location.

tasks.name: enterBuyMode_fleet
tasks.officialDescription: Enters buy/sell mode with the fleet currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) belongs to.

uses.description: Opens the buy/sell trading UI against the cargo of the fleet that the triggering actor belongs to (the shop side) and the player's cargo (the player side). Buttons/cursors are reconfigured for trading. Used for buying from another fleet's stockpile.

tasks.name: setBuyPriceMult
tasks.officialDescription: Sets Globals.buyPriceMult to 'fValue'. Item prices are multiplied by this. Reset to 1 before and after dialog mode.

uses.description: Sets the global buy-price multiplier, which is applied to all item buy prices. Useful for discount or surcharge effects during dialog. The multiplier is reset to 1 before and after dialog mode by the game engine.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The new buy-price multiplier value.

tasks.name: buyItem_confirmWindow
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE.

uses.description: Opens the slider-based buy-quantity confirmation window for purchasing a specific item, using the last float as the maximum slider value (or 1 if 0). After buying, opens either cargo or stash depending on whether the item is cargo. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The item ID being purchased.

optional.name: floats[0+]
optional.description: The slider-maximum is read from the LAST float in the list (treated as 1 if 0).

tasks.name: buyGoods_confirmWindow
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE.

uses.description: Opens the slider-based buy-quantity confirmation window for purchasing the current Location's trade good, using the last float as the maximum slider value (or 1 if 0). Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

optional.name: floats[0+]
optional.description: The slider-maximum is read from the LAST float in the list (treated as 1 if 0).

tasks.name: buyFood_fillCargo
tasks.officialDescription: Adds as much food as the player can hold to their cargo, if they can afford it, and subtracts the cost from their gold.

uses.description: Buys exactly the amount of food needed to fill the player's cargo capacity to maximum, but only if they can afford the full amount. If they can't, no food is bought and no gold is spent.

tasks.name: buyFood
tasks.officialDescription: Adds 'fValue' food to the player's cargo, if they can afford it, and subtracts the cost from their gold.

uses.description: Buys a specified amount of food, but only if the player can afford the total cost. The quantity is taken from the LAST float in the floats list (not the first). If the player can't afford it, nothing happens.

optional.name: floats[0+]
optional.description: The quantity to buy is taken from the LAST float in the list.

tasks.name: buyGoods
tasks.officialDescription: Adds 'fValue' of the current location's tradeGood_itemTypeID to the player's cargo, if they can afford it, and subtracts the cost from their gold.

uses.description: Buys a specified amount of the current Location's trade good, but only if the player can afford the total cost. The Location is determined from the current zone's ID. The quantity is taken from the LAST float in the floats list.

optional.name: floats[0+]
optional.description: The quantity to buy is taken from the LAST float in the list.

tasks.name: giveFood
tasks.officialDescription: Adds 'fValue' food to the player's cargo, if it can fit.

uses.description: Gives the player a specified quantity of 'food' as cargo, with no gold cost. The quantity is taken from the LAST float in the floats list.

optional.name: floats[0+]
optional.description: The quantity to give is taken from the LAST float in the list.

tasks.name: lose10Food
tasks.officialDescription: Removes 10 food from the player's cargo inventory.

uses.description: Removes 10 units of food from the player's cargo inventory. Has no effect if the player has no food.

tasks.name: removeCargo
tasks.officialDescription: Removes 'fValue' quantity of item ID 'sValue' from the player's cargo inventory.

uses.description: Removes a specific quantity of a named item from the player's cargo inventory.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the item to remove from cargo.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The quantity to remove.

tasks.name: removeCargo_g
tasks.officialDescription: Removes 'fValue' quantity of the item ID set in globalVar 'sValue' from the player's cargo inventory.

uses.description: Removes a specific quantity of an item from the player's cargo inventory, where the item ID is looked up indirectly through a global variable's string value.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable whose string value holds the item ID to remove.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The quantity to remove.

tasks.name: removeItem_g
tasks.officialDescription: Removes 'fValue' quantity of the item ID set in globalVar 'sValue' from the player's available inventory. Tries to remove from the stash, cargo, and any crew inventories until the quantity is fully removed.

uses.description: Removes a specific quantity of an item from the player's combined inventory (stash, cargo, and crew inventories), where the item ID is looked up indirectly through a global variable's string value. Removal cascades across inventories until the quantity has been fully consumed.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable whose string value holds the item ID to remove.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The total quantity to remove from the party's inventories.

tasks.name: removeItemFromParty
tasks.officialDescription: Removes 'fValue' quantity of item ID 'sValue' from the player's available inventory. Tries to remove from the stash, cargo, and any crew inventories until the quantity is fully removed.

uses.description: Removes a specific quantity of a named item from the player's combined inventory (stash, cargo, and crew inventories). Removal cascades across inventories until the quantity has been fully consumed.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the item to remove.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The total quantity to remove from the party's inventories.

tasks.name: refreshStatMods
tasks.officialDescription: Resets all enemy stat multipliers, player stat modifiers, boat mods and day bonuses for every actor in the current zone.

uses.description: Iterates every actor in the zone, plus every party actor and every barracks actor, and recomputes their stat modifiers. Useful after changing global modifiers (difficulty, day bonuses, fleet mods) so the effects propagate to every existing actor.

tasks.name: grantXPShare_5percent
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Gives 5% of each class's XP globalVar to every current partyActor if globalVar 'XPShare' is set to 1.

uses.description: For every party actor (and every barracks actor), awards 5% of each class's stored XP global variable as XP in that class. Active-party shares are gated on the 'XPShare' globalVar being > 0 (and on each class having more than 100 XP); barracks shares run whenever XPShare > 0 with no minimum check. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: hostile
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the current task actor to hostile = true.

uses.description: Marks the triggering actor as hostile, so they will attack the player and player allies.

tasks.name: playerInitiateCombat
tasks.officialDescription: Causes the player to initiate combat with the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog).

uses.description: Switches back to travel mode, idles both the player and the triggering actor's animations, then has the player initiate combat against the triggering actor. Clears the actorID after dispatch.

tasks.name: enemyInitiateCombat
tasks.officialDescription: Causes the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) to initiate combat with the player.

uses.description: Switches back to travel mode, idles both the player and the triggering actor's animations, then has the triggering actor initiate combat against the player. Clears the actorID after dispatch.

tasks.name: giveQuestItemReward
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Gives gold equal to the value of the item ID set in globalVar 'tavern_questItem'.

uses.description: Looks up the item ID stored in the 'tavern_questItem' global variable and awards the player gold equal to that item's value. Also tracks the reward in 'earnedCashTotal'. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: reassignQuestItem
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Sets globalVar 'tavern_questItem' to a random ID from the 'loot_quest' item's toMake list.

uses.description: Creates a 'loot_quest' item, rolls its replaceWith_toMake list to determine a concrete item ID, then stores that ID in the 'tavern_questItem' global variable. Used by tavern quest setup. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: gotoNodeIf
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used in dialog. Tries to go to dialogNode 'sValue' if formula 'sValue2' passes.

uses.description: Conditional dialog branch: if the supplied formula evaluates to 1 or greater (with the triggering actor as the formula's context), the dialog jumps to the named dialog node. Otherwise the task is a no-op.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The dialog node ID to jump to when the condition passes.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The formula whose result determines whether the branch is taken (taken when >= 1).

tasks.name: gotoNodeDelay
tasks.officialDescription: Tries to go to dialogNode 'sValue' after 'fValue' seconds. Uses the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) as the talker.

uses.description: Schedules a delayed dialog jump: after floats[0] seconds, a 'talkToActor' UI timer fires that switches dialog to the named node with the triggering actor as the talker. The interaction lockout is also extended to the same delay so input is blocked during the wait.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The dialog node ID to switch to after the delay.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The delay in seconds before the dialog switch happens.

tasks.name: delayDialog
tasks.officialDescription: If in dialog mode, delays showing the current dialogNode for fValue seconds.

uses.description: Hides the dialog window and pauses player input for floats[0] seconds, then resumes the same dialog node automatically. Useful for adding dramatic pauses between dialog statements.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The delay (in seconds) before dialog resumes.

tasks.name: incOnNewDay
tasks.officialDescription: Increases the number at the end of the dialogNodeID of the task actor by 1 if at least 1 day has passed since this task was called for the task actor.

uses.description: For the triggering actor, increments the trailing number on their current dialogNodeID — but only if at least 24 hours of in-game time have passed since the last time this task was called for that actor and node. If incremented, dialog jumps to the new node. The 'incOnNewDay_<nodeID>' global variable is bumped in both cases so the next attempt re-checks the elapsed time.

optional.name: floats[0+]
optional.description: The LAST float in the floats list is passed as an extra parameter to the changeToDialogNode call (used as a delay or flag depending on the dialog system).

tasks.name: incDefaultNode
tasks.officialDescription: Increases the number at the end of the dialogNodeID of the task actor by 1.

uses.description: Increments the trailing number at the end of the triggering actor's current dialogNodeID by 1, so subsequent conversations advance to the next node in a numbered sequence. Has no effect if the actor's current dialogNodeID is empty.

tasks.name: setAsDialogNode
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the dialogNodeID for the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) to 'sValue', or the currentDialogNode if unset.

uses.description: Assigns the triggering actor's persistent dialogNodeID. If a node ID is supplied, that value is used; otherwise the currently executing dialog node's ID is used. The next time someone talks to this actor, dialog starts at that node.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The new dialogNodeID to assign. If omitted, the currently executing dialog node's ID is used.

tasks.name: setDefaultNodeIf
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the dialogNodeID for the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) to 'sValue' if the formula in 'svalue2' passes.

uses.description: Conditionally assigns the triggering actor's persistent dialogNodeID. Evaluates the supplied formula with the actor as context; if it evaluates to 1 or greater, the dialog node ID is set to strings[0]. Otherwise the task is a no-op.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The dialogNodeID to assign if the condition passes.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The formula whose result determines whether the assignment happens (assigns when >= 1).

tasks.name: clearDialogNode
tasks.officialDescription: Clears the dialogNode of the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog).

uses.description: Clears the triggering actor's persistent dialogNodeID, setting it to an empty string so they no longer have a default dialog node when talked to.

tasks.name: flee
tasks.officialDescription: The currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) is removed from the zone.

uses.description: Causes the triggering actor to flee combat: a 'sfx_cloth1' is played, ~90% of their XP value is awarded to the player (boat XP if they are a boat, regular otherwise), a flee FX (default 'actor_flee', or override from strings[1]) plays on them, and they are removed from the zone. A confirmation popup is shown and combat resumes once the player dismisses it.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: An optional override for the FX played as the actor flees. Defaults to 'actor_flee' if omitted.

tasks.name: enemyFlee
tasks.officialDescription: All actors in the combat area that are the same faction as the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) are removed from the zone, then the playerParty is awarded XP.

uses.description: Causes the entire enemy combat group to flee: every same-faction actor within an 18-tile radius of the triggering actor is removed (with a flee FX on each), a darken FX is played at the trigger origin, and the party is awarded ~95% of the group's power level as XP.

tasks.name: playerFlee
tasks.officialDescription: Ends combat with any actors within the combat area that have the same faction as the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog), then gives 'invisible' AV to playerParty.

uses.description: The player party flees combat: every same-faction actor within an 18-tile radius of the triggering actor stops targeting and clears their 'auto' dialog node; each party actor receives a staggered 'invisible' actor-value effect (starting at 12 seconds and incrementing by 3 per actor) so they can escape unseen.

tasks.name: enemyMoraleUp
tasks.officialDescription: Increases the morale by 1 stage for actors within the combat area that are the same faction as the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog).

uses.description: Increases the morale actor-value of every same-faction actor within an 18-tile radius of the triggering actor by 1 stage for 65 seconds.

tasks.name: enemyMoraleDown
tasks.officialDescription: Lowers the morale by 1 stage for actors within the combat area that are the same faction as the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog).

uses.description: Decreases the morale actor-value of every same-faction actor within an 18-tile radius of the triggering actor by 1 stage for 65 seconds.

tasks.name: playerMoraleUp
tasks.officialDescription: Increases the morale stage by 1 for all partyActors.

uses.description: Applies a +1 morale actor-value effect (for 65 seconds) to every party actor.

tasks.name: crewMorale
tasks.officialDescription: Increases (or decreases) player crew morale by fValue.

uses.description: Modifies the player's crew morale meta-stat by the supplied delta. Positive values raise morale; negative values lower it.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The amount to add to the crew morale. Negative values lower morale.

tasks.name: peace
tasks.officialDescription: Ends combat with any actors within the combat area that have the same faction as the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog), sets their hostile to false and awards the playerParty with XP.

uses.description: Resolves combat peacefully with the enemy group: every same-faction actor within an 18-tile radius of the triggering actor has their 'auto' dialog redirected to 'auto_peaceful', their AI target cleared, their hostility removed, and their XP-on-defeat flag turned off. The player party is awarded ~95% of the enemy group's power level as XP.

tasks.name: peaceXPrewardOnly
tasks.officialDescription: Gives the XP to the playerParty that would be rewarded with the 'peace' task, but doesn't change any actors.

uses.description: Awards the player party ~95% of the triggering actor's combat group power level as XP, without modifying any actors. Used to grant peace-style XP rewards without ending the encounter.

tasks.name: moveCamXY
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used as a taskString. Adds floats[0], floats[1] to the camera's current X,Y position.

uses.description: Nudges the camera's target position by an X,Y offset taken from the first two floats. Must be invoked as a taskString so that the float list is preserved (rather than getting collapsed into other parameters).

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The X amount to add to the camera's target position.

required.name: floats[1]
required.description: The Y amount to add to the camera's target position.

tasks.name: moveXY
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used as a taskString. Moves the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) by floats[0] X and floats[1] Y, with no restrictions.

uses.description: Sets the triggering actor's movement vector to an X,Y offset taken from the first two floats, with no boundary or collision checks. Must be invoked as a taskString. Has no effect if there is no triggering actor.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The X component of the movement vector.

required.name: floats[1]
required.description: The Y component of the movement vector.

tasks.name: moveX1
tasks.officialDescription: Moves the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) to the right 1 tile, with no restrictions.

uses.description: Sets the triggering actor's movement vector to (1, 0), moving them one tile to the right with no boundary or collision checks. Has no effect if there is no triggering actor.

tasks.name: moveY1
tasks.officialDescription: Moves the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) down 1 tile, with no restrictions.

uses.description: Sets the triggering actor's movement vector to (0, 1), moving them one tile down with no boundary or collision checks. Has no effect if there is no triggering actor.

tasks.name: moveXNeg1
tasks.officialDescription: Moves the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) to the left 1 tile, with no restrictions.

uses.description: Sets the triggering actor's movement vector to (-1, 0), moving them one tile to the left with no boundary or collision checks. Has no effect if there is no triggering actor.

tasks.name: moveYNeg1
tasks.officialDescription: Moves the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) up 1 tile, with no restrictions.

uses.description: Sets the triggering actor's movement vector to (0, -1), moving them one tile up with no boundary or collision checks. Has no effect if there is no triggering actor.

tasks.name: playerFacingRot
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the current player actor's facingRot to 'fValue'.

uses.description: Sets the current player actor's facingRot to a specified value. Also schedules a near-immediate facingRot triggerEffect so the rotation is enforced even if the player is mid-movement.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The new facing rotation value for the player.

tasks.name: playerAnim
tasks.officialDescription: Makes the current player actor play animation 'sValue'.

uses.description: Plays a named animation on the current player actor. Has no effect if there is no current player.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the animation to play on the player.

tasks.name: facingRot90
tasks.officialDescription: Sets currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) to 90 facingRot.

uses.description: Sets the triggering actor's facingRot to 90° (south). Also schedules a near-immediate facingRot triggerEffect so the rotation is enforced even if the actor is mid-movement.

tasks.name: facingRot180
tasks.officialDescription: Sets currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) to 180 facingRot.

uses.description: Sets the triggering actor's facingRot to 180° (west). Also schedules a near-immediate facingRot triggerEffect so the rotation is enforced even if the actor is mid-movement.

tasks.name: facingRot0
tasks.officialDescription: Sets currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) to 0 facingRot.

uses.description: Sets the triggering actor's facingRot to 0° (north). Also schedules a near-immediate facingRot triggerEffect so the rotation is enforced even if the actor is mid-movement.

tasks.name: facingRot270
tasks.officialDescription: Sets currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) to 270 facingRot.

uses.description: Sets the triggering actor's facingRot to 270° (east). Also schedules a near-immediate facingRot triggerEffect so the rotation is enforced even if the actor is mid-movement.

tasks.name: facingRotPrev
tasks.officialDescription: Sets currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) to talkerPreviousFacingRot rot.

uses.description: Restores the triggering actor's facingRot to the value previously stashed in talkerPreviousFacingRot (typically captured at the start of dialog). Used to return the actor to their original facing once a conversation ends.

tasks.name: setPrevFacingRot
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the talkerPreviousFacingRot to the current rotation of the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog). If sValue is set, it should be the rotation to set it to instead. If sValue isn't set, but fValue is > 0, it will be set to that rotation instead (360 == 0). The talkerPreviousFacingRot is the rotation the currentActorTalkingTo will be set to once dialog finishes.

uses.description: Captures the triggering actor's current facingRot into the talkerPreviousFacingRot field so it can be restored later (typically at dialog end). If strings[0] is non-empty, that value is parsed and used instead. If strings[0] is empty but floats[0] > 0, floats[0] (modulo 360) is used instead.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: An explicit rotation value (as a string parsed to float) to store in talkerPreviousFacingRot.

optional.name: floats[0]
