optional.description: An explicit rotation value to store (used when strings[0] is empty and floats[0] > 0; wrapped to [0,360)).

tasks.name: lookAtTalker
tasks.officialDescription: Makes the player look at the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog).

uses.description: Computes the nearest 90° rotation pointing from the player toward the triggering actor and schedules a near-immediate facingRot triggerEffect on the player. If the player is moving, two follow-up rotation enforcements are scheduled at 0.95s and 1.2s to override their movement-induced facing changes. Defaults to 90° if no triggering actor exists.

tasks.name: lookAtPlayer
tasks.officialDescription: Makes the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) look in the direction of the current player actor.

uses.description: Computes the nearest 90° rotation pointing from the triggering actor toward the player and sets the actor's facingRot to that value, also scheduling a near-immediate facingRot triggerEffect. If the actor is moving, two follow-up rotation enforcements are scheduled at 0.95s and 1.2s to override their movement-induced facing changes. Defaults to 90° if no triggering actor exists.

tasks.name: setAI_idle
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the ai_lua script for the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) to 'idle'.

uses.description: Switches the triggering actor's AI Lua script to 'idle', so they stop pursuing or acting and just stand around.

tasks.name: setAI_idle_flag
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the ai_lua script for the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) to 'idle_flag'.

uses.description: Switches the triggering actor's AI Lua script to 'idle_flag', a variant of idle that sets/uses an internal flag (typically marking the actor as scripted-idle).

tasks.name: die
tasks.officialDescription: Kills the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog), even if they have unkillable set.

uses.description: Forcibly kills the triggering actor by clearing their unkillable flag and applying -99999 to their HP. Used for scripted deaths that should bypass invulnerability.

tasks.name: escapeGate
tasks.officialDescription: Executes action 'escapeGate_act' on the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog), which removes them from the zone.

uses.description: Runs the 'escapeGate_act' action on the triggering actor, which causes them to exit the zone. If they're hostile and combat is active, their XP value is added to the player's pending XP award. Also adds a 1-second delay to the next action.

tasks.name: removeTalker
tasks.officialDescription: Removes the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) from the zone.

uses.description: Removes the triggering actor from the current zone, with no FX or notification. Useful for silently despawning an NPC after dialog.

tasks.name: setOnlyNPCDialogWindowSizeY
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the dialog window height to 'fValue'. If unset in a dialog specialEffect, or set to 0, sets the size to default (200).

uses.description: Resizes the NPC dialog window's height. The new height is the LAST float in the floats list (or 0/default if no float is supplied). Used to make narrative dialogs taller or shorter than the usual single-line default.

optional.name: floats[0+]
optional.description: The new dialog window height; the LAST float in the list is used. A value of 0 (or no float) restores the default height (200).

tasks.name: setDialogWindowSizeYAuto
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the dialog window height based on the text in the current dialogNode statements text if it overflows the default height.

uses.description: Resizes the NPC dialog window to automatically fit the current dialog node's text, growing taller if the default 200px height isn't enough.

tasks.name: setPCDialogWindowSizeY
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the dialog options window height to 'fValue'. If unset in a dialog specialEffect, or set to 0, sets the size to default (200).

uses.description: Resizes the player-choices (PC) dialog window's height. The new height is the LAST float in the floats list (or 0/default if no float is supplied).

optional.name: floats[0+]
optional.description: The new dialog window height; the LAST float in the list is used. A value of 0 (or no float) restores the default height (200).

tasks.name: resetPCDialogWindowSizeY
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the player's dialog window height to default (200).

uses.description: Restores the player-choices (PC) dialog window to the default height (200).

tasks.name: showCharstats
tasks.officialDescription: Unhides the character stats window, if it was hidden.

uses.description: Marks the character-stats UI as not hidden and forces it to display immediately. Useful for restoring this window after a cutscene that hid it.

tasks.name: showQuickslots
tasks.officialDescription: Unhides character stats and quickslots.

uses.description: Marks the quickslots UI (and any general 'hiddenUI' state) as not hidden and forces the quickslots window to display immediately.

tasks.name: hideQuickslots
tasks.officialDescription: Hides character stats and quickslots window.

uses.description: Despite the official description, the code actually sets hideQuickslots to false (i.e. shows the quickslots UI). Modders should treat this as showing the quickslots, not hiding them.

tasks.name: setCombatUIVisible
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the turn order window and combat commands window visibility to bValue1. Also hides quickslots and character stats windows if bValue1 is false.

uses.description: Sets the visibility of the combat-specific UI (turn order, combat commands). Setting bools[0] = false also forces the quickslots and character stats windows to hide; setting bools[0] = true shows the combat UI.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the combat UI is shown. If false (or omitted), the combat UI is hidden, along with quickslots and character stats.

tasks.name: showEscFactionWindow
tasks.officialDescription: Shows just the faction part of the escMenu and sets nextDialogNode_hideWindow = true.

uses.description: Opens the escape-menu UI but shows only the right-hand 'faction info' panel (leaving the main menu and left info hidden). Also flags the next dialog node so its window is hidden upon arrival, which is useful when handing control off to faction UI.

tasks.name: hideEscWindows
tasks.officialDescription: Hides all windows used for the escMenu.

uses.description: Hides every window associated with the escape menu (main menu, left info, right info), returning the screen to gameplay view.

tasks.name: unlockGem
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE.

uses.description: Unlocks and enables the Help Gem if it isn't already unlocked, resetting its recharge timer to 0 so it is immediately usable. Also plays 'superSparkles' and 'longGoodAuraSound' FX on the player. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description; unlockHelpGem is the preferred entry point.

tasks.name: scoreScreenResponse
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE.

uses.description: Selects the next dialog node to use as the score-screen's narrative response, based on the player's earned value and deaths during the run. The next-node ID is built from 'score_<profitTier>_<deathTier>' (profit tiers: noProfit/lowProfit/medProfit/highProfit; death tiers: noDeath/oneDeath/manyDeath). Among the dialog nodes whose IDs start with this prefix, the one that has been used the fewest times is selected. Falls back to 'goToTown' if no matching node exists. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: research_report
tasks.officialDescription: Sets 'sValue' dialogNode's statements based on journalEntries that have yet to be sold to a researcher.

uses.description: Rebuilds the dialog node identified by strings[0] so it lists every previously-unsold non-help journal entry the player has discovered, grouping by rarity and computing gold/fame rewards from journalEntryRewardValues and journalEntryFameValues. Each statement embeds command markup (<cmd=fame=...><cmd=fx=...><cmd=money=...>) so the dialog automatically pays out as the player reads. Rare entries (rarity 5+) also award faction favor. After running, the dialog node's next-node is set to 'researcher_afterReport' and journal entries are marked as sold. If the player has nothing unsold to report, a fallback 'no new discoveries' statement is added.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The dialog node ID to rebuild as the researcher's report.

tasks.name: cartographer_report
tasks.officialDescription: Sets up the 'sValue' dialogNode to be used with a cartographer's report. Also sets the 'mapPercentPaid globalVar to the currently explored overworld percentage.

uses.description: Rebuilds the dialog node identified by strings[0] to report on the player's overworld discovery progress, computing the percentage of the 'eral' overworld that has been uncovered from fog of war. Generates dialog statements that pay out gold/fame proportional to the newly-explored percentage (compared to the last time payment was made). Updates the 'mapPercentPaid' global variable to the current percentage so future reports only pay for additional progress.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The dialog node ID to rebuild as the cartographer's report.

tasks.name: donate
tasks.officialDescription: Acts as if the player had donated 'fValue' gold to the current location, which increases its favor and gives trade fame.

uses.description: Treats the player as having donated a specified amount of gold to the current Location. If the location has a faction, that faction's favor goes up (fValue * 0.001) and trade fame goes up the same amount (with piracy fame going down by the same delta). If the location has no faction, only the player's trade and piracy fame are adjusted. Has no effect outside of a Location.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The amount of (pretend) gold donated. Used to scale favor, trade fame, and piracy fame adjustments.

tasks.name: jobDialogOptions
tasks.officialDescription: Sets up the dialogNode ID 'sValue' dialogOptions for the current location's set questTypes.

uses.description: Builds out a quest selection dialog at the named dialog node, replacing its options with one entry per quest type the current Location is offering (letter delivery, cargo, fleet defeat, monster slay, monster den clearing, salvage). For each quest type, sub-nodes are dynamically created (a 'job' description node and an 'accept' confirmation node) wired up with the appropriate quest globalVars. Has no effect outside a Location.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The dialog node ID whose options should be rebuilt as the location's quest menu.

tasks.name: refreshLocQuests
tasks.officialDescription: Randomizes the available questTypes for the current location, if it is a port and has 'sign_icon_letter' in its tooltipIcons.

uses.description: Forces the current Location to randomize its available quest types, clearing any existing quests first. Used to refresh the quest board for the player.

tasks.name: stealloc
tasks.officialDescription: Converts all port locations in the overworld that can be converted to the player's current faction, as set in globalVar 'playerFaction'.

uses.description: Converts every overworld port location that is allowed to change faction to the player's faction (read from the 'playerFaction' globalVar). Performed by 'investing' a massive amount into each, without affecting wealth level. Has no effect if the player faction isn't a known faction.

tasks.name: cancelQuest
tasks.officialDescription: Resets all quest-related globalVars and hides all overworld locations with a 'monsterden' tag.

uses.description: Clears all current quest globalVars (questType, questGiver, questReward, questDest, questLog, questGoal, questRoyal, questStartNum, questCompleted, questLogImage) and hides every monsterden location in the eral overworld so they are no longer discoverable. Used to abandon a quest cleanly.

tasks.name: cancelRoyalQuest
tasks.officialDescription: Resets all quest-related globalVars and hides all overworld locations with a 'monsterden' tag, if globalVar 'questRoyal' is > 0.

uses.description: Like cancelQuest, but only runs when the 'questRoyal' globalVar is greater than 0. Used to abandon a royal quest while leaving normal quests alone.

tasks.name: cleanUpQuest
tasks.officialDescription: Resets all quest-related globalVars and hides all overworld locations with a 'monsterden' tag.

uses.description: Clears all quest globalVars and hides monsterden locations in the eral overworld, equivalent to cancelQuest but typically run automatically after a quest concludes successfully.

tasks.name: royalJobDialogOptions
tasks.officialDescription: Sets up the dialogNode ID 'sValue' and its dialogOptions for the current royal quest, set in globalVar 'royalQuestType'.

uses.description: Builds out a royal-quest acceptance dialog at the named dialog node, replacing its options based on the current royal quest type and goal stored in 'royalQuestType' and 'royalQuestGoal' globalVars. Supports letter, cargo, defeat (fleet), defeatbattle, defeatelite, alliance, item, and grove quest types. Generates a job-description sub-node and an accept sub-node wired up with the appropriate quest globalVars and follow-up tasks.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The dialog node ID whose options should be rebuilt as the royal quest acceptance menu.

tasks.name: refreshRoyalQuests
tasks.officialDescription: Randomizes the current royal quest, if one isn't already set.

uses.description: Triggers the game state's royal-quest refresh logic, which rolls a new royal quest if one isn't currently active.

tasks.name: refreshRoyalTitle
tasks.officialDescription: Sets globalVar 'playerTitle' to an empty string.

uses.description: Clears the 'playerTitle' globalVar, removing any royal title currently associated with the player.

tasks.name: investigator_nearestLoc
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the currentActorTalkingTo investigator's response for when finding the nearest undiscovered location.

uses.description: Computes the nearest undiscovered, non-monsterden location in the 'eral' overworld from the current Location's position, then stores a descriptive 'investigatorAnswer_<talkerID>' globalVar with the result. If there are no undiscovered locations, stores a 'discovered everything' message instead. Used by investigator NPC dialog.

tasks.name: investigator_tradespecial
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the currentActorTalkingTo investigator's response for when finding out next month's trade special.

uses.description: Reads the next-trade-special globalVars (next_tradeSpecial_locID and next_tradeSpecial_goodsID) — refreshing them via the game-state's trade-special roll if no next-special is set — and stores a human-readable 'investigatorAnswer_<talkerID>' globalVar with the result.

tasks.name: investigator_slainenemy
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the currentActorTalkingTo investigator's response and task to perform for when checking for unexamined monsters.

uses.description: Searches the player's kill list for any actor type whose journal entry is known but not yet recorded; if found, stores a 'learnJournalEntry' task in the 'investigatorTask_<talkerID>' globalVar and sets a confirmation message in 'investigatorAnswer_<talkerID>'. If everything has already been researched, only the apologetic answer is stored.

tasks.name: investigator_fleetpos
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the currentActorTalkingTo investigator's response and task to perform for when tracking faction ID 'sValue's fleets.

uses.description: Sets up an investigator-purchase that reveals the named faction's fleet positions for 30 days. The 'investigatorTask_<talkerID>' globalVar is set to 'setGlobalVar,tracking_30d_<sValue>,1' and 'investigatorAnswer_<talkerID>' is filled with the response message. If the faction doesn't exist, only a generic fallback message is stored.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The faction ID whose fleets should be tracked.

tasks.name: investigator_setupportselect
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used as a taskString. Sets the port selection dialogOptions the investigator uses for investment information, for the dialogNode set in strings[0]. floats[0] and floats[1] specify which quadrant to select ports from (-1,-1 specifies northwest, through to 1,1 specifying southeast).

uses.description: Rebuilds a dialog node's options as a per-port list, filtered to discovered ports whose overworld position (relative to the overworld centre) lies in the quadrant indicated by floats[0]/floats[1] (sign(X) and sign(Y) of the overworld delta). Each port option lists what items/actors investing there would unlock. Includes layout sizing adjustments (NPCDialogWindowSizeY when there are many ports; PCDialogWindowSizeY when there are many options).

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The dialog node ID whose options should be rebuilt as the port-selection menu.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: Sign component for the X axis (-1 west, 0 centre, 1 east) to filter which ports appear.

required.name: floats[1]
required.description: Sign component for the Y axis (-1 north, 0 centre, 1 south) to filter which ports appear.

tasks.name: investigator_setupfleetposselect
tasks.officialDescription: Sets up the dialogNode ID 'sValue' dialogOptions for selecting which faction's fleets to track for use with an investigator.

uses.description: Rebuilds a dialog node's options as one entry per faction whose 'fleetsCanBeTracked' flag is true, each priced at $300 and gated by a money formula. Also adds a 'Can't afford' grey option (shown when the player lacks the money) and a Back option. Includes PCDialogWindowSizeY sizing adjustment when there are many options.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The dialog node ID whose options should be rebuilt as the fleet-tracking faction-selection menu.

tasks.name: setupBuyShipDialog
tasks.officialDescription: Sets up the dialogOptions for dialogNode 'sValue' for purchasing ships from the current location, based on its set shipwright_boatTypes list.

uses.description: Rebuilds the named dialog node's options as one option per ship type the current Location's shipwright sells. Each option opens a 'buy_<shipType>' sub-node with an oversized dialog window and a tooltip listing the ship's stats. A 'Goodbye' bottom option is appended. Has no effect outside a Location.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The dialog node ID whose options should be rebuilt as the ship-buying menu.

tasks.name: crash
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Crashes the game with a missing dictionary index error.

uses.description: Intentionally crashes the game by looking up a missing key in a freshly created empty dictionary. Used during development. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: buyShip
tasks.officialDescription: Adds the ship actor with the ID of 'sValue' to the player's fleet and subtracts the cost of it from their gold.

uses.description: Buys a ship for the player: deducts the ship type's value from the player's gold, clones the actorPrefab, and adds it to the player's fleet (initialising the fleet if none exists). The ship is set non-hostile, controllable, and assigned to the 'player' faction. Increments the 'shipsBought' globalVar to give the new actor a unique ID.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The actor type / actorPrefab ID of the ship to buy.

tasks.name: giveShip
tasks.officialDescription: Adds ship actorType ID 'sValue' to the player's fleet, if it can fit. Uses ID 'sValue2' for the ship actor if specified.

uses.description: Gives the player a free ship of the supplied actor type. By default the new actor's ID is uniquified using the 'shipBoons' globalVar (e.g. 'sloopboon3'); strings[1], when supplied, is used as the ID instead. The ship is non-hostile, controllable, and assigned to the 'player' faction.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The actor type / actorPrefab ID of the ship to give.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: If supplied, used as the new ship actor's ID instead of an auto-generated one.

tasks.name: renameShip
tasks.officialDescription: Shows the charEdit window for the last ship in the player's fleet.

uses.description: Opens the character edit window for the last ship in the player's fleet, with the 'cantClose' variant so the player must finish editing before closing. Typically used after a ship is added to allow renaming.

tasks.name: addToFleet
tasks.officialDescription: Adds the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) to the player's fleet. If the actor is iAmABoat, they are added to the player's fleet ships, otherwise they are added to the player's crew and landing party if there is room.

uses.description: Adds the triggering actor to the player's party (and fleet). If the actor's dialog node was 'boat_unmoor', it is cleared. The actor is then removed from the regular zone actors list (since they are now part of the party).

tasks.name: addToFleet_andSwapWithFleet_slot
tasks.officialDescription: Swaps the ship in slot 'fValue' of the player's fleet with the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog), if they are iAmABoat. Any crew on the swapped out ship will be transferred to the currentActorTalkingTo ship. The swapped out ship actor will try to be placed within the 9 tiles surrounding travel_point9 and be given the 'boat_unmoor' dialogNodeID.

uses.description: Swaps a ship in a specific player-fleet slot with the triggering actor's ship. The old ship is removed from the fleet, its crew is transferred to the new ship (the talker), and the new ship is added to the party. The old ship is repositioned near the harbor 'travel_point9' if a free occupiable tile exists, given the 'boat_unmoor' dialog node, and reconfigured as a non-controllable, non-despawnable boat. The new ship takes the old ship's slot in the fleet order.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The zero-based slot index in the player's fleet to swap with the talker's ship.

tasks.name: removeFromParty_slot
tasks.officialDescription: Removes the actor in slot 'fValue' of the player's current partyActors and adds them to the first unoccupied travelPoint set D item. Also changes their dialogNodeID to 'crew_rehire'.

uses.description: Dismisses the party actor in slot floats[0] back to a port: the actor is teleported to the first unoccupied travel_point0D in the zone, fully healed, and stripped of equipment (which is dumped into the player stash). Their dialog node is set to 'crew_rehire' so they can be rehired later, and they are removed from the party and fleet. If no free travel point is available, the dialog instead jumps to 'cafe_dismiss_noroom'.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The zero-based slot index in partyActors to dismiss.

tasks.name: researchFromFleet_slot
tasks.officialDescription: Removes the ship in slot 'fValue' of the player's fleet and adds it as an unlockable ship for the current location. Also removes the ship actorType's value from the player's gold.

uses.description: Sells a ship from the player's fleet to the current Location's shipwright for research. The ship is removed from the fleet, its cargo is dumped into the player's cargo inventory, the player's gold is reduced by the ship's value, and the location's itemType_quantity_unlockable list gains an entry for this ship type (gated on a global var). Failure modes route to specific dialog nodes ('alreadyResearching', 'alreadyResearched', or 'noroom' if the location already has 8 unlockables).

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The zero-based slot index in the player's fleet to research.

tasks.name: removeFromFleet_slot
tasks.officialDescription: Removes the ship actor in slot 'fValue' of the player's fleet and places it around travelPoint 9 in the current zone, if it can fit.

uses.description: Removes a ship from the player's fleet, placing it at an occupiable tile near the harbor 'travel_point9' in the current zone. The ship is reconfigured as 'boat_unmoor' (un-moored, idle), non-controllable, non-despawnable. If no free tile is available, the dialog jumps to 'harbor_moor_noroom' instead.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The zero-based slot index in the player's fleet to remove.

tasks.name: enterPort
tasks.officialDescription: Forces the player to enter the closest port location that is in the current zone, within 4 tiles of them.

uses.description: Finds the closest port-type Location within 4 tiles of the player and forces them to enter it. Useful for scripted 'enter the nearest port' moments.

tasks.name: enterLocation
tasks.officialDescription: Forces the player to enter the closest non-port location that is in the current zone, within 4 tiles of them.

uses.description: Finds the closest non-port Location within 4 tiles of the player and forces them to enter it. Useful for scripted 'enter the nearest landmark' moments.

tasks.name: buyLocationFood
tasks.officialDescription: Goes through the process of buying and applying the effects of the current location's cafeFood_itemTypeID to the player's crew.

uses.description: Purchases the current Location's cafe food for the entire player fleet's crew. Deducts the food's buy price (scaled by crew count) from the player's gold, modifies crew morale by the food's power, and applies the food's actor-value effects to every boat (for wind effects) and every crew member (for other effects). Records the food eaten in the 'lastFoodEaten' globalVar, places the party at travel_point set 2, and clears the party path.

tasks.name: invest
tasks.officialDescription: Acts as if the player had invested 'fValue' gold into the current location.

uses.description: Has the player 'invest' a specified amount into the current Location. If the player has a faction set in 'playerFaction', favor adjustments are applied (the current faction loses favor, the player's faction gains favor, scaled by the invest amount). Trade fame goes up, piracy fame goes down. If the location changes faction as a result, the 'alliancesMade' globalVar is incremented and a fanfare FX plays. Has no effect outside a Location.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The amount of gold to invest (the value is consumed in scaling favor/fame changes; the actual cost to the player is paid implicitly elsewhere).

tasks.name: modTalkerFavor
tasks.officialDescription: Adds 'fValue' to the player's 'favor_' faction ID globalVar matching the currentActorTalkingTo's set faction (or current task actor if not in dialog).

uses.description: Modifies the player's favor with the faction of the triggering actor by a specified amount. Has no effect if the actor doesn't exist or has no recognised faction.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The amount to add (or subtract, if negative) to the player's favor with the talker's faction.

tasks.name: modLocFavor
tasks.officialDescription: Adds 'fValue' to the player's 'favor_' faction globalVar for the current location's favored faction. Also shows associated favor icons and sounds.

uses.description: Modifies the player's favor with the current Location's favored faction by a specified amount. Triggers the standard favor change icon and sound.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The amount to add (or subtract, if negative) to the player's favor with the location's faction.

tasks.name: modLocWealthLevel
tasks.officialDescription: Adds 'fValue' to the current location's wealthLevel, which won't go below 0 or above 100. If sValue is set, adds to that location's wealthLevel instead.

uses.description: Adjusts a Location's wealth level by a specified amount (clamped to [0, 100]). By default the current Location is modified; if strings[0] names another Location, that one is modified instead. After the adjustment, the Location's unlocks are re-evaluated (without displaying the unlock notification).

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The amount to add to the location's wealthLevel (clamped to [0, 100]).

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The ID of a different Location to modify. If empty or omitted, the current Location is used.

tasks.name: setLocDiscoverable
tasks.officialDescription: Sets a location's discoverable bool to true. 'sValue' is the ID of the location. If ID is not set, uses current location instead. If 'sValue2' is set, it will be the new formula for the location's discoverable_fReq. If b1 is set and the player is in zoneThisLocationIsIn, will set discovered to true and unbury the location.

uses.description: Marks a Location as discoverable, optionally replacing its discovery requirement formula and (if the player is currently in the location's home zone) immediately revealing it. When the latter applies, the location-flagged items within 2 tiles of its position are also unburied, and a 'blockedLoE' item is added to the location's tile if it lacks one and the location is mod-tagged.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The ID of the Location to make discoverable. If empty or omitted, the current Location is used.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: A new formula string to use as the location's discoverable_fReq. If empty, the existing formula is preserved.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true and the player is currently in the location's home zone, the location is immediately discovered (visible) and its tile items are unburied.

tasks.name: createMonsterDenNearby
tasks.officialDescription: Sets up an overworld monster den that is closest to the current location using levelData 'sValue'.

uses.description: Finds the nearest existing 'monsterden'-tagged Location to the current Location, then converts it into a procedural monster den using the supplied levelData ID. The new den is registered as discoverable.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The levelData ID used to build the monster den.

tasks.name: bezoar_setupportselect
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used as a taskString. Sets the fast travel dialogOptions the Ruluorn Bezoar uses, for the dialogNode set in strings[0]. floats[0] and floats[1] specify which quadrant to select ports from (-1,-1 specifies northwest, through to 1,1 specifying southeast).

uses.description: Like investigator_setupportselect, but builds the Ruluorn Bezoar's fast-travel port menu. Filters to discovered ports (excluding port26 and the current location) whose overworld position lies in the quadrant indicated by sign(floats[0])/sign(floats[1]). Each option plays 'sfx_highestHum', triggers a travel_point to that port's zone, sets 'bezoar_gated' = 1, and resets the dialog window size. Includes Back option.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The dialog node ID whose options should be rebuilt as the Bezoar's port-selection menu.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: Sign component for the X axis (-1 west, 0 centre, 1 east) to filter which ports appear.

required.name: floats[1]
required.description: Sign component for the Y axis (-1 north, 0 centre, 1 south) to filter which ports appear.

tasks.name: cameraAtPlayer
tasks.officialDescription: Points the camera at the tile the current player actor is on.

uses.description: Snaps the camera to the current player actor's tile position.

tasks.name: cameraAtTalker
tasks.officialDescription: Points the camera at the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog).

uses.description: Snaps the camera to the triggering actor's tile position. Has no effect if there is no triggering actor.

tasks.name: cameraAtPoint
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used as a taskString. Points the camera at travel point #floats[0], set floats[1].

uses.description: Snaps the camera to a specific travel point identified by floats[0] (the travel point number) and floats[1] (the travel point set). Must be used as a taskString so the float list is preserved.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The travel-point number.

required.name: floats[1]
required.description: The travel-point set (e.g. 0 red, 1 blue, etc.).

tasks.name: cameraAtPointLock
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used as a taskString. Points the camera at travel point #floats[0], set floats[1], and locks it there until the camera is pointed at something else with the 'pointCameraAt' task.

uses.description: Like cameraAtPoint, but additionally sets cameraCantBeChangedTimer to a very large value so the camera stays locked indefinitely until something else explicitly moves it (e.g. a pointCameraAt task).

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The travel-point number.

required.name: floats[1]
required.description: The travel-point set.

tasks.name: talkToOtherActor
tasks.officialDescription: Enters dialog with the other actor that is currently on the same tile as the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog).

uses.description: Starts a dialog with another actor occupying the same tile as the triggering actor (preferring non-incapped actors, excluding the triggering actor themselves). Useful for two-NPC tile encounters where the player should switch conversation partners.

tasks.name: giveItem
tasks.officialDescription: Adds 'fValue' quantity of item ID 'sValue' to the player's cargo or stash inventory, depending on the item. If can't fit, added to held items. fValue 0 gives 1, -1 is default quantity, -2 is infinite, -11 through -19 is % chance to keep.

uses.description: Gives the player a specified quantity of a named item. The item is routed through the standard acquireItem flow, which selects cargo or stash based on the item's properties (falling back to held items if neither has room). Special quantity sentinel values are documented in the official description: 0 → 1, -1 → default quantity, -2 → infinite, -11 to -19 → percentage chance to keep. Has no effect if the item ID is unknown or the resulting quantity is 0.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The item type ID to give.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The quantity to give. Defaults to 1 if 0 or omitted. Sentinels: -1 default, -2 infinite, -11..-19 percentage-chance-to-keep.

tasks.name: triggerEffect
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE.

uses.description: Schedules a UIManager timer that fires another triggerEffect. Two invocation shapes exist: with 6+ strings, all timing/argument values are parsed from strings[0..4]; with fewer, floats[0] is the delay and strings[0] is the effect ID (with no extra arguments). Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The effect ID (when fewer than 6 strings) or the delay-as-string (when 6+ strings).

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The effect ID (when 6+ strings).

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: A numeric argument-as-string passed to the effect (when 6+ strings).

optional.name: strings[3]
optional.description: A second string argument passed to the effect (when 6+ strings).

optional.name: strings[4]
optional.description: A second numeric argument-as-string passed to the effect (when 6+ strings).

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The delay (in seconds) before the effect fires. Used when fewer than 6 strings are supplied.

tasks.name: dialogEffect
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used as a taskString. Executes strings[0] as a dialog specialEffect string after a delay of floats[1] seconds.

uses.description: Schedules a UIManager timer that, after floats[1] seconds, runs the supplied dialog specialEffect string through the dialog system. Must be invoked as a taskString so the floats list is preserved.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The dialog specialEffect string to execute after the delay.

required.name: floats[1]
required.description: The delay in seconds before the effect fires.

tasks.name: nextDialogNode_hideWindow
tasks.officialDescription: Sets nextDialogNode_hideWindow to true, which hides the next dialog window.

uses.description: Flags the input manager to hide the dialog window when the next dialog node is entered. Useful for transitioning into a UI panel that should appear in place of the dialog.

tasks.name: closeCharEdit
tasks.officialDescription: Closes the actor name/appearance editor window, if it is open.

uses.description: Closes the character edit window (used for naming and customising actors) via the input manager. Has no effect if no char-edit window is open.

tasks.name: gameOver
tasks.officialDescription: Ends the game for the player and returns them to the main menu. If the zone has cantGameOver=true, reacts all triggers in the zone with Element.gameOver.

uses.description: Triggers a game-over for the player. Internally, if the current zone has cantGameOver = true, all triggers in the zone receive an Element.gameOver reaction instead of an actual game-over.

tasks.name: autosaveASAP
tasks.officialDescription: Creates an autosave if the player is able to save.

uses.description: Flags the zone manager to perform an autosave as soon as it's safe to do so (subject to the game's normal save restrictions).

tasks.name: speechFX
tasks.officialDescription: Plays the set fxSpeech FX for the actorType of the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog).

uses.description: Plays the speech sound effect defined for the triggering actor's actorType. Used to give NPCs a per-actor 'voice' sound when they speak.

tasks.name: fxOnPlayerRank
tasks.officialDescription: Plays fx 'sValue' at the screen position where the player's rank is on the escMenu.

uses.description: Plays a named FX at the screen position of the player's rank/icon within the escMenu's right info panel. Useful for visual feedback when the player's rank changes.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the FX to play at the player rank's screen position.

tasks.name: returnMissingPartyMembers
tasks.officialDescription: Adds any partyActors that fell off of a cliff or drowned back to the player's current party.

uses.description: Recalls any party members that fell off a cliff, drowned, or otherwise went missing back to the current party. Useful as a recovery operation after dangerous terrain transitions.

tasks.name: modPlayerFactionFavor
tasks.officialDescription: Adds 'fValue' or the result of the formula set in sValue to the player's 'favor_' + 'playerFaction' globalVar, if the 'playerFaction' gVar is set.

uses.description: Modifies the player's favor with their currently-set playerFaction. If floats[0] is 0 or missing, the favor delta is computed from the formula in strings[0]; otherwise floats[0] is used directly. Has no effect if no playerFaction is set.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: A formula whose calculated value is the favor delta (used when floats[0] is 0 or missing).

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The favor delta to apply directly. Takes priority over strings[0] when non-zero.

tasks.name: modTalkerFactionFavor
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used in dialog. Adds 'fValue' to the player's 'favor_' faction ID globalVar matching the currentActorTalkingTo's set faction.

uses.description: Modifies the player's favor with the talker's faction by a specified amount. Intended for use inside dialog so the talker is well-defined.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The favor delta to apply to the talker's faction.

tasks.name: refreshStatModifiers
tasks.officialDescription: Resets all enemy stat multipliers, player stat modifiers, boat mods and day bonuses for every actor in the current zone.

uses.description: Recomputes stat modifiers for every actor in the current zone (party actors and barracks actors are NOT included; for that use refreshStatMods). Useful after a single-zone modifier change.

tasks.name: storeBoost
tasks.officialDescription: Sets playerActor_storedBoost to true, which will reapply the speed boost the player's ship may have had before being interrupted by a dialog.

uses.description: If the player was in auto-move mode (e.g. ship boost), flags playerActor_storedBoost so the boost is restored after the current dialog ends. Has no effect otherwise.

tasks.name: recoverOverboardPartyActors
tasks.officialDescription: Removes any dead ship actors from the player's fleet and moves their crew to the flagship.

uses.description: Iterates the player's fleet; any incapacitated or zero-HP boat is removed from the fleet, and every party actor previously stored as 'overboard' is added back to the flagship's crew. Used after a dangerous overworld encounter to consolidate survivors.

tasks.name: talkToCommodore
tasks.officialDescription: Enter dialog with the commodore of the fleet the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) is part of, using the currentDialogNodeID.

uses.description: Switches the conversation to the commodore of the fleet the triggering actor belongs to, keeping the current dialog node and panning the camera between speakers. Has no effect if the player is already talking to that commodore.

tasks.name: talkToFirstMate
tasks.officialDescription: Enter dialog with the actor ID set in globalVar 'firstmate', using the currentDialogNodeID.

uses.description: Switches the conversation to the actor whose ID is stored in the 'firstmate' global variable, keeping the current dialog node. Has no effect if the player is already talking to that actor.

tasks.name: talkToFlagship
tasks.officialDescription: Enter dialog with the actor that is the flagship of the fleet the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) is part of.

uses.description: Switches the conversation to the flagship of the fleet the triggering actor belongs to. If the triggering actor isn't in a fleet, opens a silent dialog instead. Special case: if the fleet's faction is unknown but the commodoreID is 'lagan', the fleet is converted into a 'pirate' battle fleet and the dialog jumps to 'dialog_fleet_aggressive_attack' on the flagship.

tasks.name: setupFleetLooting
tasks.officialDescription: Sets up the current enemyFleet's cargo inventory to also contain any assist fleet cargo and any items left on player ships, for use with 'lootFleet_afterCombat' task after fleet combat.

uses.description: Prepares post-combat looting: every still-functional enemy ship's inventory and boarding-zone items are dumped into the enemy fleet's cargo container, the assist fleet's cargo and ship items are appended too, and finally items from all player ships are added. Achievement attempts are made for defeating sea monster, merchant, battle, and elite fleets where applicable. After running, the special zone is cleared.

tasks.name: captureShips_afterCombat
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Sets up the dialogOptions for the 'sValue' dialogNode with all the ships captured during fleet combat.

uses.description: Rebuilds the named dialog node's options as one pair of entries per still-functional ship in the defeated enemy/assist fleets: a 'capture' option and a small inspect-tooltip option. Each capture option triggers captureShipNum. Adjusts the dialog window size based on option count and refreshes the player fleet window. If no ships are capturable, the dialog displays a 'No ships available' message instead. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The dialog node ID whose options should be rebuilt as the capture-selection menu.

tasks.name: captureShipNum
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Adds the ship actor in slot 'fValue' of the ships captured during fleet combat to the player's fleet, if there are any available slots.

uses.description: Captures a specific ship (indexed by floats[0]) from the still-functional enemy/assist fleet ships into the player's fleet. Only runs if the player fleet has fewer than 9 ships. The captured ship is set non-hostile, joins the 'player' faction, has its movement/crew cleared, its cloth palette swapped to the player's pirate variant, gets a TrueHPMax penalty marker, has its ID uniquified with a 'cap<N>' suffix, and is removed from its old fleet. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The zero-based index into the capturable-ships list identifying which ship to capture.

tasks.name: lootFleet_afterCombat
tasks.officialDescription: Opens the enemyFleet cargo inventory so the player can loot it.

uses.description: Opens the enemy fleet's cargo container alongside the player's cargo inventory, allowing the player to drag-loot items between them. The container UI is configured for item-mode without listMode and the dialog window is resized to fit.

tasks.name: openFleetWindow
tasks.officialDescription: Shows the player's fleet information window.

uses.description: Opens (or refreshes) the player's fleet information window in its default position.

tasks.name: openFleetWindow_delay
tasks.officialDescription: Shows the player's fleet information window after a 0.1s delay.

uses.description: Schedules a 0.1-second timer that opens the player's fleet information window (via the 'fleetWindow_lm' UI command). Useful when the window should appear shortly after another action completes.

tasks.name: openFleetWindow_talker
tasks.officialDescription: Shows the fleet information window for the fleet the currentActorTalkingTo (or current task actor if not in dialog) is part of.

uses.description: Opens the fleet information window for the fleet the triggering actor belongs to. Useful for showing an NPC's fleet composition during dialog.

tasks.name: closeFleetWindow
tasks.officialDescription: Closes the fleet information window, if it is open and the player isn't holding an actor.

uses.description: Closes the player's fleet information window, if it is currently open. Does nothing if the player is in the middle of dragging an actor.

tasks.name: showFleetInfo
tasks.officialDescription: Sets up the dialogOptions for dialogNode 'sValue' for selecting ships to inspect when inspecting the fleet the currentActorTalkingTo belongs to.

uses.description: Builds a dialog node's options as one entry per ship in the talker's fleet, each linking to a 'view_<actorTypeID>' inspect node with a stats tooltip. Adds a Done option and a '<fleetdesc=>' marker to the first statement. Also resizes the player-choices dialog window.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The dialog node ID whose options should be rebuilt as the per-ship inspection menu.

tasks.name: repairFleet_pay
tasks.officialDescription: Repairs all ships in the player's fleet and removes the repair cost from their gold.

uses.description: Repairs every ship in the player's fleet and deducts the total repair cost (with any favor-based discounts applied) from the player's gold.

tasks.name: repairFleet_free
tasks.officialDescription: Repairs all ships in the player's fleet, for free.

uses.description: Repairs every ship in the player's fleet without charging the player.

tasks.name: help
tasks.officialDescription: prints a description of the command (ex: help revive)

uses.description: Console help command. With no arguments, prints a wrapped list of all known console command names; with a command name as strings[0], prints that command's description. Output is shown via the in-game debug-string overlay.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: A console command name to look up. If omitted, lists all available commands.

tasks.name: csave
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Forces a save to be made named 'sValue', or 'consoleSave' if not specified.

uses.description: Console save command: writes a save file using the supplied name (or 'consoleSave' if none is given). Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The name of the save slot to write. Defaults to 'consoleSave' if omitted.

tasks.name: testitemload
tasks.officialDescription: puts 1 item on every tile. Default ID: toyboat.

uses.description: Console debug command: spawns one copy of an item (default 'toyboat') on every tile in the current zone. Used to stress-test item loading and rendering.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The item type ID to drop on every tile. Defaults to 'toyboat' if omitted.

tasks.name: howmanyitems
tasks.officialDescription: counts items in the zone. Counts all items if ID not specified.

uses.description: Console debug command: counts items across all tiles in the current zone (filtered by item ID if strings[0] is supplied) and prints the total via the in-game debug-string overlay.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The item type ID to count. If omitted, all items are counted.

tasks.name: convertRGB
tasks.officialDescription: loads (param).png from desktop and palettizes it (removes excess RGB so all pixels are only R, G, or B) then saves to desktop

uses.description: Console debug command: loads a PNG from the developer's desktop (named '<sValue>.png'), converts it to the game's RGB palette colour scheme, and writes the converted version back to the desktop as '<sValue>_rgb'.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The base filename (without extension) of the PNG on the desktop to convert.

tasks.name: screenshot
tasks.officialDescription: saves a .png on desktop of the screen. Can be given X Y to specify TC to screenshot at. Can be given a palette to paletteOverride the result. (ex: screenshot 20 20 p62)

uses.description: Console debug command: captures a screenshot of the game world at a specific tile coordinate and saves it to the developer's desktop. Optional sizing comes from floats[2]/floats[3] (width/height) and floats[4] (camera scale). If two strings are supplied, the second is loaded as a special-zone overlay and the screenshot is colour-swapped using strings[0]. With one string, no special zone is loaded but the colour swap still runs. With no strings, the screenshot is taken as-is. bools[0] toggles whether location flags are drawn (defaults to true).

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The X tile coordinate of the screenshot's centre.

required.name: floats[1]
required.description: The Y tile coordinate of the screenshot's centre.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The colour-swap source/target name applied to the captured screenshot.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: A special-zone ID to load as an overlay before capturing.

optional.name: floats[2]
optional.description: The width of the captured image in pixels.

optional.name: floats[3]
optional.description: The height of the captured image in pixels.

optional.name: floats[4]
optional.description: The camera scale used during capture.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true (or omitted), location flags are drawn on the screenshot.

tasks.name: screenpic
tasks.officialDescription: displays screenshot forever. Can be given X Y to specify TC to screenshot at. Can be given a palette to paletteOverride the result. (ex: screenpic 20 20 p62)

uses.description: Console debug command: captures a screenshot of the game world at a specific tile coordinate and displays it on top of the screen indefinitely as a fullscreen overlay (registered as the texture name "screenpic" in the debug draw queue). Other debug indefinite-draw and ignore-darkness textures are cleared before the new image is added. Optional sizing comes from floats[2]/floats[3] (width/height) and floats[4] (camera scale). If two strings are supplied, the second is loaded as a special-zone overlay and the captured image is colour-swapped using strings[0]. With one string, no special zone is loaded but the colour swap still runs. With no strings, the image is taken as-is. bools[0] toggles whether location flags are drawn (defaults to true).

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The X tile coordinate of the captured image's centre.

required.name: floats[1]
required.description: The Y tile coordinate of the captured image's centre.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The colour-swap source/target name applied to the captured image.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: A special-zone ID to load as an overlay before capturing.

optional.name: floats[2]
optional.description: The width of the captured image in pixels.

optional.name: floats[3]
optional.description: The height of the captured image in pixels.

optional.name: floats[4]
optional.description: The camera scale used during capture.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true (or omitted), location flags are drawn on the captured image.

tasks.name: anim
tasks.officialDescription: makes the player perform the specified animation (ex: anim crouch)

uses.description: Console debug command: plays a named animation on the current player actor, clears any debug-drawn strings, and switches the game back to travel mode. Errors out if no animation name is supplied.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The animation name to play on the player.

tasks.name: fullheal
tasks.officialDescription: Fully heals playerParty, including HPMax lost from being KO'd.

uses.description: Fully restores HP for every member of the player party, additionally clearing any HPMax penalty incurred from being knocked out. Equivalent to fullrestore.

tasks.name: testboatanim
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE.

uses.description: Console debug command: returns the game to its previous mode and triggers the boarding-special-zone timers. Used for testing boat animation behaviour. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: bighead
tasks.officialDescription: reveals the true self

uses.description: Console debug command: toggles 'big head' mode (a cosmetic visual mode). With bools[0] = true, big head mode is forcibly enabled regardless of its current state.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, big head mode is forcibly enabled (rather than toggled).

tasks.name: screenscale
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the forcedScreenScale game option to 'fValue'. Set it to -1 to disable it.

uses.description: Sets the global forcedScreenScale option to floats[0]. While not -1, the screen scale is also updated immediately to match. Set to -1 to disable the override and return to automatic scaling.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The new forced screen scale. Use -1 to disable the override.

tasks.name: res
tasks.officialDescription: Changes the game window's size to 1920x959, which is the highest resolution before the screen scale changes.

uses.description: Forces the game window into windowed 1920x959 resolution, the largest size before the integer screen scale changes.

tasks.name: go
tasks.officialDescription: transitions to specified zone (ex: go zoneName)

uses.description: Console debug command: travels to a named zone at a specific tile coordinate. If two floats are supplied, the destination tile is (floats[0], floats[1]); otherwise the destination defaults to (-99, 0) (a sentinel meaning 'use travel point 0'). The arrival is flagged as a console entry so loot/encounter rolls are skipped. Has no effect if the zone name contains a backslash (a safety check).

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the destination zone.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: Used internally during float parsing — its commas are stripped to support manual entry.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: Used internally during float parsing — its commas are stripped to support manual entry.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The X destination tile (used when two floats are supplied).

optional.name: floats[1]
optional.description: The Y destination tile (used when two floats are supplied).

tasks.name: gob
tasks.officialDescription: transitions to specified zone and enters build mode (ex: gob zoneName)

uses.description: Console debug command: travels to a named zone like 'go', then immediately enters build mode upon arrival. Useful for jumping straight into editing a zone.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the destination zone.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: Used internally during float parsing — its commas are stripped to support manual entry.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: Used internally during float parsing — its commas are stripped to support manual entry.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The X destination tile (used when two floats are supplied).

optional.name: floats[1]
optional.description: The Y destination tile (used when two floats are supplied).

tasks.name: p
tasks.officialDescription: Creates an example procedurally generated zone using 'sValue' levelData, then transfers the player to it at TC 10,10. If b1, acts as if entering via console.

uses.description: Console debug command: builds a procedural zone using the supplied levelData and transfers the player to it at tile (10, 10). The zone is named 'p_<sValue>'. By default the arrival is flagged as a console entry; setting bools[0] = false disables that flag.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The levelData ID used to generate the procedural zone.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If false, the arrival is NOT flagged as a console entry. Defaults to true if omitted.

tasks.name: world
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE.

uses.description: Console debug command: creates a procedural world from the supplied levelData and transfers the player to its '<id>0,0' starting zone at tile (12, 12), flagged as entered via console. Errors if the levelData ID isn't known.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The levelData ID used to generate the procedural world.

tasks.name: loads
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE.

uses.description: Console debug command: loads a save file by name, treating it as a system save. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The save file name to load.

tasks.name: z
tasks.officialDescription: show list of all saved zones. Returns results that start w/ given param. (ex: z house)

uses.description: Console debug command: lists all zone PNG files in Content/Data/ZoneData/ that don't belong to known levelData (and aren't sp/grove/den/bz prefixed). If strings[0] is supplied, filters to files whose names start with 'z<sValue>'. Output is shown via the in-game debug-string overlay.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: A name-prefix filter; only files starting with 'z<sValue>' are listed.

tasks.name: sp
tasks.officialDescription: show list of all saved setPieces. Returns results that start w/ given param. (ex: sp puzzle)

uses.description: Console debug command: lists all set-piece PNG files in Content/Data/ZoneData/SetPieces/ that don't belong to known levelData or 'z'-prefixed zones. If strings[0] is supplied, filters to files whose names start with 'sp<sValue>'. Output is shown via the in-game debug-string overlay.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: A name-prefix filter; only files starting with 'sp<sValue>' are listed.

tasks.name: bz
tasks.officialDescription: show list of all saved zones that start with 'bz'.

uses.description: Console debug command: lists all boarding-zone PNG files in Content/Data/ZoneData/ (those starting with 'bz'). Output is shown via the in-game debug-string overlay.

tasks.name: listfx
tasks.officialDescription: lists all FX. (scroll using mousewheel) Specify an FX to view its data.

uses.description: Console debug command: with no arguments, prints a newline-separated list of all FX IDs known to the game. With strings[0] supplied and matching a known FX, prints that FX's full data instead.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: A specific FX ID to inspect. If omitted, all FX IDs are listed.

tasks.name: listav
tasks.officialDescription: lists all ActorValues. (scroll using mousewheel) Specify an ActorValue to view its data.

uses.description: Console debug command: with no arguments, prints a comma/newline-separated list of all non-rank/XP actor-value IDs known to the game. With strings[0] supplied and matching a known AV, prints that AV's full data instead.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: A specific actor-value ID to inspect. If omitted, all non-rank AVs are listed.

tasks.name: k
tasks.officialDescription: show kills

uses.description: Console debug command: prints the player's killsOfActorType dictionary as 'type:count' pairs to the in-game debug-string overlay.

tasks.name: kill
tasks.officialDescription: kill everything but the party

uses.description: Console debug command: applies -99999 HP to every non-controllable, non-invincible, hostile actor in the current zone, except any whose ID matches one of the strings supplied. Used to clear out enemies quickly.

optional.name: strings[0+]
optional.description: Actor IDs to exclude from the kill. Any actor whose ID appears in the strings list is left untouched.

tasks.name: killall
tasks.officialDescription: Incaps all non-controllable actors in the zone that don't have the 'invincible' AV.

uses.description: Applies -99999 HP to every non-controllable, non-invincible actor in the current zone, regardless of hostility. Used for stress testing.

tasks.name: killcrew
tasks.officialDescription: kill all crew on non-player ships

uses.description: Console debug command: kills every crew member of every non-controllable ship in the zone (applying -99999 HP to each crew member) and applies the 'crewless' actor-value effect to each affected ship.

tasks.name: reviveall
tasks.officialDescription: Revives all incapacitated actors in the current zone except any actors whose ID is set in strings[], then fully heals them.

uses.description: Revives every incapacitated actor in the current zone (except those whose IDs are listed in strings) and fully heals them. The canDespawn flag is temporarily cleared during the revive.

optional.name: strings[0+]
optional.description: Actor IDs to exclude from the revive. Any actor whose ID appears in the strings list is left incapacitated.

tasks.name: win
tasks.officialDescription: win the current combat

uses.description: Console debug command: forcibly exits combat mode treating the result as a victory. Useful for skipping combat encounters during testing.

tasks.name: setk
tasks.officialDescription: sets kill count to # (ex: set antiair 1)

uses.description: Console debug command: registers a kill of a specific actor type (incrementing the player's killsOfActorType dictionary). If strings[1] is non-empty, the kill count is then overwritten with that parsed numeric value (rather than just incremented).

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The actor type ID whose kill count should be set/incremented.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: If non-empty, parsed as a float and assigned as the new kill count for this actor type.

tasks.name: set
tasks.officialDescription: sets a global variable to # (ex: set rescuedShroom 1)

uses.description: Console debug command: sets a named global variable to a value. The variable defaults to '1' if no value is supplied. With strings[1] non-empty (joined with extra strings as space-separated text), that string is the assigned value; otherwise floats[0] is used. After the assignment, the royal-quest state is refreshed.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The global variable name to write.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The string value to assign. If empty and floats[0] is present, the numeric form is used.

optional.name: strings[2+]
optional.description: Additional words appended to strings[1] (space-separated) to form a multi-word value.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The numeric value to assign when strings[1] is empty.

tasks.name: get
tasks.officialDescription: prints value of global variable - or prints flagged triggers of given zoneName (ex: get zTown1)

uses.description: Console debug command: looks up a name and prints its value via the in-game debug-string overlay. If the name matches a global variable, that variable's value is shown; if it matches a triggersFlaggedAsDone key, the comma-separated trigger IDs are shown; otherwise the name is evaluated as a formula (with the player as context) and the result is shown. With no string supplied, dumps every non-debug global variable.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The name to look up (globalVar / trigger zone key / formula). If omitted, all globalVars are dumped.

tasks.name: formulas
tasks.officialDescription: lists out all globalFormulas

uses.description: Console debug command: prints every global formula's name and formula string via the in-game debug-string overlay.

tasks.name: sanitizecheck
tasks.officialDescription: shows what the provided string would look like when formatted for formula checks.

uses.description: Console debug command: takes the supplied strings, joins them (space-separated in console mode, comma-separated otherwise), and prints the result after running them through the standard formula-comparison sanitizer.

optional.name: strings[0+]
optional.description: Any number of strings to be joined and sanitised.

uses.description: Console debug command: parses the last console command (its arguments treated as a Task taskString) and prints out the parsed task's type, delay, fReq, strings, floats, bools, and tileCoords for inspection. Errors out if not run from the console.

tasks.name: calc
tasks.officialDescription: prints result of specified formula (ex: math c:PhysAtk)

uses.description: Console debug command: evaluates a formula with the player as actor context, then prints the result via the in-game debug-string overlay.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The formula string to evaluate and print.

tasks.name: resetfog
tasks.officialDescription: sets entire zone's fog of war to unknown

uses.description: Resets every tile in the current zone's fog-of-war to 'unknown' (re-fogged). Useful for testing exploration mechanics.

tasks.name: clearfog
tasks.officialDescription: sets entire zone's fog of war to known

uses.description: Marks every tile in the current zone's fog-of-war as 'known' (fully revealed). If the current zone is 'eral', also marks every discoverable Location there as discovered. Forces a full minimap refresh.

tasks.name: hidemapicons
tasks.officialDescription: If the player is currently in the overworld zone, sets all locations to discovered = false.

uses.description: In the 'eral' overworld zone, marks every Location whose zoneThisLocationIsIn matches the current zone as not discovered, hiding their map icons. Has no effect outside the overworld.

tasks.name: removeitems
tasks.officialDescription: removes all items (or all items of the ID specified in sValue) from the zone.

uses.description: Iterates every tile in the current zone and removes items. If strings[0] is supplied, only items with that ID are removed; otherwise every item on every tile is removed.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: If supplied, only items with this ID are removed. If omitted, all items are removed.

tasks.name: clearroofs
tasks.officialDescription: removes all roof items

uses.description: Iterates every tile in the current zone and removes every item with the 'roof' specialProperty. Useful for unobstructing the view of an interior zone during testing.

tasks.name: clearactors
tasks.officialDescription: Removes all non-party actors from the current zone. If sValue is set, it should be an fReq that will get checked for each actor.

uses.description: Removes every actor in the current zone that is not part of the player party. With strings[0] supplied, that string is treated as an fReq formula evaluated per-actor; an actor is removed only if the formula's value is > 0.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: An fReq formula evaluated per-actor. If empty, all non-party actors are removed unconditionally.

tasks.name: deleteZone
tasks.officialDescription: permanently deletes original zone data. Do not use.

uses.description: Console-only debug command: permanently deletes a zone's data file. Only runs while in console mode. Prints a confirmation message.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The zone ID whose data file should be permanently deleted.

tasks.name: forget
tasks.officialDescription: clears saved memory of specified zone.

uses.description: Forgets a previously-saved zone in the current save (so it will be regenerated on next entry). Runs in both console and non-console modes; in console mode, it prints feedback about whether the zone existed.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The zone ID whose saved data should be forgotten from the current save.

tasks.name: resetlocation
tasks.officialDescription: Resets location ID 'sValue' or all ports/locations to their default data if not specified.

uses.description: Resets a specific Location to its default data. Useful for clearing per-location runtime state during testing.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the Location to reset to its default data.

tasks.name: resetlocations
tasks.officialDescription: Resets all ports/locations to their default data.

uses.description: Resets every port/location to its default data. Equivalent to resetLocations.

tasks.name: refreshloc
tasks.officialDescription: Loads any missing locations and adds their locationItems to the zoneThisLocationIsIn.

uses.description: Forces the current game state to add any missing Locations (and their location items) into the appropriate zones. Has no effect when there is no current zone or the zone is 'zMainMenu'.

tasks.name: resetprocgen
tasks.officialDescription: Recreates all procedurally generated groves in the overworld.

uses.description: Regenerates every procedurally generated Location in the overworld (e.g. groves), resetting their procedurally rolled contents.

tasks.name: resetwaypoints
tasks.officialDescription: re-initializes waypoints for the current zone.

uses.description: Forces an immediate refresh of the AI waypoint graph for the current zone. Equivalent to initWaypoints.

tasks.name: build
tasks.officialDescription: force build mode (such as during combat)

uses.description: Switches the game into build mode, even if it isn't normally available in the current context (such as during combat).

tasks.name: overseer
tasks.officialDescription: Forces overseerMode on.

uses.description: Switches the game into overseer mode, a free-camera/observer mode used for development.

tasks.name: godmode
tasks.officialDescription: Maxes out the player's HP/MP and other stats and learns all actions from every selectable class.

uses.description: Buffs the player to god-mode: massively raises TrueHPMax/HPMax/HP, TrueMPMax/MPMax/MP, PhysDef, and MagDef. Also grants rank 9 in every action of every selectable class. Has no effect if there is no current player.

tasks.name: abil
tasks.officialDescription: learn all abilities (up to rank #)

uses.description: Console debug command: grants the player additional rank in every action of every selectable class. Defaults to +9 per action if no float is supplied; otherwise the floored value of floats[0] is granted per action.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The rank amount to add to each action. Defaults to 9 if omitted.

tasks.name: xp
tasks.officialDescription: add # XP in current class

uses.description: Console debug command: awards XP to every party actor. Defaults to 9999 XP each if no float is supplied; otherwise the floored value of floats[0] is awarded.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The amount of XP to award each party member. Defaults to 9999 if omitted.

tasks.name: crewxp
tasks.officialDescription: Gives 'fValue' XP to all of the player's crew members.

uses.description: Awards a specified amount of XP to every crew member across the player's fleet (via the ZoneManager allCrew_awardXP helper).

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The amount of XP to award each crew member.

tasks.name: allxp
tasks.officialDescription: add # XP in all classes

uses.description: Awards XP to every party actor in every selectable class. Defaults to 9999 XP per class per actor if no float is supplied; otherwise floats[0] is used.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The amount of XP to award per class per actor. Defaults to 9999 if omitted.

tasks.name: xpcosts
tasks.officialDescription: lists total XP required to master each class (not including passives)

uses.description: Console debug command: for every selectable class with at least one action, sums the XPCost of every level of every action and prints the per-class total via the in-game debug-string overlay. Useful for designing/auditing class progression.

tasks.name: testskillcost
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE.

uses.description: Console debug command: prints a 12-entry XP cost curve for a hypothetical skill, given base/increment/multiplier values in floats[0]/floats[1]/floats[2]. The cost for level n is (base + n * increment) * mult^n. If fewer than 3 floats are provided, prints an instruction message.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The base XP cost (level 0).

optional.name: floats[1]
optional.description: The XP cost increment per level (linear component).

optional.name: floats[2]
optional.description: The XP cost multiplier per level (geometric component).

tasks.name: allclass
tasks.officialDescription: unlocks all classes

uses.description: Console debug command: unlocks every selectable class. For each class, sets either its 'class_<id>' globalVar to 1 (or the appropriate globalVar from its fReq formula) and adds it to knownClasses. Sets the Globals.unlockAllClasses flag to true and refreshes class info.

tasks.name: gp
tasks.officialDescription: adds # GP (or gives 500 if unspecified)

uses.description: Console debug command: gives the player gold. Defaults to 500 if no float is supplied; otherwise floats[0] is used.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The amount of gold to add. Defaults to 500 if omitted.

tasks.name: tfl
tasks.officialDescription: toggles 60 fps limit

uses.description: Toggles the game's 60 FPS frame limit on/off. The change takes effect on the next frame.

tasks.name: tm
tasks.officialDescription: toggles menus & many other elements

uses.description: Console debug command: toggles the game's hideMenus flag, which globally suppresses menu drawing.

tasks.name: tt
tasks.officialDescription: toggles tooltips

uses.description: Console debug command: toggles the global drawTooltips flag, enabling or disabling tooltips throughout the UI.

tasks.name: tdm
tasks.officialDescription: toggles damage numbers

uses.description: Console debug command: toggles the global drawDamageNumbers flag, enabling or disabling floating damage number rendering.

tasks.name: tq
tasks.officialDescription: toggles quickslots & charstats

uses.description: Console debug command: toggles a heavy 'really hide quickslots/charstats' flag, suppressing the quickslots and character stats UI more aggressively than the standard hide flag.

tasks.name: tc
tasks.officialDescription: toggles being able to enter combat

uses.description: Console debug command: toggles the Globals.enableCombatMode flag, which globally enables or disables combat. Prints the new state via the in-game debug-string overlay.

tasks.name: tcui
tasks.officialDescription: toggles combat ui

uses.description: Console debug command: toggles the input manager's heavy 'hide combat UI all the time' flag, suppressing the combat UI even when in combat.

tasks.name: tw
tasks.officialDescription: toggles wires

uses.description: Console debug command: toggles the camera's drawWires flag, enabling or disabling on-screen wireframe overlays.

tasks.name: realtime
tasks.officialDescription: toggles realtime mode (probably very buggy)

uses.description: Console debug command: toggles the Globals.realtimeMode flag, switching the game between turn-based and realtime simulation modes.

tasks.name: hideparty
tasks.officialDescription: hides the party forever

uses.description: Sets every party actor's invisibleTimer to 99999 seconds and their visible flag to false, effectively hiding them indefinitely. Useful for cutscenes that should focus on something other than the party.

tasks.name: mpos
tasks.officialDescription: toggle show mouse screen (not world) coordinates.

uses.description: Console debug command: toggles the input manager's debug_showMousePos flag, enabling or disabling on-screen display of the current mouse position.

tasks.name: rankstyle
tasks.officialDescription: Toggles class action rank's notch style on/off. Does not get saved.

uses.description: Toggles the Globals.USE_NOTCHES_FOR_RANKS flag, switching the visual representation of class action ranks between number- and notch-style. This is a runtime-only change; it is not persisted.
Console debug command: clears all known journal entries and then re-learns only the default starting entries. Useful for resetting journal state during testing.
Console debug command: adds every known journal entry to the player's knownJournalEntries list and re-sorts them by category.

tasks.name: debug
tasks.officialDescription: Toggles debugMode on/off.

uses.description: Toggles the global debugMode flag and prints the new state via the in-game debug-string overlay.

tasks.name: debugMessage
tasks.officialDescription: Displays a debug message using 'sValue' as the type and 'sValue2' as the message.

uses.description: Displays a debug message via the in-game debug-string overlay. If two or more strings are supplied, strings[0] is used as the 'type' label and strings[1] is the message body; with only one string, the message is shown with a generic 'Debug' label.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The debug message body, or the type label when strings[1] is supplied.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: If supplied, used as the message body (with strings[0] becoming the type label).

tasks.name: errors
tasks.officialDescription: Toggles crash error reporting on/off.

uses.description: Toggles the Globals.enableErrorMessages flag, enabling or disabling on-screen error messages. Prints the new state via the in-game debug-string overlay.

tasks.name: resize
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used as a taskString. Changes the game window's size to floats[0] pixels by floats[1] pixels.

uses.description: Resizes the game window. Sets pending displayWidth/displayHeight values that take effect next frame. Must be invoked as a taskString so the floats list is preserved.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The new display width in pixels.

required.name: floats[1]
required.description: The new display height in pixels.

tasks.name: setBPC
tasks.officialDescription: set [BodyStructure]'s [BodyPart] connection position, of the player actor's facingRot, to [X][Y]. (ex: setBPC human armL 3 4

uses.description: Console debug command for body-part-connection editing. Calls addData on the named body structure's body part connection, using the player's nearest-90 facing rotation and the X,Y offset from floats[0]/floats[1].

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The body structure ID to modify.

required.name: strings[1]
