required.description: The body part connection ID within that structure.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The X offset to apply to the connection.

required.name: floats[1]
required.description: The Y offset to apply to the connection.

tasks.name: setBP
tasks.officialDescription: set [BodyStructure]'s [BodyPart] origin position to [X][Y]. (ex: setBP human armL 3 4

uses.description: Console debug command for body-part origin editing. Sets a specific body part's origin Vector2 to (floats[0], floats[1]) on the named body structure.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The body structure ID to modify.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The body part ID within that structure.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The X coordinate for the body part's origin.

required.name: floats[1]
required.description: The Y coordinate for the body part's origin.

tasks.name: time
tasks.officialDescription: advance world time by # hours (does not consume food or affect morale).

uses.description: Advances the in-game worldTime by floats[0] (typically in hours) and refreshes the travel info window so the new time is visible.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The amount of in-game time to add to worldTime.

tasks.name: wind
tasks.officialDescription: sets the wind direction to # degrees (0 = East)

uses.description: Sets the global wind direction (windRot) to a specified rotation. Affects sailing and weather visuals.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The new wind rotation in degrees.

tasks.name: weather
tasks.officialDescription: sets weather (none, rain, snow, storm)

uses.description: Sets the current weather to a named WeatherType enum value (e.g. 'clear', 'rain', 'storm'). The new weather takes effect immediately.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The WeatherType enum name to switch to.

tasks.name: resetachievements
tasks.officialDescription: Resets all Horizon's Gate achievements for the current player's Steam account.

uses.description: Resets every Steam achievement (and all stats) for the current Steam user. Has no effect if the Steam API isn't initialised.

tasks.name: howmany
tasks.officialDescription: Displays a debug message with how many items are in the current zone. Only counts items with ID 'sValue' if set.

uses.description: Counts items across all tiles in the current zone (filtered by item ID if strings[0] is supplied) plus actors of the matching actor type, and prints the total via the in-game debug-string overlay.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The item/actor type ID to count. If omitted, all items are counted (and no actors match).

tasks.name: howmanyWorld
tasks.officialDescription: Displays a debug message with how many times 'sValue' appears in each zone file in the ZoneData folder. If mouse is pointing at an item or actor, checks for that ID.

uses.description: Console debug command: scans every zone file in ZoneData/ for the literal substring strings[0] and prints per-zone counts (plus a total). If no string is supplied, the mouse-pointed item or actor's ID is used instead: for actors, the substring is 'actorTypeID=<id>'; for items, it's 'Item] ID=<id>'.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The substring to search for in zone files. If omitted, uses the item/actor under the mouse.

tasks.name: setMainMenuUIVisible
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Sets the main menu visibility to b1. If b2, force-draws main menu BG and UIManager windows.

uses.description: Sets the main menu's visibility (true to show, false to hide). With bools[1] = true, also forces the main menu background and UIManager windows to be drawn (a 'really show everything' override). Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the main menu is shown; if false or omitted, it is hidden.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, additionally forces drawMainMenuBG and drawUIManager to true.

tasks.name: markTrainerLocDone
tasks.officialDescription: Changes the 'sign_icon_book' or 'sign_icon_2book' icon to its '_done' or '_done_done' version in the current location's tooltipIcons, if they exist.

uses.description: Iterates the current Location's tooltipIcons and progresses any 'sign_icon_book' / 'sign_icon_book_done' / 'sign_icon_2book' / 'sign_icon_2book_done' icons to the next 'done' stage. Used to update visual markers after the player completes training. Has no effect outside a Location.

tasks.name: markDojoLocDone
tasks.officialDescription: Changes the 'sign_icon_muscle' icon to 'sign_icon_muscle_done' in the current location's tooltipIcons, if it exists.

uses.description: Iterates the current Location's tooltipIcons and progresses any 'sign_icon_muscle' icon to 'sign_icon_muscle_done'. Used to update visual markers after the player completes dojo training. Has no effect outside a Location.

tasks.name: createTreasureMap
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used as a taskString. Creates the texture 'treasureMap' for use with salvage quests. The texture is created for overworld X,Y set in floats[0], floats[1].

uses.description: Generates a 'treasureMap' texture for salvage quests: loads the 'eral' overworld as a special zone, clears hazards/loot/lightning, takes a screenshot centred on the target tile, applies a colour-swap and frame overlay, and writes the result to Content/SystemSaves/current/treasureMap.png. Also stores the target coordinates in 'treasureTCX' / 'treasureTCY' globalVars for later use by addTreasuresToZone.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The X tile coordinate (in overworld coordinates) at which the treasure map is centred.

required.name: floats[1]
required.description: The Y tile coordinate (in overworld coordinates) at which the treasure map is centred.

tasks.name: tMapTest
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE.

uses.description: Console debug command: rapidly captures screenshots of the 'eral' overworld in a tiling pattern (defaulting to start at (100, 100), advancing 20 tiles X then Y), self-rescheduling every 0.1 seconds. Used to test treasure-map screenshot generation. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The starting X tile coordinate. If two floats are supplied, the test continues from (floats[0], floats[1]).

optional.name: floats[1]
optional.description: The starting Y tile coordinate.

tasks.name: addTreasuresToZone
tasks.officialDescription: Adds a buried location_shipwreck item, with loot added to it, to the current zone at the X,Y set in gVar 'treasureTCX' and 'treasureTCY'. Fails if the gVars aren't set.

uses.description: Places a buried 'location_shipwreck' container into the current zone at the tile stored in the 'treasureTCX'/'treasureTCY' globalVars, pre-filled with random gold, cargo_lost, two rolled loot items, and a lost_docs entry. Clears the globalVars after placing so a future call won't re-place. Has no effect if treasureTCX is 0.

tasks.name: replaceTriggerWithZoneSeries
tasks.officialDescription: Replaces this trigger with a zone loaded from sValue ID + the value of sValue gVar. sValue2 specifies the levelData to use for replacing terrain when applying terrain replacer items.

uses.description: Loads a zone named strings[0]+<value of globalVar strings[0]> as a special-zone overlay, applies terrain replacers/item removers (optionally driven by levelData strings[1]), and merges it into the current zone at the calling trigger's area. Increments globalVar strings[0] so the next call advances the series. Refreshes zone tile sprites and clears the special zone.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: Both the base zone ID prefix and the global variable name whose value is appended to form the actual zone ID.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The levelData to use when applying terrain replacer items. If empty, terrain replacers run without levelData context.

tasks.name: applyTerrainReplacersAndItemRemovers
tasks.officialDescription: Item and terrain replacer items in the zone are calculated and apply their results. sValue specifies the levelData to use for replacing terrain. If b1, don't update tile sprites.

uses.description: Applies terrain-replacer and item-remover items across the entire current zone, optionally driven by levelData strings[0]. Tile sprite IDs are refreshed afterwards unless bools[0] = true.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The levelData ID to use when applying terrain replacer items. If empty, no levelData context is supplied.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, suppresses the tile-sprite refresh after replacement.

tasks.name: setupPartyFromGVars
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used as a taskString. Sets the player's current party to consist of actors with IDs specified in each strings[] entry as a globalVar, which could also be the slot number of the the actor in the current partyActors list. An example: setupPartyFromGVars,gVar1,gVar2,gVar3,aFourthGVar,andSoOn

uses.description: Builds a new party from a list of global variable names. Each entry in strings is looked up in globalVars: if the value parses as an integer, the existing partyActor at that slot is used; otherwise the value is treated as an actor ID. The current party is cleared and replaced with the resolved actors in order.

optional.name: strings[0+]
optional.description: Any number of global variable names whose values identify the new party members (either as a slot index in the current partyActors list or as an actor ID).

tasks.name: healfaction
tasks.officialDescription: Heals all actors in the current zone with the faction 'sValue'. Does not restore HPMax lost from being KO'd.

uses.description: Fully heals (current HP only, not HPMax) every actor in the current zone whose faction matches strings[0].

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The faction whose actors should be healed.

tasks.name: removeTempPartyActors
tasks.officialDescription: Removes all actors from the zone that are in the player's current partyActors and have canControlFullyIfControllable = false.

uses.description: Removes every party actor (also from missing/overboard lists) whose canControlFullyIfControllable flag is false — the marker used for temporary party members added via addTalkerToParty_temp. The actors are removed from the zone and dropped from the party.

tasks.name: removeExcessLandingPartyActors
tasks.officialDescription: Removes any actors from the player's partyActors that aren't in their landingParty.

uses.description: Removes every party actor whose ID isn't currently in the landing party list. Used to reconcile the visible party with the landing party (e.g. after returning from an away mission).

tasks.name: getItemsFromZone
tasks.officialDescription: removes all items that can be picked up from sValue zone and places them at XY

uses.description: Loads the named zone as a special-zone overlay, removes every pickup-able item from it, and places all those items at the supplied tile coordinate in the current zone. The special zone is cleared afterwards. Has no effect if the source zone doesn't exist in the current save data.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The zone ID to pull items from.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The tile in the current zone where the harvested items should be deposited.

tasks.name: location_tryUnlockItems
tasks.officialDescription: Checks for any unlockable items in the current location and displays a message if anything was unlocked.

uses.description: Runs the current Location's tryUnlockItems routine, which checks each unlockable item against its requirements and surfaces a message if anything was unlocked.

tasks.name: resetFleetAI
tasks.officialDescription: Resets the current travel path for every AI fleet.

uses.description: Clears the current waypoint target, path, and AI vars on every AI-controlled fleet's flagship, forcing them to re-evaluate their travel destinations.

tasks.name: adjustFleetAlliegances
tasks.officialDescription: If the player is in a fleet battle, this turns all actors in the zone that are the same faction as the enemy fleet hostile, clears the playerEscort gVar and plays FX on the changed actors.

uses.description: When fleet combat is active, finds every actor in the zone whose faction matches the enemy fleet and whose combatLabel is 'E', flips them to hostile (with the enemy combat label color), plays an 'exclaim_red' FX on each, and clears the 'playerEscort' globalVar. Has no effect if there is no current enemy fleet.

tasks.name: openJournal
tasks.officialDescription: Opens the journal and flips to entry number 'fValue'. If b1, hides the journal's arrows and close buttons.

uses.description: Opens the journal window and pages to the entry index floats[0]. With bools[0] = true, the navigation arrows and close button are hidden, leaving the journal in a 'view-only' state. If the journal is already visible, it's animated forward first.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The journal entry index to display. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the journal's navigation buttons and close button are hidden.

tasks.name: openJournal_interactive
tasks.officialDescription: Opens the journal and flips to entry number 'fValue'.

uses.description: Opens the journal window and pages to the entry index floats[0], with full interactive controls (unlike openJournal which can hide them).

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The journal entry index to display. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: closeJournal
tasks.officialDescription: Closes the journal window, if it is open.

uses.description: Refreshes the journal window's state and hides it. Has no effect if the journal isn't currently open.

tasks.name: learnJournalEntry
tasks.officialDescription: Adds the journalEntry ID in sValue to the known journals list. If b1, play a sound. If b2, don't open journal to show the entry.

uses.description: Adds a journal entry to the player's known list. With bools[0] = true, a sound effect plays. Unless bools[1] = true, the journal is then opened to show the newly-learned entry.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The journal entry ID to learn.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, plays a sound effect when the entry is learned.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, suppresses opening the journal to show the new entry.

tasks.name: moveJournal
tasks.officialDescription: Moves the journalWindow to screen position X,Y at speed 'fValue'. If b1, X position is offset from screen center. If b2, moves journalWindow to screen center.

uses.description: Tweens the journal window to a new screen position over floats[0] seconds. With bools[0] = true, the destination is mirrored from the right edge (X is the offset from the right of the screen, then accounting for the journal's width). With bools[1] = true, the destination is overridden to the screen centre, regardless of other inputs.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: Duration of the move tween, in seconds.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The destination screen X,Y. Interpretation depends on the bools.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, X is offset from the right edge of the screen (after accounting for window width).

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the destination is forced to the screen centre.

tasks.name: addJournalGoal
tasks.officialDescription: Adds 'sValue' as a journal goal to the first available 'goal' gVar, then opens the journal and plays a new goal FX on the goal text. If b1, journal isn't opened and the new goal FX doesn't play.

uses.description: Assigns a goal string to the first empty 'goal<N>' globalVar (out of 54 slots), preferring earlier slots and re-using completed slots when the active slots are full. Any additional strings are joined onto the first with commas to form a multi-part goal. If the same goal is already present (after sanitization), nothing happens. Unless bools[0] = true, the journal is opened to the new entry's page and a 'wideThinFlash' FX plus positive sounds play.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The first part of the goal string.

optional.name: strings[1+]
optional.description: Additional parts of the goal, joined onto strings[0] with commas to form a multi-part goal.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the journal is NOT opened and the new-goal FX is suppressed.

tasks.name: completeJournalGoal
tasks.officialDescription: Completes the journal goal found with the same text as set in 'sValue'. This will open the journal, put a line through the goal, play FX and award 50 XP to all player crew. If b1, hides all interactive buttons (used for credits). If b2, doesn't give XP, open journal or play completion FX. The journal goal gVars will be stripped of spaces and special chars when checked against sValue. The check will be done in a case-insensitive manner.

uses.description: Marks an active journal goal as completed (sets its 'Done' globalVar to 'longLine'). Goals are looked up by sanitized, case-insensitive text match. By default the journal is opened and the goal is animated with a flash FX + sound, every crew member is awarded 50 XP, and a short interaction lockout is applied. bools[0] hides interactive buttons (used during credits). bools[1] suppresses the XP/FX/journal-open entirely.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The first part of the goal string to look up.

optional.name: strings[1+]
optional.description: Additional parts of the goal, joined onto strings[0] with commas to form the lookup key.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, journal navigation buttons are hidden when the journal is opened.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, no XP is granted, the journal is not opened, and no completion FX play.

tasks.name: removeJournalGoal
tasks.officialDescription: Removes the first journal goal that matches the string in 'sValue' from the list of journal 'goal' gvars, then reassigns all 'goal' gVars to be in order with no gaps. If sValue is blank, just reassigns journal goals. The journal goal gVars will be stripped of spaces and special chars when checked against sValue. The check will be done in a case-insensitive manner.

uses.description: Removes a journal goal by sanitized, case-insensitive text match (clearing its slot), then rearranges the remaining goals to fill any gaps. If strings[0] is empty, the rearrangement runs without any specific removal. Closes the journal if it was visible.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The first part of the goal string to remove.

optional.name: strings[1+]
optional.description: Additional parts of the goal, joined onto strings[0] with commas to form the lookup key.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toJournalGoalVar
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the gVar name for the first journal 'goal#' gVar that matches 'sValue2', or a blank string if that journal goal doesn't exist. If b1, will set the gVar in sValue to the 'goal#Done' gVar name instead. The journal goal gVars will be stripped of spaces and special chars when checked against sValue2. The check will be done in a case-insensitive manner.

uses.description: Locates a journal goal by sanitized, case-insensitive text match against strings[1] and writes the matching 'goal<N>' globalVar name into a destination global variable. With bools[0] = true, writes the 'goal<N>Done' name instead. Writes an empty string if no goal matches.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name to write the located goal var name into.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The goal text to look up (sanitised, case-insensitive). Defaults to empty if omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, writes the 'goal<N>Done' var name instead of 'goal<N>'.

tasks.name: setTerrainType_spriteOffsetMult
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the spriteXOffsetMult and spriteYOffsetMult of terrainType 'sValue' to X and Y.

uses.description: Sets a specific terrainType's spriteXOffsetMult and spriteYOffsetMult to the X and Y of the supplied tile coordinate. Affects how that terrain's sprite tiling repeats.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The terrainType ID to modify.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The X,Y multipliers to assign to the terrain's sprite offsets.

tasks.name: setTerrainType_spriteOffsetModulo
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the spriteXOffsetModulo and spriteYOffsetModulo of terrainType 'sValue' to X and Y.

uses.description: Sets a specific terrainType's spriteXOffsetModulo and spriteYOffsetModulo to the X and Y of the supplied tile coordinate. Affects how that terrain's sprite tiling wraps.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The terrainType ID to modify.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The X,Y modulo values to assign to the terrain's sprite offsets.

tasks.name: setTerrainTypes_spriteOffsetModulo
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the spriteXOffsetModulo and spriteYOffsetModulo of any terrainTypes whose ID starts with 'sValue', to X and Y.

uses.description: Iterates every terrainType whose ID starts with the supplied prefix and sets its spriteXOffsetModulo and spriteYOffsetModulo to the X and Y of the supplied tile coordinate. Useful for batch-updating a family of related terrains.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The terrainType ID prefix; every matching terrainType is modified.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The X,Y modulo values to assign to each matching terrain's sprite offsets.

tasks.name: discoverFog
tasks.officialDescription: Reveals a small fogOfWar area at X,Y.

uses.description: Reveals a small area of fog-of-war around a tile coordinate from the triggering actor's perspective. The discovery routine is called four times in a row to ensure full reveal (the routine reveals progressively).

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The tile around which fog-of-war should be revealed.

tasks.name: setFog
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the fogOfWar known state of all tiles in a square radius of 'fValue' from X,Y to bValue1.

uses.description: Sets the fog-of-war 'known' state of every tile within a square radius around the supplied coordinate. By default the target zone is the current one; strings[0] can name a different zone. bools[0] is the new known state (true = revealed, false = re-fogged). The radius (in tiles) comes from floats[0].

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The zone ID whose fog-of-war should be modified. If empty, the current zone is used.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The square radius (in tiles) around the supplied tile to affect. Defaults to 0 (single tile).

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: The new fog-of-war known state. True = revealed, false = re-fogged. Defaults to false.

optional.name: tileCoords[0]
optional.description: The centre tile of the affected area.

tasks.name: resetAllTileReactTimers
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the lastTimeReacted and delayTime to 0 for every tile in the current zone.

uses.description: Resets the reaction timer state on every tile in the current zone, so any pending or throttled tile reactions can fire immediately.

tasks.name: changePlayerActor
tasks.officialDescription: Changes the current player party leader to actor ID 'sValue'.

uses.description: Switches the current player-controlled actor (party leader) to a different party member named by strings[0]. The party order is preserved (the previous leader is not pushed to the back).

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The ID of the party actor that should become the new player leader.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarToActorProperty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to an actor's property, set in sValue2 as 'actorID,property'.

uses.description: Looks up an actor by ID and reads a named property on that actor via reflective data access, storing the string representation into a named global variable. The actor ID and property name can be supplied combined as 'actorID,property' in strings[1], or split as strings[1]/strings[2]. Writes empty if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the destination global variable.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: Either the actor ID alone or the combined 'actorID,property' string.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The property name to read. Used when strings[1] doesn't contain a comma.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarToPlayerFleetShipProperty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the gVar in sValue to the actor property specified in sValue2, of the player fleet's ship actor in slot fValue. If b1, the property is pulled from the actor's actorType instead of the actor.

uses.description: Reads a named property from a specific slot of the player fleet's ships and stores the string representation into a named global variable. With bools[0] = true, the lookup is performed against the actor's actorType definition instead of the actor instance. Writes empty if the slot is out of range or empty.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The name of the property on the ship actor (or actorType) to read.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The zero-based slot index in the player fleet's ships.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, reads the property from the actorType definition rather than the actor instance.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toActorMostUsedActionID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets the gVar in sValue to the action ID of the 'fValue'-th most-used action of the player actor whose ID is 'sValue2'. fValue = 0 would be most used, 1 would be second most used, etc. If b1, only selects actions that are part of an actor class. If b2, only selects actions NOT part of an actor class.

uses.description: Sorts a named actor's recorded actions_timesUsed in descending order and writes the Nth entry's action ID into a destination global variable. With bools[0] = true, only actions with non-zero XPCost (i.e., class-rooted) are considered; with bools[1] = true, only actions without XPCost are considered. Writes empty if the actor doesn't exist or no qualifying action is at the requested rank.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The ID of the actor whose action usage history is inspected. If empty, the lookup fails and empty is written.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The zero-based rank in the descending usage list (0 = most used).

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, restricts to class-rooted actions (those with non-zero XPCost).

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, restricts to non-class actions (those with zero XPCost).

tasks.name: learnJournalEntry_silent
tasks.officialDescription: Adds the journalEntry ID in sValue to the known journals list without opening the journal or playing any FX. If b1, also adds it to the reported journals list without giving any reward.

uses.description: Adds a journal entry to the player's known list without opening the journal or playing FX/sounds. With bools[0] = true, also marks the entry as already reported (sold to a researcher), so it won't be eligible for rewards via research_report.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The journal entry ID to learn silently.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the entry is also added to the journalEntries_sold list (marked as already reported).

tasks.name: unlearnJournalEntry
tasks.officialDescription: Removes the journalEntry ID in sValue from the known journals and reported journals list. If b1, doesn't remove from reported journal list.

uses.description: Removes a journal entry from the player's known list (and resorts the remaining entries by category). The default-known entries are protected and cannot be unlearned. By default the entry is also removed from the journalEntries_sold (reported) list; bools[0] = true keeps the reported flag intact.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The journal entry ID to remove.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the entry is NOT removed from the journalEntries_sold list.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarToActorTypeProperty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to a loaded actorType property, set in sValue2 as 'actorTypeID,property'. If 'actorTypeID' is the ID of an actor in the zone, their actorTypeID is used.

uses.description: Reads a named property from an actorType definition and stores it into a named global variable. If the supplied ID names an actor in the zone, that actor's actorTypeID is used instead. The actorType ID and property can be supplied combined as 'actorTypeID,property' in strings[1] or split as strings[1]/strings[2]. Writes empty if the actorType isn't found.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: Either the actorType ID, an actor ID (whose actorTypeID is used), or the combined 'actorTypeID,property' string.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The property name to read. Used when strings[1] doesn't contain a comma.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarToItemTypeProperty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to a loaded itemType property, set in sValue2 as 'itemTypeID,property'.

uses.description: Reads a named property from an itemType definition and stores it into a named global variable. The itemType ID and property can be supplied combined as 'itemTypeID,property' in strings[1] or split as strings[1]/strings[2]. Writes empty if the itemType isn't found.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: Either the itemType ID alone or the combined 'itemTypeID,property' string.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The property name to read. Used when strings[1] doesn't contain a comma.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarToLocationProperty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to a loaded location property, set in sValue2 as 'locationID,property'.

uses.description: Reads a named property from a Location and stores it into a named global variable. The location ID and property can be supplied combined as 'locationID,property' in strings[1] or split as strings[1]/strings[2]. Writes empty if the Location isn't found.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: Either the Location ID alone or the combined 'locationID,property' string.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The property name to read. Used when strings[1] doesn't contain a comma.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarToTerrainTypeProperty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to a loaded terrainType property, set in sValue2 as 'terrainTypeID,property'.

uses.description: Reads a named property from a terrainType definition and stores it into a named global variable. The terrainType ID and property can be supplied combined as 'terrainTypeID,property' in strings[1] or split as strings[1]/strings[2]. Writes empty if the terrainType isn't found.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: Either the terrainType ID alone or the combined 'terrainTypeID,property' string.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The property name to read. Used when strings[1] doesn't contain a comma.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarToActionProperty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to a loaded acton's property, set in sValue2 as 'actionID,property'.

uses.description: Reads a named property from an action definition and stores it into a named global variable. The action ID and property can be supplied combined as 'actionID,property' in strings[1] or split as strings[1]/strings[2]. Writes empty if the action isn't found.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: Either the action ID alone or the combined 'actionID,property' string.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The property name to read. Used when strings[1] doesn't contain a comma.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarToFactionProperty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to a loaded faction's property, set in sValue2 as 'factionID,property'.

uses.description: Reads a named property from a Faction and stores it into a named global variable. The faction ID and property can be supplied combined as 'factionID,property' in strings[1] or split as strings[1]/strings[2]. Writes empty if the faction isn't found.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: Either the faction ID alone or the combined 'factionID,property' string.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The property name to read. Used when strings[1] doesn't contain a comma.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarToActorClassProperty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to a loaded actorClass property, set in sValue2 as 'actorClassID,property'.

uses.description: Reads a named property from an actorClass definition and stores it into a named global variable. The class ID and property can be supplied combined as 'actorClassID,property' in strings[1] or split as strings[1]/strings[2]. Writes empty if the actorClass isn't found.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: Either the actorClass ID alone or the combined 'actorClassID,property' string.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The property name to read. Used when strings[1] doesn't contain a comma.

tasks.name: setGlobalVarToSupportAbilityProperty
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to a loaded supportAbility property, set in sValue2 as 'supportAbilityID,property'.

uses.description: Reads a named property from a SupportAbility definition and stores it into a named global variable. The ability ID and property can be supplied combined as 'supportAbilityID,property' in strings[1] or split as strings[1]/strings[2]. Writes empty if the supportAbility isn't found.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: Either the supportAbility ID alone or the combined 'supportAbilityID,property' string.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The property name to read. Used when strings[1] doesn't contain a comma.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toActorItemTypeInSlot
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the itemType ID of the item in slot 'fValue' of the inventory of the actor whose ID is 'sValue2'. If sValue2 is unset, tries to use the actorID of the current task. The gVar will be blank if no item is found in the slot.

uses.description: Reads the item type ID of the inventory slot floats[0] on a specific actor (defaulting to the triggering actor when strings[1] is empty) and writes it into a named global variable. Writes empty if the actor doesn't exist, the slot is out of range, or the slot is empty.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The actor ID to inspect. Defaults to the triggering actor when empty or omitted.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The zero-based inventory slot index. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toActorAugmentItemTypeInMainWeapon
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the itemType ID of the augmented item in slot 'fValue' of the main hand weapon of the actor whose ID is 'sValue2'. If sValue2 is unset, tries to use the actorID of the current task. The gVar will be blank if no item is found in the main hand weapon augment slot.

uses.description: Reads the item type ID of an augment slot on an actor's main-hand weapon (the favoredHand inventory slot) and writes it into a named global variable. Writes empty if the actor doesn't exist, has no main weapon, the weapon has no augment container, or the slot is empty.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The actor ID to inspect. Defaults to the triggering actor when empty or omitted.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The zero-based augment slot index on the weapon. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toActorAugmentItemTypeInOffhandWeapon
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the itemType ID of the augmented item in slot 'fValue' of the offhand weapon of the actor whose ID is 'sValue2'. If sValue2 is unset, tries to use the actorID of the current task. The gVar will be blank if no item is found in the offhand weapon augment slot.

uses.description: Reads the item type ID of an augment slot on an actor's offhand weapon (the slot opposite favoredHand) and writes it into a named global variable. Writes empty if the actor doesn't exist, has no offhand weapon, the weapon has no augment container, or the slot is empty.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The actor ID to inspect. Defaults to the triggering actor when empty or omitted.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The zero-based augment slot index on the weapon. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toActorAugmentItemTypeInArmor
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the itemType ID of the augmented item in slot 'fValue' of the armor of the actor whose ID is 'sValue2'. If sValue2 is unset, tries to use the actorID of the current task. The gVar will be blank if no item is found in the armor augment slot.

uses.description: Reads the item type ID of an augment slot on an actor's armor (inventory slot 2) and writes it into a named global variable. Writes empty if the actor doesn't exist, has no armor, the armor has no augment container, or the slot is empty.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The actor ID to inspect. Defaults to the triggering actor when empty or omitted.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The zero-based augment slot index on the armor. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toItemTypeIDAtTile
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the itemType ID of the first non-invisible item found at tile X,Y with a quantity more than or equal to 'fValue' (defaults to 1 if 0). If 'sValue2' is set, it should be a 'property,value' that the item's itemType property needs to also match. If b1, 'property,value' needs to NOT match. If b2, will only match invisible itemTypes instead.

uses.description: Scans items at the supplied tile (filtered to non-invisible items by default, or to invisible items when bools[1] is true) for one whose quantity is at least floats[0]. Optionally requires (or excludes, with bools[0]) the item's itemType to have a property,value match supplied via strings[1] (as 'property,value' or split across strings[1]/strings[2]). Writes the first matching item's ID into a named global variable.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The tile to scan for items.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: Either the itemType property name (combined 'property,value' form or split form). If empty, no property filter is applied.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The value the itemType property must match. Used when strings[1] doesn't contain a comma.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The minimum quantity an item must have to match. Defaults to 1 if 0 or omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the property,value check is inverted (the item must NOT match).

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, only invisible items are considered (instead of only non-invisible items).

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toItemTypeIDAtTileWithSpecialProp
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the itemType ID of the first item found at tile X,Y with a quantity more than or equal to 'fValue' (defaults to 1 if 0) that has all specialPropertys set in 'sValue2' as a 'comma,separated,list'. An example sValue2 would be 'sprite2xWidth,cannotBePickedUp,animated'. If b1, sValue2 is the list of specialPropertys the itemType must NOT have any of.

uses.description: Scans items at the supplied tile for one whose quantity is at least floats[0] and which has all the specialProperties named in strings[1] (comma-separated or split across additional strings). With bools[0] = true, the filter is inverted: the item must NOT have any of those special properties. Writes the first matching item's ID into a named global variable.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The tile to scan for items.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: A specialProperty enum name (or the first of a comma-separated list).

optional.name: strings[2+]
optional.description: Additional specialProperty enum names to include in the filter.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The minimum quantity an item must have to match. Defaults to 1 if 0 or omitted.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the filter is inverted (the item must NOT have ANY of the listed special properties).

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toActorTypeID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the actorTypeID of the actor whose ID is 'sValue2'. If sValue2 is blank or not set, will try to use the current Task actorID.

uses.description: Writes the actorTypeID of a named actor (defaulting to the triggering actor when strings[1] is empty) into a named global variable. Writes empty if the actor doesn't exist.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The actor ID to look up. Defaults to the triggering actor when empty or omitted.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toPartyActorTypeID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the actorTypeID of the current player party actor in slot 'fValue'.

uses.description: Writes the actorTypeID of the party actor in slot floats[0] into a named global variable. Writes empty if no actor exists in that slot.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The zero-based party slot index. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toRandomActorTypeName
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to a random name from the randomNames property of the actorType with the ID set in 'sValue2'. If sValue2 is blank or not set, will try to use the current Task actorID's actorTypeID.

uses.description: Picks a random name from an actorType's randomNames list and writes it into a named global variable. strings[1] can name an actor type directly, or name an actor whose actorTypeID is used. If the chosen randomName itself names an actor type, that type's randomNames are also recursively chained (when the list has exactly one entry). If the actorType has no randomNames, the actorType's display name is used instead.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: An actorType ID or an actor ID (whose actorTypeID is used). Defaults to the triggering actor when empty.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toActorClassID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the actorClass ID in slot 'fValue' (0 = primary, 1 = secondary, 2 = third, 3 = fourth) of the actor whose ID is 'sValue2'. If sValue2 is blank or not set, will try to use the current Task actorID.

uses.description: Writes the actor's class ID from a specific slot (0 = primary actorClass, 1 = secondaryClass, 2 = actorClass3, 3 = actorClass4) into a named global variable. Defaults to the triggering actor when strings[1] is empty. Writes empty if the actor doesn't exist or the slot is unrecognised.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The actor ID to look up. Defaults to the triggering actor when empty or omitted.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The class slot to read (0..3). Defaults to 0 (primary class) if omitted.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toClassIDThatHasActionID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the 'fValue'-th (starting at 0) actorClass ID that has an action whose ID is 'sValue2' in its actions list.

uses.description: Iterates every actorClass and writes the floats[0]-th class that contains the named action into a global variable. Writes empty if no class (or no Nth class) matches.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The action ID to search for in each class's actions list.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The zero-based index among matching classes. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toClassIDThatHasSupportAbilityID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the 'fValue'-th (starting at 0) actorClass ID that has a supportAbility whose ID is 'sValue2' in its supportAbilities list.

uses.description: Iterates every actorClass and writes the floats[0]-th class that contains the named support ability into a global variable. Writes empty if no class (or no Nth class) matches.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The support ability ID to search for in each class's supportAbilities list.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The zero-based index among matching classes. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toActorSupportAbilityID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the currently equipped supportAbility ID in slot 'fValue' (0, 1 or 2) of the actor whose ID is 'sValue2'. If sValue2 is blank or not set, will try to use the current Task actorID.

uses.description: Writes the support ability ID equipped in slot floats[0] of a named actor into a named global variable. Defaults to the triggering actor when strings[1] is empty. Writes empty if the actor doesn't exist or the slot is out of range.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The actor ID to look up. Defaults to the triggering actor when empty or omitted.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The support ability slot index (0..2). Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toLocationStashItemIDSlot
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the itemType ID of a stash item in slot 'fValue' of a location's item inventory. 'sValue2' is the ID of the location. If ID is not set, uses current location instead. If there is no item in the slot specified or the location hasn't been visited yet, an empty string is set.

uses.description: Writes the item type ID of a Location's stash slot floats[0] into a named global variable. Defaults to the current Location when strings[1] is empty. Writes empty if no location, no item in the slot, or the location hasn't been visited.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The Location ID to inspect. Defaults to the current Location when empty or omitted.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The zero-based stash slot index. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toLocationCargoItemIDSlot
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the itemType ID of a cargo item in slot 'fValue' of a location's cargo inventory. 'sValue2' is the ID of the location. If ID is not set, uses current location instead. If there is no item in the slot specified or the location hasn't been visited yet, an empty string is set.

uses.description: Writes the item type ID of a Location's cargo slot floats[0] into a named global variable. Defaults to the current Location when strings[1] is empty. Writes empty if no location, no item in the slot, or the location hasn't been visited.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The Location ID to inspect. Defaults to the current Location when empty or omitted.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The zero-based cargo slot index. Defaults to 0 if omitted.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toLocationShipIDSlot
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the actorType ID of a ship in slot 'fValue' of a location's shipwright_boatTypes list. 'sValue2' is the ID of the location. If ID is not set, uses current location instead. If there is no item in the slot specified, an empty string is set.

uses.description: Writes the actorType ID of a Location's shipwright boat type in slot floats[0] into a named global variable. Defaults to the current Location when strings[1] is empty. Writes empty if no location or the slot is out of range.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The Location ID to inspect. Defaults to the current Location when empty or omitted.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The zero-based index into the shipwright's boat types list.

tasks.name: setGlobalVar_toLocationFactionID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the currently favored faction ID of a location. 'sValue2' is the ID of the location. If ID is not set, uses current location instead. Sets an empty string if the location is neutral or not a port.

uses.description: Writes a Location's currently favored faction ID into a named global variable. Defaults to the current Location when strings[1] is empty. Writes empty if the location doesn't exist, is neutral, or isn't a port.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The Location ID to inspect. Defaults to the current Location when empty or omitted.

tasks.name: modLocationIDFactionIDFavor
tasks.officialDescription: Adds 'fValue' faction_favor for 'sValue' faction ID to a port location. 'sValue2' is the ID of the location. If ID is not set, uses current location instead. If b1, resets every faction's favor to 0 before adding fValue to sValue (will unset ever-loyal ports). If b2, sets the port to be ever-loyal to sValue faction instead.

uses.description: Modifies the faction-favor map of a port Location. If bools[0] = true, all faction favors at the port are reset to 0 first (unsetting any 'ever-loyal' status). If bools[1] = true, the port is then marked ever-loyal to strings[0]. Otherwise, if the port can change faction, floats[0] is added to its favor for strings[0] (clamped to [-50, 50]). Has no effect outside a port location or with an unknown faction.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The faction ID whose favor at the location should be modified.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The Location ID to modify. Defaults to the current Location when empty or omitted.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The favor delta to apply (clamped to [-50, 50]).

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, resets every faction's favor at the location to 0 before applying the delta.

optional.name: bools[1]
optional.description: If true, the location is marked ever-loyal to strings[0] (overriding the favor delta).

tasks.name: setLocZoneID
tasks.officialDescription: Sets a location's zoneID to 'sValue'. 'sValue2' is the ID of the location. If ID is not set, uses current location instead. Keep in mind that changing a location's zoneID while the player is in it might make it unable to be left, if unaccounted for.

uses.description: Sets a Location's zoneID to a new value. Defaults to the current Location when strings[1] is empty. Be cautious: changing the zoneID while the player is inside the location can leave them unable to exit normally.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The new zoneID to assign to the Location.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The Location ID to modify. Defaults to the current Location when empty or omitted.

tasks.name: setLocName
tasks.officialDescription: Sets a location's name to 'sValue'. 'sValue2' is the ID of the location. If ID is not set, uses current location instead.

uses.description: Sets a Location's display name. Defaults to the current Location when strings[1] is empty.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The new display name for the Location.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The Location ID to modify. Defaults to the current Location when empty or omitted.

tasks.name: removeIconFromLocationID
tasks.officialDescription: Removes 'sValue' icon from a location's tooltipIcons list, if it exists. 'sValue2' is the ID of the location. If ID is not set, uses current location instead.

uses.description: Removes an icon ID from a Location's tooltipIcons list. Defaults to the current Location when strings[1] is empty. Has no effect if the icon isn't in the list.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The icon ID to remove from the Location's tooltipIcons list.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The Location ID to modify. Defaults to the current Location when empty or omitted.

tasks.name: swapIconForLocationID
tasks.officialDescription: Changes an icon from a location's tooltipIcons list to another icon, specified in sValue as 'oldIcon, newIcon'. 'sValue2' is the ID of the location. If ID is not set, uses current location instead. If b1, 'newIcon' will be added to list even if 'oldIcon' doesn't exist.

uses.description: Swaps an icon in a Location's tooltipIcons list. The old and new icons can be supplied combined as 'oldIcon,newIcon' in strings[0] or split as strings[0]/strings[1]. The Location is selected by strings[2] (or strings[1] depending on argument shape) or defaults to the current Location. If the old icon isn't present and bools[0] is true, the new icon is appended instead.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: Either the old icon ID alone or the combined 'oldIcon,newIcon' string.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The new icon ID. Used when strings[0] doesn't contain a comma; can also be the Location ID depending on argument shape.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The Location ID to modify (when both icons are supplied separately). Defaults to the current Location.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the new icon is added to the list even if the old icon isn't present.

tasks.name: addItemToLocationID
tasks.officialDescription: Adds 'sValue' as the ID of an itemType or actorType to the stash, cargo or shipwright of a location that has been visited by the player at least once. fValue is the quantity to add, defaulting to 1. If fValue is -2, removes all non-infinite versions of the item before adding an infinite one. 'sValue2' is the ID of the location. If ID is not set, uses current location instead.

uses.description: Adds an itemType or actorType ID for sale at a Location (in stash, cargo, or shipwright as appropriate). The quantity comes from floats[0] (defaulting to 0/1). A quantity of -2 marks the entry infinite (replacing any finite copies). The Location must have been visited at least once.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The itemType or actorType ID to add for sale.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The Location ID to modify. Defaults to the current Location when empty or omitted.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The quantity to add. Defaults to 0 if omitted; -2 makes the entry infinite (removing finite copies first).

tasks.name: removeItemFromLocationID
tasks.officialDescription: Removes 'sValue' as the ID of an itemType or actorType from the stash, cargo or shipwright of a location. fValue is the quantity to remove, defaulting to 1. If fValue is negative, removes all. 'sValue2' is the ID of the location. If ID is not set, uses current location instead. 'sValue' can be a key word to remove everything of a type: removeAllStash, removeAllCargo, removeAllShips

uses.description: Removes an item or actor type from a Location's sale lists. Three keywords are recognised: 'removeAllStash' clears the location's stash, 'removeAllCargo' clears the cargo, 'removeAllShips' clears the shipwright list. Otherwise removes floats[0] of the named item (negative = all). Defaults to the current Location when strings[1] is empty.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The itemType/actorType ID to remove, or one of the special keywords 'removeAllStash' / 'removeAllCargo' / 'removeAllShips'.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The Location ID to modify. Defaults to the current Location when empty or omitted.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The quantity to remove. Defaults to 0 if omitted; negative removes all.

tasks.name: addUnlockableItemToLocationID
tasks.officialDescription: Adds an itemType or actorType to a location's itemType_quantity_unlockable list with a formula, set in sValue as 'ID, formula'. fValue is the quantity of the unlockable, defaulting to 1, with -2 being infinite. 'sValue2' is the ID of the location. If ID is not set, uses current location instead.

uses.description: Adds an unlockable item (or actor) entry to a Location, gated by a formula that must evaluate to a passing value before the item appears for sale. The ID and formula can be supplied combined as 'ID,formula' in strings[0] or split as strings[0]/strings[1]. The quantity comes from floats[0] (defaulting to 0/1; -2 = infinite). The Location can be supplied via strings[1]/strings[2] (depending on argument shape) or defaults to the current Location. After adding, tryUnlockItems is run.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: Either the item/actor ID alone or the combined 'ID,formula' string.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The unlock formula. Used when strings[0] doesn't contain a comma; can also be the Location ID depending on argument shape.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: The Location ID to modify (when both ID and formula are supplied separately). Defaults to the current Location.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The quantity to unlock. Defaults to 0 if omitted; -2 makes the entry infinite.

tasks.name: removeUnlockableItemFromLocationID
tasks.officialDescription: Removes an item from a location's itemType_quantity_unlockable list that matches an ID, formula and quantity, set in sValue as 'ID, formula' and fValue as quantity. 'sValue2' is the ID of the location. If ID is not set, uses current location instead. If any property to check isn't set, it will match all of that property (eg, fValue = 0 will match any quantity).

uses.description: Removes an entry from a Location's itemType_quantity_unlockable list by matching ID, formula, and quantity. Any field left blank acts as a wildcard. The ID and formula can be supplied combined as 'ID,formula' in strings[0] or split, and the Location can be supplied via strings[1]/strings[2] or defaults to the current Location.

required.name: strings[0]

tasks.officialDescription: Sets a location's enterOnTravelPointNum to fValue. Defaults to 0 if negative. 'sValue' is the ID of the location. If ID is not set, uses current location instead.

uses.description: Adds an item to a fleet's cargo. The fleet is identified by strings[1] (which can be the commodore ID, any ship ID, or 'enemyFleet'/'assistFleet'). Quantity is floats[0] (defaulting to 1 if 0). Has no effect if the item ID is unknown or the fleet/cargo can't be resolved.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The itemType ID to add to the fleet's cargo.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The fleet identifier: a commodore ID, a ship ID, or 'enemyFleet' / 'assistFleet'.

optional.name: floats[0]

If fValue is 0, will remove 1. If fValue is -1 or less, removes all of sValue itemType. If sValue isn't set or is blank, 'fValue' quantity of all items are removed.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: The itemType ID to remove. If empty, the operation applies to every item in cargo.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: The fleet identifier: a commodore ID, a ship ID, or 'enemyFleet' / 'assistFleet'.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: The quantity to remove. Defaults to 1 if 0 or omitted; negative removes all.

uses.description: Writes the itemType ID of a fleet cargo slot floats[0] into a named global variable. The fleet is identified by strings[1]. Writes empty when the fleet or cargo can't be resolved, the slot is out of range, or the slot is empty.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination global variable name.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The fleet identifier: a ship ID, or 'enemyFleet' / 'assistFleet'.

tasks.officialDescription: Sets gVar 'sValue' to the 'fValue'-th (starting at 0) journal Notes entry string.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The centre tile around which actors are gathered.

optional.name: strings[0]
optional.description: A faction filter. If empty, all actors within range are moved.

required.description: The faction ID every player-established port should become ever-loyal to.

tasks.name: addLocationUnlockable
tasks.officialDescription: Adds an unlockable to the current Location of ID sValue, quantity fValue, and fReq sValue2. Use addUnlockableItemToLocationID to add an unlockable to a specific location instead.

uses.description: Adds an unlockable entry to the current Location with the specified ID, quantity, and gating formula, then runs tryUnlockItems to immediately surface any new unlocks.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The itemType or actorType ID to add as an unlockable.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The gating formula string.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The quantity for the unlockable (default 1; -2 = infinite).

tasks.name: task
tasks.officialDescription: execute a task set as a comma-separated string in 'sValue'

uses.description: Parses strings[0] as a comma-separated task string and executes the resulting Task immediately in the calling trigger's area. Useful for nesting tasks dynamically.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: A comma-separated task string to parse and execute.

tasks.name: task_gvar
tasks.officialDescription: execute a comma-separated task as defined by global variable 'sValue'

uses.description: Looks up a comma-separated task string in a global variable's value, parses it, and executes the resulting Task immediately. Has no effect if the global var's value is '0'.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The name of the global variable holding the task string to execute.

tasks.name: dominateworld
tasks.officialDescription: Sets wealth to 100 for all overworld ports and converts them to the player's current faction.

uses.description: Maxes out the wealth level (100) of every overworld port and, if the player has a faction set, marks every port ever-loyal to it. Used as a debug/admin cheat.

tasks.name: gc
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Forces program garbage collection.

uses.description: Console debug command: clears the in-memory zone cache, then forces two full .NET garbage collection passes (with WaitForPendingFinalizers between them). Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: clear
tasks.officialDescription: Clears all text from the console window.

uses.description: Clears the indefinite debug-draw queue, the dark-ignore draw queue, and the in-game debug-string overlay. Used to scrub all debug overlays.

tasks.name: debug_drawStrings
tasks.officialDescription: Displays a debug message using 'sValue' as the type and 'sValue2' as the message.

uses.description: Displays a debug message via the in-game debug-string overlay, using strings[0] as the type label and strings[1] as the body. Has no effect if either is empty.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The type label for the debug message.

required.name: strings[1]
required.description: The message body for the debug message.

tasks.name: loadItOnUpYo
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Moves the player to zone strings[0] at position tileCoords[0], facing floats[0] with floats[1] as the fadeSpeed.

uses.description: Transfers the player to a named zone at the supplied tile, with the supplied facing rotation and fade-to-black speed. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The destination zone ID.

required.name: floats[0]
required.description: The party's facing rotation in the destination zone.

required.name: floats[1]
required.description: The fade-to-black speed for the transition.

required.name: tileCoords[0]
required.description: The tile in the destination zone where the party should arrive.

tasks.name: maximizeWindowSet
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Maximizes the game window if bValue1 is true.

uses.description: Sets the game window's maximised state to bools[0]. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, the window is maximised; otherwise restored to its previous size. Defaults to false.

tasks.name: maximizeWindowIfTrue
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Maximizes the game window if bValue1 is true.

uses.description: If bools[0] is true, maximises the game window and disables the main-menu background and UIManager drawing. If bools[0] is false (or omitted), does nothing. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

optional.name: bools[0]
optional.description: If true, maximises the window and suppresses main-menu/UI background drawing.

tasks.name: changeScreenResolution2
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE.

uses.description: Cycles the game to its alternate (2nd) screen-resolution preset. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: uploadmod
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Uploads a new mod using the mod folder 'sValue'.

uses.description: Initiates a Steam Workshop upload of the named mod folder. Validates the mod first and only proceeds if valid. Locks player input while the upload is in flight. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The mod folder name to upload.

tasks.name: getmod
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Sets mod ID 'sValue' as the mod currently being edited, for use with other mod tasks.

uses.description: Parses strings[0] as a Steam Workshop file ID and stores it as the 'currently being edited' mod for subsequent editmod/editmodtags calls. If parsing fails, the ID is reset to 0. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The Steam Workshop file ID, as a number string.

tasks.name: editmod
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Uploads mod changes for the mod currently being edited, using mod folder 'sValue'.

uses.description: Uploads updated content (folder contents and preview image) for the previously-selected Steam Workshop mod. Validates the mod folder first. Locks player input during the upload. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The mod folder name supplying the updated content.

tasks.name: editmodtags
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Changes the tags for the mod currently being edited to each strings[] set.

uses.description: Sets the Workshop tags of the previously-selected mod to the comma-joined list of every string supplied. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

required.name: strings[0+]
required.description: The tag values to set on the mod (joined into a comma-separated list).

tasks.name: loadModPageInBrowser
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE. Opens the modding reference guide URL (located on Steam) in the player's default browser.

uses.description: Opens the previously-selected mod's Steam Workshop page in the user's default web browser. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: test
tasks.officialDescription: DO NOT USE.

uses.description: Debug command: schedules a 'test2' task to run after 1 second, then runs a regex demonstration that replaces 'd:sword' with 'beans' in a hard-coded input and prints the result. Marked DO NOT USE by the official description.

tasks.name: UIPartInputCommand
tasks.officialDescription: Calls handleUIPartInput_command(string0, string1, float0, string2, float1)

uses.description: Forwards a UI command to the input manager. If at least 3 strings and 2 floats are supplied, all are passed through. Otherwise only strings[0] is sent. Used as a generic bridge from the task system into the UI command pipeline.

required.name: strings[0]
required.description: The UI command name.

optional.name: strings[1]
optional.description: A string argument passed through to handleUIPartInput_command.

optional.name: strings[2]
optional.description: A second string argument passed through to handleUIPartInput_command.

optional.name: floats[0]
optional.description: A float argument passed through to handleUIPartInput_command.

optional.name: floats[1]
optional.description: A second float argument passed through to handleUIPartInput_command.

tasks.name: noSpeechFXLookOrAnim
tasks.officialDescription: Must be used as a dialog specialEffect. The current dialogNode won't show its text reveal FX or play any set animations. The currentActorTalkingTo also won't look at the player.

uses.description: A no-op task that, by virtue of being scheduled, suppresses certain auto-behaviours in dialog: text-reveal FX, automatic animations, and the talker looking at the player. The shared switch body for 'none', 'doNothing', and 'test2' is empty. Often used in dialog specialEffect lists where the side effect (presence) matters more than executing anything.
